FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the higher frequency of HER2 immunoreactivity in urothelial carcinoma of the bladder compared to the upper urinary tract, the association of HER2 immunoreactivity with progression-free survival (PFS) and disease-specific overall survival (OS) in advanced urothelial carcinoma patients, and the significance of HER2 immunoreactivity as a prognostic factor for PFS and OS in patients receiving adjuvant M-VEC chemotherapy, although this significance is lost in multivariate analysis.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of how HER2 immunoreactivity interacts with specific components of the M-VEC chemotherapy regimen to influence clinical outcomes in urothelial carcinoma patients. Specifically, it does not explore whether the presence of HER2 immunoreactivity affects the efficacy of individual chemotherapeutic agents within the M-VEC regimen.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the presence of HER2 immunoreactivity in urothelial carcinoma will enhance the efficacy of epirubicin, a component of the M-VEC regimen, thereby improving progression-free survival in patients treated with this chemotherapy, as measured by a significant increase in PFS in HER2-positive patients compared to HER2-negative patients receiving ep

abstract: Halofantrine is a newly developed antimalarial drug used for the treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria. The introduction of this drug has been delayed because of its possible side effects, and due to insufficient studies on adverse reactions in humans. There have been no studies investigating its effect on hearing. Thirty guinea pigs were divided into three groups: a control group, a halofantrine therapeutic dose group and a halofantrine double therapeutic dose group. One cochlea specimen from each animal was stained with haematoxylin and eosin and the other with toluidine blue. No changes were detected in the control group. The halofantrine therapeutic dose group showed loss and distortion of inner hair cells and inner phalangeal cells, and loss of spiral ganglia cells. In the halofantrine double therapeutic dose group, the inner and outer hair cells were distorted and there was loss of spiral ganglia cells.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that guinea pigs exposed to a therapeutic dose of halofantrine will exhibit significant recovery of inner hair cell morphology and function within 12 weeks after cessation of the drug, as measured by auditory brainstem response and cochlear histology.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that halofantrine, an antimalarial drug, causes dose-dependent damage to the cochlea in guinea pigs, including loss and distortion of inner and outer hair cells and loss of spiral ganglia cells, even at therapeutic doses.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether the hearing loss caused by halofantrine is reversible, and if so, what timeframe is required for recovery, as well as the underlying mechanisms by which halofantrine induces ototoxicity.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that guinea pigs exposed to a therapeutic dose of halofantrine will exhibit significant recovery of inner hair cell morphology and function within 12 weeks after cessation of the drug, as measured by auditory brainstem response and cochlear histology.

abstract: Measurement of visceral adipose tissue (VAT) needs to be accurate and sensitive to change for risk monitoring. The purpose of this study is to determine the CT slice location where VAT area can best reflect changes in VAT volume and body weight. 60 plain abdominal CT images from 30 males [mean age (range) 51 (41-68) years, mean body weight (range) 71.1 (101.9-50.9) kg] who underwent workplace screenings twice within a 1-year interval were evaluated. Automatically calculated and manually corrected areas of the VAT of various scan levels using "freeform curve" region of interest on CT were recorded and compared with body weight changes. The strongest correlations of VAT area with VAT volume and body weight changes were shown in a slice 3 cm above the lower margin of L3 with r values of 0.853 and 0.902, respectively.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the optimal CT slice location for measuring visceral adipose tissue area in relation to VAT volume and body weight changes will vary significantly between genders, with females requiring a slice location more superior to the lower margin of L3 compared to males, due to differences in body composition and fat distribution.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the strongest correlations between visceral adipose tissue (VAT) area and both VAT volume and body weight changes occur at a specific CT slice location, 3 cm above the lower margin of L3, with correlation coefficients of 0.853 and 0.902, respectively. This suggests that measuring VAT area at this particular slice can accurately reflect changes in VAT volume and body weight.

GAP: While the study identifies an optimal CT slice location for measuring VAT area in relation to VAT volume and body weight changes in males, it does not investigate whether this correlation holds true across different demographics, such as females or individuals of various ethnic backgrounds, nor does it explore the physiological mechanisms underlying this correlation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the optimal CT slice location for measuring visceral adipose tissue area in relation to VAT volume and body weight changes will vary significantly between genders, with females requiring a slice location more superior to the lower margin of L3 compared to males, due to differences in body composition and fat distribution.

abstract: The accepted treatment protocol for necrotizing fasciitis (NF) consists of extensive surgery and wide spectrum antibiotics. Hyperbaric oxygenation (HBO) has been recommended as adjuvant therapy for NF, improving patient mortality and outcome. However, the beneficial effect of HBO for NF remains controversial. A retrospective evaluation of treatment outcome in 37 patients treated for NF between 1984 and 1993 was carried out. The mortality rate, morbidity criteria, and risk factors for grave prognosis were compared between a group of 25 patients who received HBO as part of their treatment protocol and a group of the remaining 12 patients treated by surgical excision and antibiotics alone. The two groups were found to be similar with regard to age, gender, the incidence of individual risk factors for ominous prognosis, and the Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation (APACHE) II score for disease's severity on presentation. The mortality rate among the HBO-treated patients was 36%, as opposed to 25% in the non-HBO group. The mean number of surgical débridements required per patient was significantly higher in the HBO group: 3.3 compared with 1.5 in the non-HBO-treated patients. Although the average length of hospitalization for survivors was shorter for the HBO group, the difference between the groups did not reach statistical significance.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the increased production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) due to hyperbaric oxygenation therapy exacerbates tissue damage in necrotizing fasciitis patients, leading to a higher requirement for surgical débridements and contributing to the observed higher mortality rate in this group.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study compared outcomes of necrotizing fasciitis (NF) treatment with and without hyperbaric oxygenation (HBO) and found that the mortality rate was higher in the HBO group (36%) compared to the non-HBO group (25%), and the HBO group required a significantly higher number of surgical débridements per patient. Despite this, the average length of hospitalization for survivors was shorter in the HBO group, although not statistically significant.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of why HBO treatment, despite its potential benefits in reducing hospitalization length, is associated with a higher mortality rate and increased need for surgical débridements in NF patients. The mechanisms by which HBO influences the progression and treatment outcomes of NF are not clearly understood and require further investigation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the increased production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) due to hyperbaric oxygenation therapy exacerbates tissue damage in necrotizing fasciitis patients, leading to a higher requirement for surgical débridements and contributing to the observed higher mortality rate in this group.

abstract: To assess if the Hawkins sign can predict whether or not astragalus fractures of the neck will develop avascular necrosis. It is also assessed whether the occurrence of this complication is related to the displacement of the fracture, soft tissue injury, or delay in the reduction or surgery. The results were compared with those found in the literature. A retrospective study was conducted on 23 talar neck fractures recorded over a a period of thirteen years. The following variables were analysed: displacement of the fracture, soft tissue injury, delay and type of treatment, complications, observation of the Hawkins sign, and functional outcome. There were 7 type I Hawkins fractures, 11 type II, and 4 type III and 1 type IV. Four cases developed avascular necrosis (2 Hawkins type II and 2 type III). Hawkins sign was observed in 12 cases, of which none developed necrosis. Four cases with negative Hawkins sign developed necrosis. No statistically significant differences were found when comparing the development of avascular necrosis with the displacement of the fracture, soft tissue injury, or delay in treatment. Differences were found when comparing the development of avascular necrosis with the Hawkins sign (P=.03).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that early surgical intervention in talar neck fractures with a negative Hawkins sign will reduce the incidence of avascular necrosis compared to delayed surgery, thereby improving functional outcomes in these patients.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the presence of the Hawkins sign is significantly associated with a lower risk of developing avascular necrosis in talar neck fractures, with no cases of necrosis observed in patients with a positive Hawkins sign. The development of avascular necrosis was not significantly related to the displacement of the fracture, soft tissue injury, or delay in treatment. The study also reported that 4 cases with negative Hawkins sign developed necrosis, suggesting a potential predictive value of the Hawkins sign.

GAP: While the study established a correlation between the Hawkins sign and the risk of avascular necrosis, it does not elucidate the underlying biological mechanisms by which the Hawkins sign influences the development of avascular necrosis, nor does it investigate whether early intervention based on the presence or absence of the Hawkins sign can improve patient outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that early surgical intervention in talar neck fractures with a negative Hawkins sign will reduce the incidence of avascular necrosis compared to delayed surgery, thereby improving functional outcomes in these patients.

abstract: Changes in the spectrum of general surgery and the delivery of surgical care have placed the requirement for a mandatory general surgery rotation in the surgical clerkship in question. We tested the hypothesis that equal mastery of surgical clerkship objectives can be obtained in a clerkship with and without general surgery. Students chose any two surgical rotations and were assessed by written examination, objective structured clinical examination (OSCE), ward evaluations, self-assessment objectives questionnaire, and satisfaction survey. Data for 54 students showed no differences in scores between groups on any parameter. No specific concerns related to the absence of general surgery were identified.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that surgical residents who complete a clerkship without a general surgery rotation will exhibit equivalent or non-inferior clinical decision-making skills and patient outcomes compared to those who complete a traditional clerkship with a general surgery rotation, when assessed over a 2-year period following completion of their residency training.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study indicate that students who participated in a surgical clerkship with or without a general surgery rotation showed no significant differences in their mastery of surgical clerkship objectives, as assessed through various evaluation methods including written examinations, OSCE, ward evaluations, self-assessment, and satisfaction surveys.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of whether the absence of a mandatory general surgery rotation in a surgical clerkship has any long-term effects on the clinical competence and decision-making abilities of surgical residents in real-world practice settings, beyond the initial clerkship period.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that surgical residents who complete a clerkship without a general surgery rotation will exhibit equivalent or non-inferior clinical decision-making skills and patient outcomes compared to those who complete a traditional clerkship with a general surgery rotation, when assessed over a 2-year period following completion of their residency training.

abstract: To investigate the effectiveness of acupuncture in treating phonotraumatic vocal fold lesions.STUDY DESIGN/ A total of 123 dysphonic individuals with benign vocal pathologies were recruited. They were given either genuine acupuncture (n = 40), sham acupuncture (n = 44), or no treatment (n = 39) for 6 weeks (two 30-minute sessions/wk). The genuine acupuncture group received needles puncturing nine voice-related acupoints for 30 minutes, two times a week for 6 weeks, whereas the sham acupuncture group received blunted needles stimulating the skin surface of the nine acupoints for the same frequency and duration. The no-treatment group did not receive any intervention but attended just the assessment sessions. One-hundred seventeen subjects completed the study (genuine acupuncture = 40; sham acupuncture = 43; and no treatment = 34), but only 84 of them had a complete set of vocal functions and quality of life measures (genuine acupuncture = 29; sham acupuncture = 33; and no-treatment = 22) and 42 of them with a complete set of endoscopic data (genuine acupuncture = 16; sham acupuncture = 15; and no treatment = 11). Significant improvement in vocal function, as indicated by the maximum fundamental frequency produced, and also perceived quality of life, were found in both the genuine and sham acupuncture groups, but not in the no-treatment group. Structural (morphological) improvements were, however, only noticed in the genuine acupuncture group, which demonstrated a significant reduction in the size of the vocal fold lesions.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that genuine acupuncture will induce increased expression of anti-inflammatory cytokines and decreased expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines in vocal fold tissue, leading to a reduction in inflammation and subsequent healing of phonotraumatic vocal fold lesions, as measured by histological analysis and quantitative PCR of tissue samples collected before and after a 6-week course of acupuncture treatment.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that both genuine and sham acupuncture groups showed significant improvement in vocal function and quality of life, but only the genuine acupuncture group demonstrated structural improvements, including a reduction in the size of vocal fold lesions. The improvements in vocal function and quality of life were measured by maximum fundamental frequency produced and perceived quality of life, respectively.

GAP: The study does not explain the underlying mechanisms by which genuine acupuncture leads to structural improvements in vocal fold lesions, nor does it investigate the potential long-term effects of acupuncture treatment on vocal fold lesions. Specifically, the study does not examine the biological processes that occur at the level of the vocal fold tissue itself in response to acupuncture.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that genuine acupuncture will induce increased expression of anti-inflammatory cytokines and decreased expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines in vocal fold tissue, leading to a reduction in inflammation and subsequent healing of phonotraumatic vocal fold lesions, as measured by histological analysis and quantitative PCR of tissue samples collected before and after a 6-week course of acupuncture treatment.

abstract: With the advancement of an aging society in the world, an increasing number of elderly patients have been hospitalized due to aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH). There is no study that compares the elderly cases of aSAH who receive the definitive treatment with those who treated conservatively. The aim of this study was to investigate the feasibility of the definitive surgery for the acute subarachnoid cases aged 80 or older. We reviewed 500 consecutive cases with acute aSAH with surgical indication for aneurysm repair. Inoperable cases such as dead-on-arrival and the cases with both pupils dilated were excluded. We compared the cases aged 80 or older that received clipping or coil embolization with the controls that the family selected conservative treatment. 69 cases were included in this study (ranged 80-98, male:female=9:60). 56 cases (81.2%) had an aneurysm in the anterior circulation. 23 cases received clipping, 20 cases coil embolization and 26 cases treated conservatively. The cases with aneurysm repair showed significantly better clinical outcome than the controls, while World Federation of Neurological Surgeons (WFNS) grade on admission and premorbid modified Rankin Scale showed no difference between them.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that elderly patients with aSAH who undergo definitive treatment with clipping or coil embolization will exhibit improved cognitive function and quality of life at 12-month follow-up, compared to those who receive conservative treatment, as measured by standardized cognitive and functional assessment tools.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that elderly patients (80 years or older) with aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) who received definitive treatment (clipping or coil embolization) had significantly better clinical outcomes compared to those who received conservative treatment. The majority of aneurysms were located in the anterior circulation, and there were no significant differences in WFNS grade on admission or premorbid modified Rankin Scale between the treatment groups.

GAP: The study does not investigate the long-term effects of definitive treatment versus conservative management on cognitive function, quality of life, or survival rates in elderly patients with aSAH, nor does it explore the potential factors that influence the decision to pursue surgical intervention in this age group.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that elderly patients with aSAH who undergo definitive treatment with clipping or coil embolization will exhibit improved cognitive function and quality of life at 12-month follow-up, compared to those who receive conservative treatment, as measured by standardized cognitive and functional assessment tools.

abstract: Recent evaluations of IT innovations in primary care have highlighted variations between centres and practices in uptake and use. We evaluated whether structural characteristics of a general practice were associated with variations in use of a web-based clinical information system underpinning a Managed Clinical Network in diabetes, between the years 2001 and 2003. Using a computerised audit trail, we calculated the numbers of web-based operations that occurred in each practice, stratified by staff type and year, and adjusted for the numbers of registered diabetic patients. In regression analyses, we determined whether total use was associated with structural characteristics of the practice (total list size, training status, numbers of GPs (general practitioners), mean age of the GPs, numbers of female GPs, level of deprivation of the population and whether staff had received advanced training in diabetes care). Initially there were a few practices which made very frequent use of the information system, with relatively high numbers of practices using the facility infrequently. However, overall use gradually became more evenly spread. This effect was particularly evident among nurse users. Frequent use by GPs was evident in only a small number of practices, with mean GP use decreasing over the three years. In linear regression analyses, none of the general practice variables were associated with online use, either overall or stratified by staff type, except for the numbers of diabetes-educated staff. This was consistently associated with increased use by nurses and GPs.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that general practices that provide ongoing training and education to their staff on the effective integration of web-based clinical information systems into daily clinical workflows will exhibit a significant increase in the frequency and consistency of system use by healthcare professionals, compared to practices that do not provide such training, as measured by audit trail data over a 12-month period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include the observation that the use of a web-based clinical information system in general practices varied between centers, with an initial disparity that gradually became more evenly spread, particularly among nurse users. The frequency of use by GPs was low and decreased over time in most practices. Linear regression analyses showed that the number of diabetes-educated staff was the only practice variable consistently associated with increased online use by both nurses and GPs.

GAP: The study identifies an association between the number of diabetes-educated staff and the use of the web-based clinical information system but does not explore the specific mechanisms by which this association occurs, such as whether the education of staff leads to increased confidence in using the system, better understanding of its benefits, or changes in workplace policies that encourage its use.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that general practices that provide ongoing training and education to their staff on the effective integration of web-based clinical information systems into daily clinical workflows will exhibit a significant increase in the frequency and consistency of system use by healthcare professionals, compared to practices that do not provide such training, as measured by audit trail data over a 12-month period.

abstract: The purpose of this study is to examine whether or not well differentiated (w-d) hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is indeed clinically early cancer. Seventy six patients with solitary small HCCs up to 3 cm in diameter, who underwent hepatectomy, were observed for at least 2 years for possible recurrence. These patients were divided into two groups: 10 patients with w-d HCCs (Edmondson and Steiner's grade I) and 66 patients with less differentiated (l-d) HCCs (Edmondson and Steiner's grade I-II, II-III, and III). The histological analysis revealed that w-d HCCs had lower incidences of fibrous capsule formation (P<0.01), when compared to l-d HCCs. There were no significant differences in the incidence of intrahepatic metastasis, or portal vein invasion. In a resected specimen of w-d HCC, barium sulfate and gelatin were injected into portal vein and a transparent specimen was made. The transparent specimen showed that the portal vein in the tumor seemed to be intact. Microscopically, cancer cell infiltration into the fibrous frame of the portal tract was present. There were no significant differences in the disease free survival between the two groups. An analysis of tumor volume doubling time in recurrent foci suggested that minute cancerous foci had been present at the time of operation.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the intact portal veins within well-differentiated hepatocellular carcinomas serve as a conduit for the dissemination of cancer cells, leading to an increased risk of recurrence in patients with w-d HCC, which can be tested by analyzing the correlation between portal vein integrity and recurrence patterns in a larger cohort of patients with w-d H
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include: (1) well-differentiated (w-d) hepatocellular carcinomas (HCCs) have lower incidences of fibrous capsule formation compared to less differentiated (l-d) HCCs, (2) no significant differences in intrahepatic metastasis, portal vein invasion, or disease-free survival between w-d and l-d HCCs, and (3) analysis of tumor volume doubling time suggests that minute cancerous foci were present at the time of operation.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the presence of intact portal veins within w-d HCCs, as observed in the transparent specimen, contributes to the recurrence of HCC by facilitating the spread of cancer cells through the portal venous system, and if so, how this process affects the clinical outcome of patients with w-d HCC.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the intact portal veins within well-differentiated hepatocellular carcinomas serve as a conduit for the dissemination of cancer cells, leading to an increased risk of recurrence in patients with w-d HCC, which can be tested by analyzing the correlation between portal vein integrity and recurrence patterns in a larger cohort of patients with w-d H

abstract: To compare adherence to follow-up recommendations for colposcopy or repeated Papanicolaou (Pap) smears for women with previously abnormal Pap smear results. Retrospective cohort study. Three northern California family planning clinics. All women with abnormal Pap smear results referred for initial colposcopy and a random sample of those referred for repeated Pap smear. Medical records were located and reviewed for 90 of 107 women referred for colposcopy and 153 of 225 women referred for repeated Pap smears. Routine clinic protocols for follow-up--telephone call, letter, or certified letter--were applied without regard to the type of abnormality seen on a Pap smear or recommended examination. Documented adherence to follow-up within 8 months of an abnormal result. Attempts to contact the patients for follow-up, adherence to follow-up recommendations, and patient characteristics were abstracted from medical records. The probability of adherence to follow-up vs the number of follow-up attempts was modeled with survival analysis. Cox proportional hazards models were used to examine multivariate relationships related to adherence. The rate of overall adherence to follow-up recommendations was 56.0% (136/243). Adherence to a second colposcopy was not significantly different from that to a repeated Pap smear (odds ratio, 1.40; 95% confidence interval, 0.80-2.46). The use of as many as 3 patient reminders substantially improved adherence to follow-up. Women without insurance and women attending 1 of the 3 clinics were less likely to adhere to any follow-up recommendation (hazard ratio for no insurance, 0.43 [95% confidence interval, 0.20-0.93], and for clinic, 0.35 [95% confidence interval, 0.15-0.73]).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that implementing a personalized follow-up strategy, based on individual patient characteristics such as age, insurance status, and previous adherence behavior, will significantly increase the rate of adherence to follow-up recommendations for colposcopy or repeated Pap smears among women with abnormal Pap smear results compared to standard, non-personalized follow-up protocols.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include: (1) the overall adherence to follow-up recommendations for colposcopy or repeated Pap smears after abnormal Pap smear results was 56.0%, (2) adherence to a second colposcopy was not significantly different from that to a repeated Pap smear, (3) multiple patient reminders (up to 3) improved adherence to follow-up, and (4) lack of insurance and attendance at a specific clinic were associated with lower adherence to follow-up recommendations.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of whether personalized follow-up strategies, tailored to individual patient characteristics and preferences, could further improve adherence to recommended follow-up care for women with abnormal Pap smear results. The current study applied routine clinic protocols without regard to the type of abnormality or patient-specific factors, suggesting a potential gap in understanding how tailored approaches might impact adherence.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that implementing a personalized follow-up strategy, based on individual patient characteristics such as age, insurance status, and previous adherence behavior, will significantly increase the rate of adherence to follow-up recommendations for colposcopy or repeated Pap smears among women with abnormal Pap smear results compared to standard, non-personalized follow-up protocols.

abstract: Accurate and updated information on airborne pollen in specific areas can help allergic patients. Current monitoring systems are based on a morphologic identification approach, a time-consuming method that may represent a limiting factor for sampling network enhancement. To verify the feasibility of developing a real-time polymerase chain reaction (PCR) approach, an alternative to optical analysis, as a rapid, accurate, and automated tool for the detection and quantification of airborne allergenic pollen taxa. The traditional cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide-based method was modified for DNA isolation from pollen. Taxon-specific DNA sequences were identified via bioinformatics or literature searches and were PCR amplified from the matching allergenic taxa; based on the sequences of PCR products, complementary or degenerate TaqMan probes were developed. The accuracy of the quantitative real-time PCR assay was tested on 3 plant species. The setup of a modified DNA extraction protocol allowed us to achieve good-quality pollen DNA. Taxon-specific nuclear gene fragments were identified and sequenced. Designed primer pairs and probes identified selected pollen taxa, mostly at the required classification level. Pollen was properly identified even when collected on routine aerobiological tape. Preliminary quantification assays on pollen grains were successfully performed on test species and in mixes.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the real-time PCR approach developed for airborne pollen detection will maintain its accuracy and reliability when applied to a high-volume, outdoor air sampling system, with a correlation coefficient of at least 0.9 between PCR-based pollen counts and traditional morphologic identification methods, across a range of environmental conditions.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The researchers have successfully developed a real-time polymerase chain reaction (PCR) approach for the detection and quantification of airborne allergenic pollen taxa, using a modified DNA extraction protocol and taxon-specific DNA sequences. They were able to accurately identify pollen taxa at the required classification level, even when collected on routine aerobiological tape, and performed preliminary quantification assays on pollen grains.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the feasibility of using real-time PCR for pollen detection and quantification, it does not investigate the potential for this method to be used in real-world, high-throughput monitoring systems, nor does it assess the impact of environmental factors, such as humidity and temperature, on the accuracy and reliability of the PCR-based approach.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the real-time PCR approach developed for airborne pollen detection will maintain its accuracy and reliability when applied to a high-volume, outdoor air sampling system, with a correlation coefficient of at least 0.9 between PCR-based pollen counts and traditional morphologic identification methods, across a range of environmental conditions.

abstract: Compared with computed tomography (CT) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), positron emission tomography (PET) may have additional value in the assessment of primary and recurrent cervical cancer. However, the degree of tumour uptake of (18)F-2-fluoro-2-deoxy-D: -glucose (FDG) uptake is sometimes influenced by diabetes mellitus (DM). Therefore, we conducted this prospective study to compare the diagnostic ability of FDG-PET in patients with cervical cancer complicated by DM and those without DM. Patients with untreated locally advanced primary or clinically curable recurrent cervical carcinoma were enrolled. Both FDG-PET and MRI/CT scans were performed within 2 weeks. Patients were categorised into the following groups: hyperglycaemic DM (fasting blood sugar>126 mg/dl), euglycaemic DM and non-DM. The lesions were confirmed histologically or by clinical follow-up. The receiver operating characteristic curve method, with calculation of the area under the curve (AUC), was used to evaluate the discriminative power. From February 2001 to January 2003, 219 patients (75 with primary and 144 with recurrent cervical cancer) were eligible for analysis. Sixteen had hyperglycaemic DM, 12 had euglycaemic DM and 191 were in the non-DM group. The diagnostic power of PET in the hyperglycaemic DM, euglycaemic DM and non-DM groups did not differ significantly with regard to the identification of either metastatic lesions (AUC, 0.967/0.947/0.925, P>0.05) or primary tumours/local recurrence (AUC, 0.950/0.938/0.979, P>0.05). Considering all DM patients, PET showed a significantly higher detection power than MRI/CT scans in respect of metastatic lesions (AUC=0.956 vs 0.824, P=0.012).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the glucose metabolism in cervical cancer cells, as measured by FDG-PET, will be altered in patients with hyperglycaemic diabetes mellitus compared to those with euglycaemic diabetes mellitus or without diabetes, leading to a difference in the predictive value of FDG-PET for patient outcomes, such as recurrence-free survival and overall survival.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The diagnostic power of FDG-PET in detecting metastatic lesions and primary tumors/local recurrence in cervical cancer patients is not significantly affected by the presence of diabetes mellitus (DM), with FDG-PET showing a higher detection power than MRI/CT scans for metastatic lesions in DM patients. The study found similar areas under the curve (AUC) for FDG-PET in hyperglycaemic DM, euglycaemic DM, and non-DM groups for both metastatic lesions and primary tumors/local recurrence.

GAP: The study does not investigate the underlying mechanisms by which diabetes mellitus, particularly hyperglycaemic and euglycaemic conditions, may influence the uptake of FDG in tumors, nor does it explore how these conditions might impact the long-term prognostic value of FDG-PET in cervical cancer patients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the glucose metabolism in cervical cancer cells, as measured by FDG-PET, will be altered in patients with hyperglycaemic diabetes mellitus compared to those with euglycaemic diabetes mellitus or without diabetes, leading to a difference in the predictive value of FDG-PET for patient outcomes, such as recurrence-free survival and overall survival.

abstract: To describe the biomechanical and wound healing characteristics of corneas after excimer laser keratorefractive surgery. Histologic, ultrastructural, and cohesive tensile strength evaluations were performed on 25 normal human corneal specimens, 206 uncomplicated LASIK specimens, 17 uncomplicated sub-Bowman's keratomileusis (SBK) specimens, 4 uncomplicated photorefractive keratectomy (PRK) specimens, 2 uncomplicated advanced surface ablation (ASA) specimens, 5 keratoconus specimens, 12 postoperative LASIK ectasia specimens, and 1 postoperative PRK ectasia specimen and compared to previously published studies. Histologic and ultrastructural studies of normal corneas showed significant differences in the direction of collagen fibrils and/or the degree of lamellar interweaving in Bowman's layer, the anterior third of the corneal stroma, the posterior two-thirds of the corneal stroma, and Descemet's membrane. Cohesive tensile strength testing directly supported these morphologic findings as the stronger, more rigid regions of the cornea were located anteriorly and peripherally. This suggests that PRK and ASA, and secondarily SBK, should be biomechanically safer than conventional LASIK with regard to risk for causing keratectasia after surgery. Because adult human corneal stromal wounds heal slowly and incompletely, all excimer laser keratorefractive surgical techniques still have some distinct disadvantages due to inadequate reparative wound healing. Despite reducing some of the risk for corneal haze compared to conventional PRK, ASA cases still can develop corneal haze or breakthrough haze from the hypercellular fibrotic stromal scarring. In contrast, similar to conventional LASIK, SBK still has the short- and long-term potential for interface wound complications from the hypocellular primitive stromal scar.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) and their inhibitors, such as MMP-2 and tissue inhibitor of metalloproteinase-2 (TIMP-2), will differ significantly between PR
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study include significant differences in the direction of collagen fibrils and the degree of lamellar interweaving in different layers of the cornea, with the anterior and peripheral regions being stronger and more rigid. The study also found that different surgical techniques (PRK, ASA, SBK, and LASIK) have varying degrees of risk for causing keratectasia and corneal haze, with PRK and ASA being potentially biomechanically safer than LASIK. Additionally, the study highlights the limitations of adult human corneal stromal wound healing, leading to distinct disadvantages for all excimer laser keratorefractive surgical techniques.

abstract: Radiotherapy reduces local recurrence rates but is also capable of short- and long-term toxicity. It may also render treatment of local recurrence more challenging if it develops despite previous radiotherapy. This study examined the impact of radiotherapy for the primary rectal cancer on outcomes after pelvic exenteration for local recurrence. We conducted a retrospective review of exenteration databases. The study took place at a quaternary referral center that specializes in pelvic exenteration. Patients referred for pelvic exenteration from October 1994 to November 2012 were reviewed. Patients who did and did not receive radiotherapy as part of their primary rectal cancer treatment were compared. The main outcomes of interest were resection margins, overall survival, disease-free survival, and surgical morbidities. There were 108 patients, of which 87 were eligible for analysis. Patients who received radiotherapy for their primary rectal cancer (n = 41) required more radical exenterations (68% vs 44%; p = 0.020), had lower rates of clear resection margins (63% vs 87%; p = 0.010), had increased rates of surgical complications per patient (p = 0.014), and had a lower disease-free survival (p = 0.022). Overall survival and disease-free survival in patients with clear margins were also lower in the primary irradiated patients (p = 0.049 and p<0.0001). This difference in survival persisted in multivariate analysis that corrected for T and N stages of the primary tumor. This study is limited by its retrospective nature and heterogeneous radiotherapy regimes among radiotherapy patients.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the tumor microenvironment of rectal cancer patients who receive preoperative radiotherapy will exhibit increased expression of pro-fibrotic and immunosuppressive factors, leading to a more aggressive tumor phenotype and poorer surgical outcomes compared to non-irradiated patients.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients who received radiotherapy for their primary rectal cancer and later underwent pelvic exenteration for local recurrence had more radical exenterations, lower rates of clear resection margins, increased surgical complications, and lower disease-free survival compared to those who did not receive radiotherapy. Additionally, overall and disease-free survival were lower in patients with clear margins who received primary irradiation.

GAP: The study does not investigate the molecular or cellular mechanisms underlying the observed differences in outcomes between irradiated and non-irradiated patients, particularly how radiotherapy affects the tumor microenvironment and its potential impact on surgical outcomes and survival rates.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the tumor microenvironment of rectal cancer patients who receive preoperative radiotherapy will exhibit increased expression of pro-fibrotic and immunosuppressive factors, leading to a more aggressive tumor phenotype and poorer surgical outcomes compared to non-irradiated patients.

abstract: Lymphedema may be identified by simpler circumference changes as compared with changes in limb volume. Ninety breast cancer patients were prospectively enrolled in an academic trial, and seven upper extremity circumferences were measured quarterly for 3 years. A 10% volume increase or greater than 1 cm increase in arm circumference identified lymphedema with verification by a lymphedema specialist. Sensitivity and specificity of several different criteria for detecting lymphedema were compared using the academic trial as the standard. Thirty-nine cases of lymphedema were identified by the academic trial. Using a 10% increase in circumference at two sites as the criterion, half the lymphedema cases were detected (sensitivity 37%). When using a 10% increase in circumference at any site, 74.4% of cases were detected (sensitivity 49%). Detection by a 5% increase in circumference at any site was 91% sensitive.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that breast cancer patients who undergo quarterly upper extremity circumference measurements and receive early intervention upon detection of a 5% or greater increase in circumference at any site will exhibit a significant reduction in lymphedema severity and improvement in quality of life compared to those who receive standard care without regular circumference measurements.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that simpler circumference changes can be used to identify lymphedema, with a 5% increase in circumference at any site being 91% sensitive for detecting lymphedema, and that the sensitivity of detection increases as the criteria for circumference increase are less stringent, but still specific.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the early detection of lymphedema using circumference measurements can lead to improved patient outcomes, such as reduced severity of lymphedema or improved quality of life, and what the optimal timing and frequency of these measurements are for effective early detection and intervention.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that breast cancer patients who undergo quarterly upper extremity circumference measurements and receive early intervention upon detection of a 5% or greater increase in circumference at any site will exhibit a significant reduction in lymphedema severity and improvement in quality of life compared to those who receive standard care without regular circumference measurements.

abstract: Synchronous liver metastases (SLM) occur in 20% of colorectal cancers (CRC). Resection of SLM and CLC can be undertaken at different centres (separate management, SM) or at the same centre (global management, GM). Retrospective study of SLM and CRC resections carried out during 01/2000 - 12/2006 by SM or GM, using a combined or delayed strategy. Morphologic characteristics and type of CRC and SLM resection were similar for the GM (n = 45) or SM (n = 66) groups. In patients with delayed liver resection (62 SM, 17 GM), chemotherapy prior to liver surgery was used in 92% and 38% of SM and GM patients (P<0.0001) and the median delay between procedures was 212 and 182 days, respectively (P = 0.04). First step of liver resection was more often performed during colorectal surgery in the GM group (62 vs. 6% for SM, P<0.0001) and the mean number of procedures (CRC+SLM) was lower (1.6 vs. 2.3, P = 0.003). Three-month mortality was 3% for GM and 0% for SM (n.s.). Overall survival rates were 67% and 51% for SM and GM at 3 years (n.s.), and 35 and 31% at 5 years (n.s.). Disease-free survival to 5 years was higher in SM patients (14% vs. 11%, P = 0.009).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the improved disease-free survival rates observed in patients undergoing separate management for synchronous liver metastases and colorectal cancer are due to the more frequent use of neoadjuvant chemotherapy in this group, which reduces the likelihood of microscopic disease dissemination at the time of surgical resection.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include: (1) similar morphologic characteristics and resection types for synchronous liver metastases (SLM) and colorectal cancer (CRC) in both global management (GM) and separate management (SM) groups, (2) differences in treatment approaches between GM and SM, such as the use of chemotherapy prior to liver surgery and the timing of liver resection, (3) varying outcomes in terms of the number of procedures, mortality rates, overall survival rates, and disease-free survival rates between GM and SM groups.

GAP: A key knowledge gap left unanswered by this work is the underlying biological or physiological mechanisms that contribute to the differences in disease-free survival rates between patients undergoing GM and SM for SLM and CRC. Specifically, the study does not explore how the timing and approach to chemotherapy and surgical resection impact the recurrence of cancer in these patients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the improved disease-free survival rates observed in patients undergoing separate management for synchronous liver metastases and colorectal cancer are due to the more frequent use of neoadjuvant chemotherapy in this group, which reduces the likelihood of microscopic disease dissemination at the time of surgical resection.

abstract: Studies have shown that schizophrenia patients have motion perception deficit, which was thought to cause eye-tracking abnormality in schizophrenia. However, eye movement closely interacts with motion perception. The known eye-tracking difficulties in schizophrenia patients may interact with their motion perception. Two speed discrimination experiments were conducted in a within-subject design. In experiment 1, the stimulus duration was 150 msec to minimize the chance of eye-tracking occurrence. In experiment 2, the duration was increased to 300 msec, increasing the possibility of eye movement intrusion. Regular eye-tracking performance was evaluated in a third experiment. At 150 msec, speed discrimination thresholds did not differ between schizophrenia patients (n = 38) and control subjects (n = 33). At 300 msec, patients had significantly higher thresholds than control subjects (p = .03). Furthermore, frequencies of eye tracking during the 300 msec stimulus were significantly correlated with speed discrimination in control subjects (p = .01) but not in patients, suggesting that eye-tracking initiation may benefit control subjects but not patients. The frequency of eye tracking during speed discrimination was not significantly related to regular eye-tracking performance.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that schizophrenia patients will exhibit altered neural activity in the frontal eye fields during eye-tracking initiation, compared to control subjects, which will be associated with impaired speed discrimination performance at stimulus durations that allow for eye movement.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that schizophrenia patients do not differ from control subjects in speed discrimination thresholds at a stimulus duration of 150 msec, but they have significantly higher thresholds at 300 msec. Additionally, the frequency of eye tracking during the 300 msec stimulus is correlated with speed discrimination in control subjects, but not in schizophrenia patients. This suggests that eye-tracking initiation may benefit control subjects but not patients in speed discrimination tasks.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific aspects of eye movement or motion perception are disrupted in schizophrenia patients, and how these disruptions contribute to the observed deficits in speed discrimination. Specifically, it is unclear whether the inability of schizophrenia patients to benefit from eye-tracking initiation in speed discrimination tasks is due to abnormalities in the planning, execution, or processing of eye movements.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that schizophrenia patients will exhibit altered neural activity in the frontal eye fields during eye-tracking initiation, compared to control subjects, which will be associated with impaired speed discrimination performance at stimulus durations that allow for eye movement.

abstract: We have previously reported the feasibility of diagnostic and therapeutic peritoneoscopy including liver biopsy, gastrojejunostomy, and tubal ligation by an oral transgastric approach. We present results of per-oral transgastric splenectomy in a porcine model. The goal of this study was to determine the technical feasibility of per-oral transgastric splenectomy using a flexible endoscope. We performed acute experiments on 50-kg pigs. All animals were fed liquids for 3 days prior to procedure. The procedures were performed under general anesthesia with endotracheal intubation. The flexible endoscope was passed per orally into the stomach and puncture of the gastric wall was performed with a needle knife. The puncture was extended to create a 1.5-cm incision using a pull-type sphincterotome, and a double-channel endoscope was advanced into the peritoneal cavity. The peritoneal cavity was insufflated with air through the endoscope. The spleen was visualized. The splenic vessels were ligated with endoscopic loops and clips, and then mesentery was dissected using electrocautery. Endoscopic splenectomy was performed on six pigs. There were no complications during gastric incision and entrance into the peritoneal cavity. Visualization of the spleen and other intraperitoneal organs was very good. Ligation of the splenic vessels and mobilization of the spleen were achieved using commercially available devices and endoscopic accessories.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the gastric wall incision created during per-oral transgastric splenectomy will exhibit complete healing with minimal scarring and no significant alteration in gastric motility or digestive enzyme secretion at 6 weeks post-procedure in a porcine model.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the successful performance of per-oral transgastric splenectomy in a porcine model using a flexible endoscope, with no complications during gastric incision and entrance into the peritoneal cavity, and effective visualization and ligation of the splenic vessels and mobilization of the spleen.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the long-term effects and safety of the per-oral transgastric approach for splenectomy, particularly regarding the healing of the gastric wall incision, potential for infection, and impact on the digestive system's functionality post-procedure.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the gastric wall incision created during per-oral transgastric splenectomy will exhibit complete healing with minimal scarring and no significant alteration in gastric motility or digestive enzyme secretion at 6 weeks post-procedure in a porcine model.

abstract: Fournier's gangrene is known to have an impact in the morbidity and despite antibiotics and aggressive debridement, the mortality rate remains high. To assess the morbidity and mortality in the treatment of Fournier's gangrene in our experience. The medical records of 14 patients with Fournier's gangrene who presented at the University Hospital Center "Mother Teresa" from January 1997 to December 2006 were reviewed retrospectively to analyze the outcome and identify the risk factor and prognostic indicators of mortality. Of the 14 patients, 5 died and 9 survived. Mean age was 54 years (range from 41-61): it was 53 years in the group of survivors and 62 years in deceased group. There was a significant difference in leukocyte count between patients who survived (range 4900-17000/mm) and those died (range 20.300-31000/mm3). Mean hospital stay was about 19 days (range 2-57 days).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that reducing leukocyte count through targeted anti-inflammatory therapy in patients with Fournier's gangrene will decrease mortality rates and improve clinical outcomes, as measured by a reduction in hospital stay and improvement in survival rates, compared to standard treatment alone.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that Fournier's gangrene has a high mortality rate despite treatment, with a significant difference in leukocyte count between survivors (4900-17000/mm^3) and non-survivors (20300-31000/mm^3), and a mean hospital stay of 19 days. The mean age of survivors was 53 years, while the mean age of non-survivors was 62 years.

GAP: The study identified a correlation between high leukocyte count and mortality, but it does not explain the underlying mechanisms by which elevated leukocyte counts contribute to increased mortality in Fournier's gangrene patients, nor does it investigate potential therapeutic strategies to modulate leukocyte count and improve outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that reducing leukocyte count through targeted anti-inflammatory therapy in patients with Fournier's gangrene will decrease mortality rates and improve clinical outcomes, as measured by a reduction in hospital stay and improvement in survival rates, compared to standard treatment alone.

abstract: Kidneys from elderly donors tend to be implanted in recipients who are also elderly. We present the results obtained after 10 years of evolution on transplanting elderly kidneys into young recipients. Ninety-one consecutive transplants are studied, carried out in our center with kidneys from cadaver donors older than 60 years implanted in recipients younger than 60 years. The control group is made up of 91 transplants, matched with those from the study group, whose donor and recipient were younger than 60 years. There were no differences between groups with regard to recipient age, sex, cause of death and renal function of the donor, hepatitis C and cytomegalovirus serologies, cold ischemia time, tubular necrosis, immediate diuresis, need for dialysis, human leukocyte antigen incompatibilities, hypersensitized patients, acute rejection, waiting time on dialysis, and days of admission. Survival in both groups at 1, 5, and 10 years was 97.6%, 87.2%, and 76.6% vs. 98.8%, 87.5%, and 69.5% for the patient (P=0.642), 92.9%, 81.3%, and 64.2% vs. 93.9%, 76.4%, and 69.5% for the graft (P=0.980), and 94.4%, 92.6%, and 77.4% vs. 94.3%, 86.7%, and 84.4% for the graft with death censured (P=0.747), respectively. Creatininaemias at 1, 5, and 10 years were 172, 175, and 210 vs. 139, 134, and 155 (P<0.05).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the increased creatinine levels in young recipients of kidneys from elderly donors are associated with accelerated fibrosis and vascular changes in the transplanted kidneys, which can be mitigated by targeted therapeutic interventions aimed at reducing oxidative stress and improving endothelial function.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that transplanting kidneys from elderly donors into young recipients results in similar patient and graft survival rates compared to transplanting kidneys from younger donors into young recipients, but with slightly higher creatinine levels in the elderly donor group, indicating potentially reduced renal function over time.

GAP: The study does not investigate the underlying mechanisms or factors contributing to the observed difference in renal function, as indicated by creatinine levels, between the two groups, nor does it explore potential long-term consequences of this difference on the health and quality of life of the recipients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the increased creatinine levels in young recipients of kidneys from elderly donors are associated with accelerated fibrosis and vascular changes in the transplanted kidneys, which can be mitigated by targeted therapeutic interventions aimed at reducing oxidative stress and improving endothelial function.

abstract: To determine the impact of Florida's Medicaid Demonstration 4 years post-implementation on per member per month (PMPM) Medicaid expenditures and whether receiving care through HMOs versus provider service networks (PSNs) in the Demonstration was associated with PMPM expenditures.DATA: Florida Medicaid claims from two fiscal years prior to implementation of the Demonstration (FY0405, FY0506) and the first four fiscal years after implementation (FY0607-FY0910) from two urban Demonstration counties and two urban non-Demonstration counties. A difference-in-difference approach was used to compare changes in enrollee expenditures before and after implementation of the Demonstration overall and specifically for HMOs and PSNs. Claims data were extracted for enrollees in the Demonstration and non-Demonstration counties and collapsed into monthly amounts (N = 26,819,987 person-months). Among SSI enrollees, the Demonstration resulted in lower increases in PMPM expenditures over time ($40) compared with the non-Demonstration counties ($186), with Demonstration PSNs lowering PMPM expenditures by $7 more than HMOs. Savings were also seen among TANF enrollees but to a lesser extent.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the implementation of care coordination services in Provider Service Networks (PSNs) will result in a greater reduction in per member per month (PMPM) expenditures compared to Health Maintenance Organizations (HMOs) among SSI enrolle
raw_output:

The Demonstration led to a $40 increase in PMPM expenditures among SSI enrollees, compared to a $186 increase in non-Demonstration counties. Additionally, PSNs lowered PMPM expenditures by $7 more than HMOs.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific aspects of the Provider Service Networks (PSNs) and Health Maintenance Organizations (HMOs) contribute to the differences in PMPM expenditures. abstract: Extracranial internal carotid artery stenosis is a risk factor for perioperative stroke in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass surgery (CAB). Although selective and non-selective methods of preoperative carotid screening have been advocated, it remains unclear if this screening is clinically relevant.AIM: To test whether selective carotid screening is as effective as non-selective screening in detecting significant carotid disease. The case records of patients consecutively undergoing CAB were reviewed. Patients were stratified retrospectively into high- or low-risk groups according to risk factors for significant carotid stenosis and perioperative stroke: peripheral vascular disease (PVD), carotid bruit, diabetes mellitus, age>70 years and/or history of cerebrovascular disease. Prevalence of carotid stenosis detected by ultrasonography, surgical management and perioperative stroke rates were determined in each group. Overall, 205 consecutive patients underwent preoperative carotid screening. The prevalence of significant carotid stenosis was 5.8%. Univariate analysis confirmed that PVD (P=0.005), carotid bruit (P=0.003) and diabetes mellitus (P=0.05) were significant risk factors for stenosis. Carotid stenosis was a risk factor for stroke (P=0.03). Prevalence of carotid stenosis was higher in the high-risk group (9.1%) than the low-risk group (1.2%) (P<0.05). All concomitant or staged carotid endarterectomies/CAB (5/205) and all patients who had perioperative strokes (5/205) were in the high-risk group (P=0.01).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass surgery who are identified as high-risk for carotid stenosis, prophylactic carotid endarterectomy prior to CAB will reduce the incidence
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract are that selective carotid screening in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass surgery (CAB) can identify significant carotid stenosis, a risk factor for perioperative stroke. The prevalence of carotid stenosis is higher in high-risk groups, defined by factors such as peripheral vascular disease (PVD), carotid bruit, diabetes mellitus, age > 70 years, and history of cerebrovascular disease. The study also found that all cases of concomitant or staged carotid endarterectomies/CAB and all perioperative strokes occurred in the high-risk group.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether early intervention or more aggressive management of carotid stenosis in the high-risk group can reduce the incidence of perioperative stroke in patients undergoing CAB. It also does not address the long-term outcomes of patients with significant carotid stenosis who undergo CAB, and whether selective screening and subsequent management can improve these outcomes.

abstract: One of the sites most frequently invaded by gastric cancer is the mesocolon; however, the UICC does not mention this anatomical site as an adjacent structure involved in gastric cancer. The purpose of this study was to characterize and classify mesocolon invasion from gastric cancer. We examined 806 patients who underwent surgery for advanced gastric carcinoma from 1992 to 2007 at the Department of Surgery, Gangnam Severance Hospital, Korea. Among these, patients who showed macroscopically direct invasion into the mesocolon were compared to other patients with advanced gastric cancer. The curability, number and extent of nodal metastasis, and the survival of the mesocolon invasion group were significantly worse than these factors in the T3 group. However, the survival of the mesocolon invasion group after curative resection was much better than that of patients who had incurable factors.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific adhesion molecules and proteolytic enzymes in gastric cancer cells is significantly altered in cases of mesocolon invasion, contributing to the increased aggressiveness and metastatic potential observed in these patients.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that gastric cancer invasion into the mesocolon is associated with worse curability, increased nodal metastasis, and poorer survival compared to other advanced gastric cancer cases without mesocolon invasion, classified as T3. However, patients with mesocolon invasion who underwent curative resection had significantly better survival rates than those with incurable factors.

GAP: The study does not investigate the molecular or pathological mechanisms underlying the aggressive behavior of gastric cancer that invades the mesocolon, nor does it explore potential therapeutic targets specific to this pattern of invasion.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the expression of specific adhesion molecules and proteolytic enzymes in gastric cancer cells is significantly altered in cases of mesocolon invasion, contributing to the increased aggressiveness and metastatic potential observed in these patients.

abstract: To determine the relationship between injury severity surrogates and other patient factors with the development and severity of heterotopic ossification (HO) following open reduction internal fixation of acetabular fractures treated with a posterior approach. Retrospective review. Academic level 1 trauma center. Two hundred forty-one patients who were treated through a posterior approach with a minimum of 6-month radiographic follow-up were identified from an acetabular fracture database. None. The occurrence and severity (Brooker Grade III/IV) of HO 6 months postsurgery. Length of stay (LOS) in the intensive care unit (ICU), non-ICU LOS>10 days, and HO prophylaxis with external radiation beam therapy (XRT) were significantly associated with the development of HO in a multivariate model [ 1-2 days, odds ratio (OR) = 4.33, 95% confidence interval (CI): 1.03-18.25; 3-6 days, OR = 4.1, 95% CI, 1.27-13.27;>6 days, OR = 11.7, 95% CI, 3.24-42.22; non-ICU LOS>10 days (vs. 0-6 days): OR = 7.6, 95% CI, 2.6-22.25; XRT HO prophylaxis: OR = 0.29, 95% CI, 0.10-0.85]. Other variables evaluated in multivariate modeling not significantly associated with development and severity of HO included age, gender, mechanism of injury, injury severity score, presence of neurologic injury, Letournel fracture type, occurrence of hip dislocation, interval from injury to surgery, operative time, and estimated blood loss.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific osteogenic transcription factors, such as runt-related transcription factor 2 (Runx2), will be significantly altered in patients with longer intensive care unit stays and non-ICU stays
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of this study include the identification of factors significantly associated with the development of heterotopic ossification (HO) after open reduction internal fixation of acetabular fractures treated with a posterior approach. These factors include length of stay in the intensive care unit (ICU), non-ICU length of stay greater than 10 days, and the use of external radiation beam therapy (XRT) as a prophylactic measure for HO. Specifically, longer ICU stays and longer non-ICU stays are associated with an increased risk of developing HO, while the use of XRT is associated with a decreased risk.

GAP: Despite identifying significant associations between certain patient factors and the development of HO, the study leaves unanswered the question of how these factors influence the biological pathways leading to HO. Specifically, the molecular mechanisms by which longer hospital stays and the use of XRT affect the formation of heterotopic bone are not addressed. Understanding these mechanisms could provide insights into potential therapeutic targets for preventing HO.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the expression of specific osteogenic transcription factors, such as runt-related transcription factor 2 (Runx2), will be significantly altered in patients with longer intensive care unit stays and non-ICU stays

abstract: In primary and secondary prevention trials, statins have been shown to reduce the risk of stroke. In addition to lipid lowering, statins have a number of antiatherothrombotic and neuroprotective properties. In a preliminary observational study, we explored whether clinical outcome is improved in patients who are on treatment with statins when stroke occurs. We conducted a population-based case-referent study of 25- to 74-year-old stroke patients with, for each case of a patient who was on statin treatment at the onset of stroke (n=125), 2 referent patients who were not treated with statins but were matched for age, gender, year of onset, and stroke subtype (n=250). The unadjusted odds ratio for early discharge to home (versus late discharge or death) was 1.41 (95% CI 0.91 to 2.17) when patients on statin treatment were compared with referent stroke patients not on statins. Prognostic factors were, in general, more unfavorable among patients on statins. When this was adjusted for in a logistic regression model, the use of statins was a moderately strong but statistically nonsignificant predictor of discharge to home (multiple-adjusted odds ratio 1.42, 95% CI 0.90 to 2.22).

raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that statins have been shown to reduce the risk of stroke and have antiatherothrombotic and neuroprotective properties. In a population-based case-referent study, patients on statin treatment at the onset of stroke had a higher odds ratio for early discharge to home compared to those not on statins, although this association was not statistically significant after adjusting for prognostic factors.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the observed trend towards better outcomes in statin-treated patients is due to the pleiotropic effects of statins (e.g., anti-inflammatory, endothelial protection) or other factors, and what specific mechanisms or patient subgroups might be responsible for this potential benefit.
