abstract: To compare children's, parents' and physicians' perceptions of children's body size. We administered a structured questionnaire of body size perception using a descriptive Likert scale keyed to body image figures to children ages 12 to 18 years. The same scale was given to parents of children ages 5 to 18 years. The sample consisted of 91 children and their parents being seen in the Pediatric Gastroenterology Clinic for concerns unrelated to overweight. Weight and height of the children were measured, and body mass index (BMI) was calculated. The children's BMI percentiles were categorized as underweight (<15th), normal (15th-85th), overweight (85th-95th), and obese (95th and above). The attending physician independently completed the body image and description scale and indicated the figure that most accurately represented the patient without reference to BMI standards. Accuracy of the patients', parents', and doctors' estimates were statistically compared. The sample population consisted of 6.4% underweight, 70.5% normal weight, 7.7% overweight, and 15.4% obese. Forty-four percent of parents underestimated children's body size using word descriptions and 47% underestimated using figures. Forty percent of the children underestimated their own body size using descriptions and 43% underestimated using figures. The physicians in this study had a higher percentage of correct estimates; however, they underestimated 33% of the patients using both word descriptions and figures. Some obese children were not recognized, and several average children were perceived as underweight.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that children who are perceived as being of a lower weight status than their actual BMI percentile by either themselves, their parents, or their physicians will be less likely to engage in healthy weight management behaviors over a 12-month period compared to children whose weight status is accurately perceived.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that there are significant discrepancies in the perceptions of children's body size among children, parents, and physicians, with a substantial percentage of underestimation by all groups, including 44% of parents, 40-43% of children, and 33% of physicians. The accuracy of estimates varied, with physicians having the highest percentage of correct estimates but still underestimating 33% of the time. The sample population consisted of 6.4% underweight, 70.5% normal weight, 7.7% overweight, and 15.4% obese children.

GAP: The study highlights the issue of inaccurate body size perception but does not explore the potential consequences of these misperceptions on the health outcomes of the children, particularly in terms of how these perceptions might influence behaviors, interventions, or treatment plans. It also does not investigate what factors contribute to the discrepancies in perception among the different groups.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that children who are perceived as being of a lower weight status than their actual BMI percentile by either themselves, their parents, or their physicians will be less likely to engage in healthy weight management behaviors over a 12-month period compared to children whose weight status is accurately perceived.

abstract: In a prospective study 218 preschool children were enrolled (stratified in 2 training programs, one specialized for phonologic awareness in order to prevent dyslexia, the other consisting in training of general perception) during the last year of kindergarten. After finishing the first grade 131 children were compared in their reading and writing abilities. In the whole group only a slight difference was found between both training modalities concerning their writing abilities. However, children with a history of hearing loss, actual hearing loss or pathologic middle ear findings profited most from the specialized training program compared to the control in their reading abilities.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that children with a history of hearing loss who receive the specialized phonologic awareness training program will exhibit improved speech sound discrimination skills, as measured by phoneme identification tasks, compared to those who receive the general perception training program.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that preschool children with a history of hearing loss, actual hearing loss, or pathologic middle ear findings benefited more from a specialized training program for phonologic awareness in terms of their reading abilities, compared to a general perception training program. However, there was only a slight difference in writing abilities between the two training programs for the whole group.

GAP: The study does not investigate the specific mechanisms by which the specialized training program improves reading abilities in children with hearing difficulties, nor does it examine whether the benefits of this program extend to other aspects of language development, such as speech or vocabulary.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that children with a history of hearing loss who receive the specialized phonologic awareness training program will exhibit improved speech sound discrimination skills, as measured by phoneme identification tasks, compared to those who receive the general perception training program.

abstract: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is an immune-mediated inflammatory demyelinating disease of uncertain etiology. Although the mechanisms of inducting autoimmunity by some of the infectious agents have been investigated, there is not yet enough research on streptococcal infections.MATERIAL/ To understand the effect of past group A streptococcal infection on MS, antistreptolysin O (ASO) and antideoxyribonuclease B (ADNase B) were measured in 21 patients with relapsing-remitting MS and 21 healthy blood donors by nephelometric assay. ADNase B levels in the patients with MS were found to be significantly higher than in the controls (p<0.001); however, ASO levels were similar in both groups.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that elevated ADNase B levels in response to past group A streptococcal infections will correlate with increased disease activity and lesion formation in relapsing-remitting MS patients, as measured by MRI and clinical exacerbation rates over a 12-month follow-up period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (MS) have significantly higher levels of antideoxyribonuclease B (ADNase B) compared to healthy controls (p<0.001), while antistreptolysin O (ASO) levels are similar in both groups.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether the elevated ADNase B levels in MS patients are a contributing factor to the development or progression of the disease, and what the specific mechanisms are by which group A streptococcal infections might influence MS pathogenesis.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that elevated ADNase B levels in response to past group A streptococcal infections will correlate with increased disease activity and lesion formation in relapsing-remitting MS patients, as measured by MRI and clinical exacerbation rates over a 12-month follow-up period.

abstract: The endogenous estradiol metabolite, 2-methoxyestradiol (2ME), has been shown to be a potent inhibitor of cell growth and a strong anti-angiogenic substance. We investigated for the first time whether in vitro combinations of 2ME with various chemotherapeutic compounds may result in an additive inhibitory effect on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells. As a model two different human ovary cancer cell lines were used. All cell lines were incubated with equimolar concentrations of 2ME (0.8-25 microM) and the chemotherapeutics epirubicine, doxorubicine, paclitaxel, docetaxel, carboplatin, vinorelbine, 5-fluorouracil and mafosfamide. Proliferation was measured after four days using the ATP-chemosensitivity test. For both ovary cancer cell lines a significant additive effect of 2ME with epirubicine and carboplatin was observed at the lower concentration range of these chemotherapeutic substances.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the combination of 2ME with epirubicine and carboplatin will exhibit a significant additive inhibitory effect on cell proliferation in other estrogen receptor-positive cancer cell lines, such as breast cancer cells, at concentrations similar to those effective in ovary cancer cells.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that 2-methoxyestradiol (2ME) exhibits an additive inhibitory effect on the proliferation of human ovary cancer cells when combined with certain chemotherapeutic compounds, specifically epirubicine and carboplatin, at lower concentration ranges. This additive effect was observed in two different human ovary cancer cell lines.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether this additive effect of 2ME with epirubicine and carboplatin can be extended to other types of cancer cells beyond ovary cancer, and what the underlying mechanisms are that drive this synergistic inhibition of cell proliferation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the combination of 2ME with epirubicine and carboplatin will exhibit a significant additive inhibitory effect on cell proliferation in other estrogen receptor-positive cancer cell lines, such as breast cancer cells, at concentrations similar to those effective in ovary cancer cells.

abstract: Reconstructing the natural joint line in knee revision surgery improves clinical and functional outcome but may be challenging when both cartilage and bone were removed during previous operations. Assessing joint lines (JLs) by means of bony landmarks is inadvisable because of large variations in human anatomy. Because of the inherent symmetry of the human body, we hypothesised that JLs may be directly assessed by measuring the distances from the bony landmarks to the JL of the contralateral knee by means of radiographic images. Using scaled weight-bearing radiographs in anteroposterior view of both knees, two independent observers measured the distances from the fibular head, the medial and lateral epicondyle, and the adductor tubercle to the JL. A two-sided p value of ≤0.05 was considered statistically significant. Two hundred knees of 100 patients (50 men and 50 women) were examined. For the fibular head, the mean difference between the treated and the control knee was 0.0 mm with narrow confidence limits ranging from -1.1 to 1.1.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that utilizing a machine learning algorithm trained on radiographic images to predict the optimal joint line position based on the contralateral knee's anatomy will significantly improve the accuracy of joint line reconstruction in knee revision surgery compared to traditional methods, especially in patients with substantial bone loss or anatomical asymmetry.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract are that reconstructing the natural joint line in knee revision surgery improves outcomes, and measuring distances from bony landmarks to the joint line using radiographic images of the contralateral knee can be a reliable method, with the mean difference between treated and control knees being 0.0 mm for the fibular head, indicating high symmetry and potential for accurate assessment.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether this method of assessing joint lines using the contralateral knee as a reference can be applied to patients with significant asymmetry or deformities, and how accurate this method is in predicting optimal joint line positioning in knee revision surgery for individual patients, particularly in cases where previous operations have resulted in significant bone loss.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that utilizing a machine learning algorithm trained on radiographic images to predict the optimal joint line position based on the contralateral knee's anatomy will significantly improve the accuracy of joint line reconstruction in knee revision surgery compared to traditional methods, especially in patients with substantial bone loss or anatomical asymmetry.

abstract: There are a number of factors responsible for the longevity of unicompartmental knee replacements (UKR). These include the magnitude of postoperative alignment and the type of material used. The effect of component design and material on postoperative alignment, however, has not been explored. We retrospectively reviewed 89 patients who underwent UKR with robotic guidance. Patients were divided into two groups, according to whether they had received an all-polyethylene inlay component (Inlay group) or a metal-backed onlay component (Onlay group). We explored the magnitude of mechanical alignment correction obtained in both groups. Mean postoperative mechanical alignment was significantly closer to neutral in the Onlay group (mean=2.8°; 95% CI=2.4°, 3.2°) compared to the Inlay group (mean=3.9°; 95% CI=3.4°, 4.4°) (R2=0.65; P=0.003), adjusting for gender, BMI, age, side and preoperative mechanical alignment (Fig. 2). Further exploration revealed that the thickness of the tibial polyethyelene insert had a significant effect on postoperative alignment when added to the model (R2=0.68; P=0.01).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients who receive a metal-backed onlay component with an optimized tibial polyethylene insert thickness will exhibit a lower rate of implant wear and revision surgery at 5-year follow-up compared to those receiving an all-polyethylene inlay component.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients who underwent unicompartmental knee replacement (UKR) with a metal-backed onlay component (Onlay group) had a mean postoperative mechanical alignment significantly closer to neutral compared to those with an all-polyethylene inlay component (Inlay group). Additionally, the thickness of the tibial polyethylene insert was found to have a significant effect on postoperative alignment.

GAP: The study identified the impact of component design (Onlay vs. Inlay) and the thickness of the tibial polyethylene insert on postoperative mechanical alignment, but it does not explore how these factors influence the long-term clinical outcomes, such as the rate of implant wear, patient satisfaction, or the need for revision surgery.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients who receive a metal-backed onlay component with an optimized tibial polyethylene insert thickness will exhibit a lower rate of implant wear and revision surgery at 5-year follow-up compared to those receiving an all-polyethylene inlay component.

abstract: To determine whether there is a relationship between VEGF expression and renal vein and vena cava invasion in stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma and to evaluate the impact of VEGF expression on survival in pT3 renal cell carcinoma. 78 patients with a pT3a or pT3b tumour without vena cava invasion or pT3b tumour with vena cava invasion were compared for age, gender, Fuhrman grade and immunohistochemical expression of VEGF. All these variables were submitted to univariate and multivariate analysis to establish their impact on survival. Only tumour size appeared to be significantly different between the 3 groups. On univariate analysis, invasion of the perirenal fat, lymph node involvement, distant metastases and VEGF expression were significantly associated with survival (p<0.01). On multivariate analysis, lymph node involvement, distant metastases and VEGF expression (OR 6.07) were identified as independent predictive factors of survival.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that inhibition of VEGF expression in stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma will reduce the incidence of distant metastases and improve overall survival in patients, and that this effect will be mediated through a decrease in angiogenesis and an increase in tumor cell apoptosis.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that VEGF expression, along with lymph node involvement and distant metastases, is an independent predictive factor of survival in patients with stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma. Additionally, factors such as invasion of the perirenal fat and tumor size are associated with survival outcomes in these patients. The expression of VEGF is significantly associated with survival, with an odds ratio of 6.07, indicating a strong correlation.

GAP: One of the knowledge gaps left unanswered by this work is the mechanism by which VEGF expression influences survival in stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma patients. Specifically, it is unclear whether VEGF expression promotes metastasis, enhances tumor growth, or affects the tumor microenvironment in a way that impacts patient survival. Furthermore, the abstract does not explore potential therapeutic strategies targeting VEGF to improve survival outcomes in these patients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that inhibition of VEGF expression in stage pT3 renal cell carcinoma will reduce the incidence of distant metastases and improve overall survival in patients, and that this effect will be mediated through a decrease in angiogenesis and an increase in tumor cell apoptosis.

abstract: Deaths from injury and poisoning (suicide, accidents, undetermined deaths, and homicide) are the major cause of death among young men aged 15-39 years in England and Wales and have been increasing in recent years.AIM: To describe common characteristics among young men who die from injury and poisoning. We employed a retrospective survey methodology to investigate factors associated with deaths by injury and poisoning among young men aged 15-39 years (n = 268) in Merseyside and Cheshire during 1995. Data were collected from Coroner's inquest notes and General Practitioner records. The most common cause of death was poisoning by alcohol and drugs (29.1%, n = 78). A high proportion of cases were unemployed (39.4%, n = 106). Cases were also more likely to be single compared to the general population (74.2% vs 55.5%). Self-destructive behaviour was evident in 77% of deaths (n = 206).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that targeted interventions aimed at improving employment opportunities and social support networks among young men aged 15-39 years will significantly reduce the incidence of self-destructive behaviors leading to death by injury or poisoning in this demographic.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract are that deaths from injury and poisoning are a major cause of death among young men aged 15-39 years in England and Wales, with poisoning by alcohol and drugs being the most common cause. Additionally, a high proportion of these individuals were unemployed, single, and exhibited self-destructive behavior.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether there is a specific correlation between the socioeconomic factors (such as unemployment) and the likelihood of engaging in self-destructive behaviors that lead to death by injury or poisoning among young men. It also does not explore potential interventions that could mitigate these risks based on the identified characteristics.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that targeted interventions aimed at improving employment opportunities and social support networks among young men aged 15-39 years will significantly reduce the incidence of self-destructive behaviors leading to death by injury or poisoning in this demographic.

abstract: To determine the duration of continuing pregnancy after antenatal corticosteroid (AC) administration and to evaluate the potential opportunity for rescue AC. Retrospective analysis of women at 24-32 weeks' gestation who received AC at one institution. Six hundred ninety-two women received AC. Two hundred forty-seven (35.7%) delivered at>or = 34 weeks' gestation. Three hundred twenty-one (46.4%) delivered within 1 week of AC; 92 of those women (13.3%) delivered within 24 hours. Only 124 (17.9%) remained pregnant 1 week after AC and delivered at<34 weeks. The latter were compared to women delivering>2 week after AC but>or = 34 weeks. More likely to deliver at<34 weeks were those women who received AC for premature preterm rupture of membranes (OR 3.83, 95% CI 2.06-7.17), twins (OR 2.90, 95% CI 1.42-5.95) or before 28 weeks (OR 2.21, 95% CI 1.38-3.52).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression levels of specific inflammatory cytokines, such as interleukin-6 (IL-6), in maternal serum at the time of antenatal corticosteroid administration will predict the likelihood of delivery at <34 weeks' gestation in women with premature preterm rupture of membranes or multiple gestations.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The administration of antenatal corticosteroids (AC) to women at 24-32 weeks' gestation leads to varying durations of continuing pregnancy, with 35.7% delivering at ≥34 weeks' gestation, 46.4% delivering within 1 week of AC, and factors such as premature preterm rupture of membranes, twins, and AC administration before 28 weeks increasing the likelihood of delivery at <34 weeks.

GAP: The current study does not investigate the underlying biological mechanisms by which antenatal corticosteroids influence the duration of pregnancy, particularly in relation to the identified risk factors, nor does it explore potential biomarkers or predictors of pregnancy outcome following AC administration.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the expression levels of specific inflammatory cytokines, such as interleukin-6 (IL-6), in maternal serum at the time of antenatal corticosteroid administration will predict the likelihood of delivery at <34 weeks' gestation in women with premature preterm rupture of membranes or multiple gestations.

abstract: The pressures delivered by autotitrating continuous positive airways pressure (CPAP) devices not only treat obstructive sleep apnoea (OSA) effectively but also give potentially interesting physiological information about the forces impinging on the pharynx. In earlier work from this unit, we used correlations between autoCPAP pressure and both OSA severity and obesity, to construct an algorithm to estimate the fixed CPAP pressure a patient required for subsequent clinical use. We wished to discover if these relationships could be reliably extended to a much more obese group. We performed a prospective cohort study in an obese population. Measurements of obesity were made, OSA severity was recorded, and the 95th centile autoCPAP pressure was recorded during 1 week of autoCPAP. Spearman's rank correlation was performed between measurements of obesity and autoCPAP pressure, and between OSA severity and autoCPAP pressure. Fifty-four obese individuals (median body mass index (BMI) 43.0 kg/m(2)), 52 % of whom had OSA (apnoea-hypopnoea index (AHI) ≥ 15), had a median 95th centile autoCPAP pressure of 11.8 cmH2O. We found no significant correlation between autoCPAP pressure and neck circumference, waist circumference or BMI. There was a moderate correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity (AHI r = 0.34, p = 0.02; oxygen desaturation index (ODI) r = 0.48, p < 0.001).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity in obese individuals is mediated by the size and collapsibility of the upper airway, which can be quantitatively assessed through imaging studies and correlated with autoCPAP pressure settings to improve the predictive accuracy of CPAP therapy.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that autotitrating continuous positive airways pressure (CPAP) devices can effectively treat obstructive sleep apnoea (OSA) and provide physiological information about pharyngeal forces. In a cohort of 54 obese individuals, there was no significant correlation between autoCPAP pressure and measures of obesity (neck circumference, waist circumference, or BMI), but a moderate correlation was found between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, as measured by apnoea-hypopnoea index (AHI) and oxygen desaturation index (ODI).

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific physiological mechanisms underlie the correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity, particularly in the absence of a significant correlation with traditional measures of obesity. It also does not explore how other factors might influence the relationship between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the correlation between autoCPAP pressure and OSA severity in obese individuals is mediated by the size and collapsibility of the upper airway, which can be quantitatively assessed through imaging studies and correlated with autoCPAP pressure settings to improve the predictive accuracy of CPAP therapy.

abstract: The objective of the study was to determine whether risk of recurrent preterm birth differs based on the clinical presentation of a prior spontaneous preterm birth (SPTB): advanced cervical dilatation (ACD), preterm premature rupture of membranes (PPROM), or preterm labor (PTL). This retrospective cohort study included singleton pregnancies from 2009 to 2014 complicated by a history of prior SPTB. Women were categorized based on the clinical presentation of their prior preterm delivery as having ACD, PPROM, or PTL. Risks for sonographic short cervical length and recurrent SPTB were compared between women based on the clinical presentation of their prior preterm birth. Log-linear regression was used to control for confounders. Of 522 patients included in this study, 96 (18.4%) had prior ACD, 246 (47.1%) had prior PPROM, and 180 (34.5%) had prior PTL. Recurrent PTB occurred in 55.2% of patients with a history of ACD compared with 27.2% of those with PPROM and 32.2% with PTL (P = .001). The mean gestational age at delivery was significantly lower for those with a history of ACD (34.0 weeks) compared with women with prior PPROM (37.2 weeks) or PTL (37.0 weeks) (P = .001). The lowest mean cervical length prior to 24 weeks was significantly shorter in patients with a history of advanced cervical dilation when compared with the other clinical presentations.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that women with a history of advanced cervical dilatation will exhibit distinct alterations in the cervical microbiome composition and increased expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines in cervical tissue compared to women with a history of preterm premature rupture of membranes or pre
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the risk of recurrent preterm birth (PTB) varies significantly based on the clinical presentation of a prior spontaneous preterm birth (SPTB), with advanced cervical dilatation (ACD) having the highest risk of recurrence (55.2%) and the shortest mean gestational age at delivery (34.0 weeks). Women with a history of ACD also had the shortest mean cervical length prior to 24 weeks compared to those with preterm premature rupture of membranes (PPROM) or preterm labor (PTL).

GAP: The study identifies the risk of recurrent PTB based on the clinical presentation of prior SPTB but does not explore the underlying biological mechanisms that contribute to the increased risk of recurrence in women with a history of ACD. Specifically, it does not investigate how the cervical tissue and its microbiome may differ between women with ACD and those with other clinical presentations, potentially leaving a gap in understanding preventive or therapeutic strategies.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that women with a history of advanced cervical dilatation will exhibit distinct alterations in the cervical microbiome composition and increased expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines in cervical tissue compared to women with a history of preterm premature rupture of membranes or pre

abstract: It is unclear whether traveling long distances to high-volume centers would compensate for travel burden among patients undergoing rectal cancer resection. The purpose of this study was to determine whether operative volume outweighs the advantages of being treated locally by comparing the outcomes of patients with rectal cancer treated at local, low-volume centers versus far, high-volume centers. This was a population-based study. The National Cancer Database was queried for patients with rectal cancer. Patients with stage II or III rectal cancer who underwent surgical resection between 2006 and 2012 were included. The outcomes of interest were margins, lymph node yield, receipt of neoadjuvant chemoradiation, adjuvant chemotherapy, readmission within 30 days, 30-day and 90-day mortality, and 5-year overall survival. A total of 18,605 patients met inclusion criteria; 2067 patients were in the long-distance/high-volume group and 1362 in the short-distance/low-volume group. The median travel distance was 62.6 miles for the long-distance/high-volume group and 2.3 miles for the short-distance/low-volume group. Patients who were younger, white, privately insured, and stage III were more likely to have traveled to a high-volume center. When controlled for patient factors, stage, and hospital factors, patients in the short-distance/low-volume group had lower odds of a lymph node yield ≥12 (OR = 0.51) and neoadjuvant chemoradiation (OR = 0.67) and higher 30-day (OR = 3.38) and 90-day mortality (OR = 2.07) compared with those in the long-distance/high-volume group. The short-distance/low-volume group had a 34% high risk of overall mortality at 5 years compared with the long-distance/high-volume group. We lacked data regarding patient and physician decision making and surgeon-specific factors.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with rectal cancer who travel to high-volume centers for treatment will exhibit improved quality of life and reduced symptom burden at 6-month and 1-year follow-up, compared to those treated at local, low-volume centers, as measured by standardized patient-reported outcome measures.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients with rectal cancer who traveled to high-volume centers for treatment had better outcomes, including higher lymph node yield, increased receipt of neoadjuvant chemoradiation, lower 30-day and 90-day mortality, and improved 5-year overall survival, compared to those treated at local, low-volume centers. The benefits of treatment at high-volume centers were observed despite the increased travel burden for patients.

GAP: The study lacked data on patient and physician decision-making processes and surgeon-specific factors that influence the choice of treatment center, which could provide further insight into how to optimize treatment outcomes for patients with rectal cancer. Additionally, the study did not examine the potential impact of travel burden on patient outcomes beyond mortality and readmission rates.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with rectal cancer who travel to high-volume centers for treatment will exhibit improved quality of life and reduced symptom burden at 6-month and 1-year follow-up, compared to those treated at local, low-volume centers, as measured by standardized patient-reported outcome measures.

abstract: It is commonly accepted that pathological gambling results from the interaction of multiple risk factors. Among these, dopamine replacement therapy (DRT) prescribed for Parkinson disease can be cited. Another dopamine agonist, aripiprazole, could be a new risk factor. We decided to explore this potential adverse drug reaction (ADR). Based on a cohort of 166 pathological gamblers starting treatment in our department, data of each of the 8 patients treated by aripiprazole at inclusion were analyzed. The patients involved were schizophrenic or bipolar, mostly young men with a history of addictive disorders and regular gambling prior to the prescription of aripiprazole. For each one of them, the causality of aripiprazole was considered, using an algorithm. The probability that pathological gambling is actually due to aripiprazole is "possible" in 7 cases out of 8, and "doubtful" in one.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the risk of developing pathological gambling in patients treated with aripiprazole will increase in a dose-dependent manner, with higher doses of aripiprazole (>15mg/day) being associated with a significantly higher incidence of pathological gambling compared to lower doses (<7.5mg/day) over a 6-month treatment period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract are that aripiprazole, a dopamine agonist, may be a new risk factor for pathological gambling, with 7 out of 8 patients showing a "possible" causal link between aripiprazole treatment and the development of pathological gambling, particularly in young men with a history of addictive disorders and regular gambling.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether the dose or duration of aripiprazole treatment influences the likelihood of developing pathological gambling, as well as the potential interactions between aripiprazole and individual differences in dopamine receptor density or function that may contribute to this adverse drug reaction.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the risk of developing pathological gambling in patients treated with aripiprazole will increase in a dose-dependent manner, with higher doses of aripiprazole (>15mg/day) being associated with a significantly higher incidence of pathological gambling compared to lower doses (<7.5mg/day) over a 6-month treatment period.

abstract: Using murine models, we have shown that the lysosomotropic amine, chloroquine, is effective in the prevention of graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) mediated by donor T cells reactive with recipient minor histocompatibility antigens (MiHCs). Because lysosomotropic amines can suppress major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class II antigen presentation, their mechanism of action is potentially different from current immune suppressant drugs used to control GVHD such as cyclosporine. We investigated the use of cyclosporine and the lysosomotropic amines chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine in combination for additive or synergistic immunosuppression on T-cell responses in vitro to MiHC and MHC in mice. We found that similar concentrations of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine suppress the T-cell response to MiHC in mice (C57BL/6 anti-BALB.B) and that lysosomotropic amines in combination with cyclosporine result in synergistic suppression of a proliferative response to MiHC. Similar suppression and synergy appear to be present in an alloreactive response (C57BL/6 anti-BALB/c). Direct inhibition by chloroquine of T-cell proliferative responses induced by anti-CD3epsilon in the absence of antigen-presenting cells is present at higher concentrations than that required to suppress responses to MiHC or MHC. Chloroquine appears to induce decreased T-cell viability at high concentrations. This effect does not appear to be due to decreased T-cell production of interleukin-2 or interferon-gamma. At lower concentrations (<25 microg/ml), chloroquine can also decrease the ability of antigen-presenting cells to stimulate an a C57BL/6 anti-BALB/c T-cell response and can inhibit MHC class II expression after activation with lipopolysaccharide.
hypothesis: GAP: Despite these findings, the abstract leaves unanswered the question of how chloroquine's inhibition of MHC class II expression on antigen-presenting cells affects the specific subsets of T cells (such as regulatory T cells or effector T cells) involved in the immune response to allografts, and whether this effect contributes to its synergistic immunosuppressive action when combined with cyclo
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the effectiveness of the lysosomotropic amine chloroquine in preventing graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) in murine models, its mechanism potentially differing from traditional immune suppressants like cyclosporine by suppressing major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class II antigen presentation. Additionally, combining chloroquine or hydroxychloroquine with cyclosporine results in synergistic suppression of T-cell responses to minor histocompatibility antigens (MiHC) and MHC in mice. Chloroquine also directly inhibits T-cell proliferative responses at higher concentrations and decreases T-cell viability without affecting interleukin-2 or interferon-gamma production. At lower concentrations, chloroquine impairs antigen-presenting cells' ability to stimulate T-cell responses and inhibits MHC class II expression.

GAP: Despite these findings, the abstract leaves unanswered the question of how chloroquine's inhibition of MHC class II expression on antigen-presenting cells affects the specific subsets of T cells (such as regulatory T cells or effector T cells) involved in the immune response to allografts, and whether this effect contributes to its synergistic immunosuppressive action when combined with cyclo

abstract: To assess the outcomes of patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC) whose treatment was determined by computerized tomography (CT) and/or magnetic resonance imaging staging and to analyze the impact of induction chemotherapy and accelerated fractionated radiotherapy. The analysis is based on 122 of 143 previously untreated patients with NPC treated with radiation therapy at The University of Texas M. D. Anderson Cancer Center between 1983 and 1992. Excluded were 4 patients treated with palliative intent, 4 children, 12 patients not staged with CT, and 1 patient who died of a cerebrovascular accident prior to completion of treatment. The stage distribution was as follows: AJCC Stage I-2, Stage II-7, Stage III-12, Stage IV-101; Tl-15, T2-33, T3-22, T4-52; N0-32, N1-10, N2-47, N3-32, Nx-1. Fifty-nine (48%) patients had squamous cell carcinoma; 63 (52%) had lymphoepitheliomas, undifferentiated NPC or poorly differentiated carcinoma, NOS (UNPC). Sixty-seven patients (65 with Stage IV disease) received induction chemotherapy. Fifty-eight patients (24 of whom had induction chemotherapy) were treated with the concomitant boost fractionation schedule. The median follow-up for surviving patients was 57 months. The overall actuarial 2- and 5-year survival rates were 78 and 68%, respectively. Forty-nine patients (40%) had disease recurrence. Thirty-three (27%) had local regional failures; 19 at the primary site only, 8 in the neck and 6 in both. Local failure occurred in 31% of patients staged T4 compared to 13% of T1-T3 (p = 0.007). Sixteen patients failed at distant sites alone. Among Stage IV patients the 5-year actuarial rates for patients who did and did not receive induction chemotherapy were as follows: overall survival: 68 vs. 56% (p = 0.02), freedom from relapse: 64 vs. 37% (p = 0.01), and local control: 86 vs. 56% (p = 0.009). The actuarial 5-year distant failure rate in patients with UNPC who were treated with induction chemotherapy and controlled in the primary and neck was 13%. In patients who did not receive chemotherapy, the actuarial 5-year local control rates for patients treated with concomitant boost or conventional fractionation were 66 and 67%, respectively.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific biomarkers associated with tumor aggressiveness and treatment response, such as Epstein-Barr virus DNA levels and p53 mutation status, will be significantly altered in patients with nasoph
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the overall actuarial 2- and 5-year survival rates for patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC) being 78% and 68%, respectively, with 40% of patients experiencing disease recurrence. The study also found that induction chemotherapy improved overall survival, freedom from relapse, and local control in Stage IV patients, and that local failure occurred more frequently in patients with T4 staged tumors. Additionally, the 5-year actuarial distant failure rate was lower in patients with undifferentiated NPC who received induction chemotherapy and had local control.

GAP: One of the knowledge gaps left unanswered by this study is the specific biological mechanisms by which induction chemotherapy exerts its beneficial effects on overall survival and local control in patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma, particularly in relation to the tumor's histological type and stage. The study provides evidence for the clinical efficacy of induction chemotherapy but does not explore the underlying molecular or cellular changes that contribute to these outcomes.

abstract: Treatment of neonatal testicular torsion has two objectives: salvage of the involved testicle (which is rarely achieved) and preservation of the contralateral gonad. The second goal universally involves contralateral testicular scrotal fixation to prevent the future occurrence of contralateral torsion. However, there is controversy with regards to management of a synchronous contralateral hydrocele. It has been our policy not to address the contralateral hydrocele through an inguinal incision to minimize potential injury to the spermatic cord. Our objective in this study was to determine whether the decision to manage a contralateral hydrocele in cases of neonatal testicular torsion solely through a scrotal approach is safe and effective. We reviewed all cases of neonatal testicular torsion occurring at our institution between the years 1999 and 2006. Age at presentation, physical examination, ultrasonographic and intraoperative findings were recorded. Patients were followed after initial surgical intervention to determine the likelihood of developing a subsequent hydrocele or hernia. Thirty-seven patients were identified as presenting with neonatal torsion. Age of presentation averaged 3.5 days (range 1-14 days). Left-sided pathology was seen more commonly than the right, with a 25:12 distribution. All torsed testicles were nonviable. Twenty-two patients were noted to have a contralateral hydrocele at presentation. All hydroceles were opened through a scrotal approach at the time of contralateral scrotal fixation. No patient underwent an inguinal exploration to examine for a patent process vaginalis. None of the patients who presented with a hydrocele have developed a clinical hydrocele or hernia after an average 7.5 years (range 4.3-11.2) follow-up.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with neonatal testicular torsion who undergo contralateral hydrocele management via a scrotal approach will exhibit comparable fertility outcomes, including sperm quality and testicular function, to those without a history of testicular torsion or hydrocele, when evaluated at adulthood.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that in cases of neonatal testicular torsion, managing a synchronous contralateral hydrocele through a scrotal approach without inguinal exploration is safe and effective, with no patients developing a clinical hydrocele or hernia after an average 7.5 years of follow-up. All torsed testicles were nonviable, and left-sided pathology was more common than right-sided. The study also established that contralateral scrotal fixation can be successfully performed without addressing the hydrocele through an inguinal incision, minimizing potential injury to the spermatic cord.

GAP: The study does not investigate the long-term effects of this management strategy on fertility outcomes, such as sperm quality, testicular function, or the potential for future testicular complications in patients who underwent scrotal approach for contralateral hydrocele management in the context of neonatal testicular torsion.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with neonatal testicular torsion who undergo contralateral hydrocele management via a scrotal approach will exhibit comparable fertility outcomes, including sperm quality and testicular function, to those without a history of testicular torsion or hydrocele, when evaluated at adulthood.

abstract: The purpose of this study is to measure the accuracy and reliability of normally sighted, visually impaired, and blind pedestrians at making street crossing decisions using visual and/or auditory information. Using a 5-point rating scale, safety ratings for vehicular gaps of different durations were measured along a two-lane street of one-way traffic without a traffic signal. Safety ratings were collected from 12 normally sighted, 10 visually impaired, and 10 blind subjects for eight different gap times under three sensory conditions: (1) visual plus auditory information, (2) visual information only, and (3) auditory information only. Accuracy and reliability in street crossing decision-making were calculated for each subject under each sensory condition. We found that normally sighted and visually impaired pedestrians were accurate and reliable in their street crossing decision-making ability when using either vision plus hearing or vision only (P>0.05). Under the hearing only condition, all subjects were reliable (P>0.05) but inaccurate with their street crossing decisions (P<0.05). Compared to either the normally sighted (P = 0.018) or visually impaired subjects (P = 0.019), blind subjects were the least accurate with their street crossing decisions under the hearing only condition.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the implementation of standardized, amplified, and spectrally enhanced auditory cues at street crossings will significantly improve the accuracy of street crossing decisions for blind pedestrians under hearing-only conditions, compared to standard auditory cues currently in use.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study indicate that normally sighted and visually impaired pedestrians are accurate and reliable in their street crossing decisions when using visual or combined visual and auditory information, but all subjects, including blind individuals, are less accurate when relying solely on auditory information. Specifically, blind subjects were found to be the least accurate in their street crossing decisions under the hearing-only condition compared to normally sighted and visually impaired subjects.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of how the accuracy of street crossing decisions under auditory-only conditions can be improved for all pedestrians, especially for blind individuals. It does not explore potential interventions or factors that could enhance auditory cues to make them more reliable for decision-making.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the implementation of standardized, amplified, and spectrally enhanced auditory cues at street crossings will significantly improve the accuracy of street crossing decisions for blind pedestrians under hearing-only conditions, compared to standard auditory cues currently in use.

abstract: Sulfasalazine is a widely used anti-inflammatory agent in the treatment of inflammatory bowel disease and several rheumatological disorders. Although as many as 20% of treated patients may experience reversible, dose-dependent side effects, less frequent but potentially severe, systemic reactions have also been reported. A severe systemic reaction to sulfasalazine developed in a 21-year old female with rheumatoid arthritis characterized by eosinophilia, granulomatous enteritis and myelotoxicity, cholestatic hepatitis, and seizures. The clinical course and management of this patient are presented as well as a review of the incidence and outcome of severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that genetic variations in the cytochrome P450 enzyme system, specifically in the CYP2C9 gene, will be significantly more prevalent in patients who experience severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine compared to those who only experience mild or no side effects.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The abstract reports on a case of a severe systemic reaction to sulfasalazine in a 21-year-old female with rheumatoid arthritis, characterized by multiple symptoms including eosinophilia, granulomatous enteritis, myelotoxicity, cholestatic hepatitis, and seizures, indicating that sulfasalazine can cause severe and varied systemic reactions in some patients.

GAP: The abstract highlights a severe systemic reaction but does not investigate or explain the underlying mechanisms or genetic predispositions that might make certain individuals more susceptible to such extreme reactions, leaving a gap in understanding why only a subset of patients experience these severe side effects.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that genetic variations in the cytochrome P450 enzyme system, specifically in the CYP2C9 gene, will be significantly more prevalent in patients who experience severe systemic reactions to sulfasalazine compared to those who only experience mild or no side effects.

abstract: To determine the practices and knowledge of harmful effects regarding use of Chaalia and Pan Masala in three schools of Mahmoodabad and Chanesar Goth, Jamshed Town, Karachi, Pakistan. To achieve the objective a cross-sectional design was used in three government schools of Mahmoodabad and Chanesar Goth, Jamshed Town, Karachi. Students of either gender drawn from these schools fulfilling the inclusion and exclusion criteria were interviewed using a pre-coded structured questionnaire. Along with demographic data, questions regarding frequency of Chaalia and Pan Masala use, practices of this habit in friends and family and place of procurement of these substances, were inquired. Knowledge was assessed about harmful effects and its source of information. In addition, practices in relation to that knowledge were assessed. A total of 370 students were interviewed over a period of six weeks, of which 205 (55.4%) were boys. The ages of the students were between 10 and 15 years. Thirty one percent of the fathers and 62% of the mothers were uneducated. The frequency of use of any brand of Chaalia was found to be 94% and that of Pan Masala was 73.8%. Eighty five percent of them were regular users. A large majority (88%) procured the substances themselves from near their homes. Ninety five percent of the children had friends with the same habits. Eighty four percent were using the substances in full knowledge of their families. Chaalia was considered harmful for health by 96% and Pan Masala by 60%. Good taste was cited as a reason for continuing the habit by 88.5% of the children and use by friends by 57%. Knowledge about established harmful effects was variable. Knowledge about harmful effects was high in both "daily" and "less than daily users".
hypothesis: We hypothesize that a targeted educational intervention focusing on the specific health risks of Chaalia and Pan Masala will significantly reduce the frequency of consumption among adolescent users who report daily or near-daily use of these substances.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that a high percentage of students (94% for Chaalia and 73.8% for Pan Masala) use these substances, with the majority procuring them near their homes and using them with the knowledge of their families. Despite acknowledging the harmful health effects (96% for Chaalia and 60% for Pan Masala), the students continue the habit mainly due to the good taste and peer influence. The knowledge about established harmful effects varies among users.

GAP: The study does not investigate the potential correlation between the frequency of use and the awareness of specific health risks associated with Chaalia and Pan Masala consumption, nor does it explore interventions that could effectively reduce the consumption of these substances among adolescents.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that a targeted educational intervention focusing on the specific health risks of Chaalia and Pan Masala will significantly reduce the frequency of consumption among adolescent users who report daily or near-daily use of these substances.

abstract: Tinnitus can be related to many different aetiologies such as hearing loss or a noise trauma, but it can also be related to the somatosensory system of the cervical spine, called cervicogenic somatic tinnitus (CST). Case studies suggest a positive effect of cervical spine treatment on tinnitus complaints in patients with CST, but no experimental studies are available. To investigate the effect of a multimodal cervical physical therapy treatment on tinnitus complaints in patients with CST. Randomized controlled trial. Patients with a combination of severe subjective tinnitus (Tinnitus Functional Index (TFI): 25-90 points) and neck complaints (Neck Bournemouth Questionnaire (NBQ) > 14 points). All patients received cervical physical therapy for 6 weeks (12 sessions). Patients were randomized in an immediate-start therapy group (n = 19) and a 6-week delayed-start therapy group (n = 19). TFI and NBQ-scores were documented at baseline, after the wait-and-see period in the delayed-start group, after treatment and after 6 weeks follow-up. The Global Perceived Effect (GPE) was documented at all measuring moments, except at baseline. In all patients (n = 38) TFI and NBQ-scores decreased significantly after treatment (p = 0.04 and p < 0.001). NBQ-scores remained significantly lower after follow-up (p = 0.001). Immediately after treatment, 53% (n = 38) experienced substantial improvement of tinnitus. This effect was maintained in 24% of patients after follow-up at six weeks.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with CST who exhibit greater improvements in cervical range of motion and reduced neck muscle tension following multimodal cervical physical therapy will demonstrate more sustained reductions in tinnitus severity, as measured by TFI scores, at 12-week follow-up compared to those with lesser improvements in cervical function.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that a multimodal cervical physical therapy treatment significantly decreased tinnitus complaints and neck complaints in patients with cervicogenic somatic tinnitus (CST), with 53% of patients experiencing substantial improvement immediately after treatment and 24% maintaining this improvement after a 6-week follow-up. The treatment also resulted in significant decreases in Tinnitus Functional Index (TFI) and Neck Bournemouth Questionnaire (NBQ) scores, which were maintained for NBQ scores after follow-up.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the effectiveness of cervical physical therapy in reducing tinnitus complaints in patients with CST, it does not investigate the specific mechanisms by which this therapy exerts its effects, nor does it explore predictors of long-term response to treatment. Specifically, the study does not examine how individual differences in treatment response might be related to specific characteristics of the patients' tinnitus or cervical spine function.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with CST who exhibit greater improvements in cervical range of motion and reduced neck muscle tension following multimodal cervical physical therapy will demonstrate more sustained reductions in tinnitus severity, as measured by TFI scores, at 12-week follow-up compared to those with lesser improvements in cervical function.

abstract: The technique of induced sputum has allowed to subdivide asthma patients into inflammatory phenotypes according to their level of granulocyte airway infiltration. There are very few studies which looked at detailed sputum and blood cell counts in a large cohort of asthmatics divided into inflammatory phenotypes. The purpose of this study was to analyze sputum cell counts, blood leukocytes and systemic inflammatory markers in these phenotypes, and investigate how those groups compared with healthy subjects. We conducted a retrospective cross-sectional study on 833 asthmatics recruited from the University Asthma Clinic of Liege and compared them with 194 healthy subjects. Asthmatics were classified into inflammatory phenotypes. The total non-squamous cell count per gram of sputum was greater in mixed granulocytic and neutrophilic phenotypes as compared to eosinophilic, paucigranulocytic asthma and healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Sputum eosinophils (in absolute values and percentages) were increased in all asthma phenotypes including paucigranulocytic asthma, compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Eosinophilic asthma showed higher absolute sputum neutrophil and lymphocyte counts than healthy subjects (p < 0.005), while neutrophilic asthmatics had a particularly low number of sputum macrophages and epithelial cells. All asthma phenotypes showed an increased blood leukocyte count compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005), with paucigranulocytic asthmatics having also increased absolute blood eosinophils compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005). Neutrophilic asthma had raised CRP and fibrinogen while eosinophilic asthma only showed raised fibrinogen compared to healthy subjects (p < 0.005).

raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that asthmatic patients can be subdivided into inflammatory phenotypes based on their level of granulocyte airway infiltration, and each phenotype exhibits distinct characteristics in terms of sputum cell counts, blood leukocytes, and systemic inflammatory markers compared to healthy subjects. Specifically, the mixed granulocytic and neutrophilic phenotypes have higher total non-squamous cell counts, eosinophilic asthma has higher absolute sputum neutrophil and lymphocyte counts, and all asthma phenotypes have increased blood leukocyte counts. Additionally, certain phenotypes, such as neutrophilic asthma, exhibit elevated levels of systemic inflammatory markers like CRP and fibrinogen.

GAP: While the study provides valuable insights into the inflammatory phenotypes of asthma and their characteristics, it leaves unanswered the question of how these phenotypes respond to treatment and whether the observed differences in sputum cell counts, blood leukocytes, and systemic inflammatory markers can be used to predict treatment outcomes. Specifically, the study does not investigate the relationship between inflammatory phenotype and response to therapy, which is crucial for personalized medicine approaches.

abstract: To assess the impact of the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program established in Jamaica since the late 1980s on the HIV/AIDS epidemic. AIDS case reports, HIV testing of blood donors, antenatal clinic attenders (ANC), food service workers, sexually transmitted disease (STD) clinic attenders, female prostitutes, homosexuals and other groups were used to monitor the HIV/AIDS epidemic. Primary and secondary syphilis and cases of congenital syphilis were also monitored. National knowledge, attitude and practice (KAP) surveys were conducted in 1988, 1989, 1992, 1994 and 1996. The annual AIDS incidence rate in Jamaica increased only marginally in the past three years from 18.5 per 100000 population to 21.4 in 1997. HIV prevalence in the general population groups tested has been about 1% or less. Among those at high risk, HIV prevalence rates have risen to 6.3% (95% confidence interval 5.0-8.0) in STD clinic attenders, around 10% and 21% in female prostitutes in Kingston and Montego Bay respectively and approximately 30% among homosexuals. Syphilis rates and congenital syphilis cases have declined. The proportion of men aged 15-49 years reporting sex with a non-regular partner declined from 35% in 1994 to 26% in 1996 (P<0.001). Women ever having used condoms increased from 51% in 1988 to 62.5% in 1992 and 73% in 1994 and 1996 (P<0.001), while condom use with a non-regular partner increased from 37% in 1992 to 73% in 1996 (P= 0.006). Condom use among men was high over the period: around 81% had ever used condoms and 77% used them with non-regular partners. Gay men, inner-city adults and adolescents aged 12-14 years all reported increases in condom use while condom sales and distribution increased from around 2 million in 1985 to 10 million in 1995.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that targeted interventions within the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program, focusing on high-risk groups such as female prostitutes and homosexuals, will result in a significant reduction in new HIV infections among these populations over a two-year period, as measured by a decrease in HIV seroconversion rates compared to a control group not receiving targeted interventions.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include a marginal increase in the annual AIDS incidence rate in Jamaica, low HIV prevalence in the general population but higher rates among high-risk groups, a decline in syphilis rates and congenital syphilis cases, and an increase in condom use among various groups, including men, women, gay men, inner-city adults, and adolescents.

GAP: Despite the observed increase in condom use and decline in syphilis rates, the abstract leaves unanswered the question of how the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program specifically impacts the HIV transmission dynamics among high-risk groups, such as female prostitutes and homosexuals, in terms of reducing new HIV infections over time.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that targeted interventions within the comprehensive HIV/STD Control Program, focusing on high-risk groups such as female prostitutes and homosexuals, will result in a significant reduction in new HIV infections among these populations over a two-year period, as measured by a decrease in HIV seroconversion rates compared to a control group not receiving targeted interventions.

abstract: The morbidity and mortality associated with Panton-Valentine leucocidin (PVL)-positive Staphylococcus aureus suggest that this toxin is a key marker of disease severity. Nevertheless, the importance of PVL in the pathogenesis of primary bacteraemia caused by S. aureus is uncertain. We have determined the prevalence of PVL-encoding genes among isolates of S. aureus from bacteraemic patients. Consecutive bacteraemia isolates of S. aureus (n=244) from patients hospitalized in 25 centres in the UK and Ireland during 2005 were screened for PVL and mecA genes. PVL-positive isolates were characterized by toxin gene profiling, PFGE, spa-typing and MIC determinations for a range of antimicrobials. Four out of 244 isolates (1.6%) were PVL-positive and susceptible to oxacillin [methicillin-susceptible S. aureus (MSSA)]. Eighty-eight out of 244 (36%) were oxacillin-resistant (methicillin-resistant S. aureus), but none was PVL-positive. The four patients (two males: 30 and 33 years; two females: 62 and 80 years) had infection foci of: skin and soft tissue, unknown, indwelling line, and surgical site, and were located at one centre in Wales, one in England and two in Ireland. One of four PVL-positive isolates was resistant to penicillin and fusidic acid, the remainder were susceptible to all antibiotics tested. Genotypic analyses showed that the four isolates represented three distinct strains; the two isolates from Ireland were related.

raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that PVL-positive Staphylococcus aureus isolates are relatively rare (1.6% of 244 isolates) and are associated with methicillin-susceptible S. aureus (MSSA), whereas methicillin-resistant S. aureus (MRSA) isolates are not PVL-positive. The PVL-positive isolates represented three distinct strains and were generally susceptible to antibiotics, except for one isolate that was resistant to penicillin and fusidic acid. The infection foci of PVL-positive isolates varied, including skin and soft tissue, unknown, indwelling line, and surgical site.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of how the presence of PVL toxin affects the clinical outcome of bacteraemia caused by MSSA, particularly in comparison to PVL-negative MSSA infections. The study describes the prevalence and characteristics of PVL-positive isolates but does not investigate the impact of PVL on disease severity or patient outcomes.

abstract: Impaired fasting glucose (IFG) below the diagnostic threshold for diabetes mellitus (DM) is associated with macrovascular pathology and increased mortality after percutaneous coronary interventions. The study goal was to determine whether pre-operative fasting blood glucose (fB-glu) is associated with an increased mortality after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG). During 2001-03, 1895 patients underwent primary CABG [clinical DM (CDM) in 440/1895; complete data on fB-glu for n=1375/1455]. Using pre-operative fB-glu, non-diabetics were categorized as having normal fB-glu (<5.6 mmol/L), IFG (5.6<or =fB-glu<6.1 mmol/L), or suspected DM (SDM) (>or =6.1 mmol/L). fB-glu was normal in 59%. The relative risks of 30 day and 1 year mortality compared with patients with normal fB-glu was 1.7 [95% confidence interval (CI): 0.5-5.5] and 2.9 (CI: 0.8-11.2) with IFG, 2.8 (CI: 1.1-7.2) and 1.9 (CI: 0.5-6.3) with SDM vs. 1.8 (CI: 0.8-4.0) and 1.6 (CI: 0.6-4.3) if CDM, respectively. The receiver operator characteristic area for the continuous variable fB-glu and 1 year mortality was 0.65 (P=0.002).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with impaired fasting glucose (5.6≤fB-glu<6.1 mmol/L) will exhibit increased levels of systemic inflammation, as measured by elevated C-reactive protein (CRP) and interleukin-6 (IL-6
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that impaired fasting glucose (IFG) and suspected diabetes mellitus (SDM) are associated with increased mortality after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG), with relative risks of 30-day and 1-year mortality being higher in patients with IFG and SDM compared to those with normal fasting blood glucose (fB-glu) levels. The receiver operator characteristic area for the continuous variable fB-glu and 1-year mortality was 0.65, indicating a moderate predictive value of fB-glu for 1-year mortality.

GAP: The study establishes an association between IFG, SDM, and increased mortality after CABG, but it does not investigate the underlying mechanisms by which impaired fasting glucose levels contribute to higher mortality rates in these patients. Specifically, it does not explore how IFG and SDM affect the inflammatory response, vascular function, or other physiological processes that could influence outcomes after CABG.
