However, at follow-up assessment there was no significant difference between patient groups nor did any consistent pattern emerge.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the implementation of standardized, socioeconomic-status-sensitive care pathways during the initial three months of treatment will lead to a significant reduction in the disparity of quality of life scores between lung cancer patients of lower and higher socioeconomic status, as measured by the EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13 questionnaires, at the six-month follow-up assessment.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found significant differences in quality of life scores between lung cancer patients of lower socioeconomic status and those of higher socioeconomic status at baseline, with lower socioeconomic status patients having more health problems, less functioning, and more symptoms. Specifically, significant differences were found in physical mobility, energy, role functioning, physical functioning, and breathlessness at baseline. However, these differences disappeared at the three-month follow-up assessment.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what factors contribute to the disappearance of socioeconomic disparities in quality of life scores between lung cancer patients of lower and higher socioeconomic status over time, particularly during the initial treatment period.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the implementation of standardized, socioeconomic-status-sensitive care pathways during the initial three months of treatment will lead to a significant reduction in the disparity of quality of life scores between lung cancer patients of lower and higher socioeconomic status, as measured by the EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13 questionnaires, at the six-month follow-up assessment.

abstract: The levels of bone formation and resorption can be assessed at the tissue level by bone histomorphometry on transiliac bone biopsies. Systemic biochemical markers of bone turnover reflect the overall bone formation and resorption at the level of the entire skeleton but cannot discriminate the different skeletal compartments. Our aim was to investigate the correlations between the serum biochemical markers of formation and resorption with histomorphometric parameters. We performed post hoc analysis of a previous clinical study. Patients were selected from the general population. A total of 371 untreated postmenopausal osteoporotic women aged 50 to 84 years with a lumbar T-score ≤ -2.5 SD or ≤ -1 SD with at least one osteoporotic fracture. Transiliac bone biopsies were obtained after a double tetracycline labeling, and blood samples were collected. The static and dynamic parameters of formation and bone resorption were measured by histomorphometry. Serum biochemical markers of formation (bone alkaline phosphatase [ALP]; procollagen type I N-terminal propeptide [PINP]) and resorption (C-terminal crosslinking telopeptide of collagen type 1 [sCTX]) were assessed. The mean values of biochemical markers were: bone ALP, 15.0 ± 5.2 ng/mL; PINP, 56.2 ± 21.9 μg/mL; and sCTX, 0.58 ± 0.26 ng/mL. Bone ALP and PINP were significantly correlated with both the static and dynamic parameters of formation (0.21 ≤ r' ≤ 0.36; 0.01 ≥ P ≥ .0001). sCTX was significantly correlated with all resorption parameters (0.18 ≤ r' ≤ 0.24; 0.02 ≥ P ≥ .0001).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the ratio of serum bone alkaline phosphatase to C-terminal crosslinking telopeptide of collagen type 1 will predict the annual rate of bone mineral density loss in
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that serum biochemical markers of bone formation (bone alkaline phosphatase and procollagen type I N-terminal propeptide) and resorption (C-terminal crosslinking telopeptide of collagen type 1) are significantly correlated with histomorphometric parameters of bone formation and resorption in postmenopausal osteoporotic women. Specifically, bone alkaline phosphatase and procollagen type I N-terminal propeptide are correlated with static and dynamic parameters of formation, while C-terminal crosslinking telopeptide of collagen type 1 is correlated with all resorption parameters.

GAP: Despite the correlations found between serum biochemical markers and histomorphometric parameters, the study leaves unanswered the question of whether these correlations can be used to predict the rate of bone loss or the risk of osteoporotic fractures in individual patients. Additionally, the study does not investigate the potential impact of intervening variables, such as lifestyle or genetic factors, on the relationship between biochemical markers and bone health.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the ratio of serum bone alkaline phosphatase to C-terminal crosslinking telopeptide of collagen type 1 will predict the annual rate of bone mineral density loss in

abstract: We sought to determine the feasibility and assess the clinical outcomes associated with an early extubation strategy for all children undergoing congenital heart surgery, including neonates (age,<30 days). We performed a linked database analysis of all patients undergoing congenital heart surgery from July 1, 2010 to December 31, 2012. We collected data on the cardiac diagnoses, preoperative status, procedure, and postoperative course, including the duration of invasive and noninvasive ventilation, failure of extubation, hemodynamic data, length of stay, complications, and mortality. A multivariable model was used to assess the independent factors associated with an inability to extubate within the operating room and with delayed extubation (>24 hours). We operated on 613 children, including 97 neonates. Intraoperative extubation was achieved in 71% of the cases and early extubation (≤ 24 hours) was achieved in 89% of the cases. The overall mortality was 1.5% (9 of 613 patients). Early extubation was associated with lower mortality (1% vs 9%, P<.001) and a lower rate of reintubation (4% vs 23%, P<.001) compared with delayed extubation. Notably, 63% of the neonates were extubated within 24 hours, including 67% of arterial switch operations and 54% of total anomalous pulmonary venous return repairs. Norwood operations were the only procedure in which no patient was extubated within the first 24 hours. Multivariable logistic regression demonstrated that the predictors of delayed extubation included preoperative mechanical ventilation, weight<5 kg, a longer procedure time, and the need for postoperative inotrope support. Implementation of an early extubation strategy was associated with low rates of complications (5.1 per 10 procedures), short lengths of intensive care unit stay (median, 1 day; interquartile range, 1-3), and short hospital stays (median, 4 days; interquartile range, 3-6).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the use of total intravenous anesthesia with propofol and remifentanil, as compared to inhalational anesthesia, will result in a significant reduction in the time to
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the feasibility and success of an early extubation strategy in children undergoing congenital heart surgery, with 89% of cases achieving extubation within 24 hours, and this strategy being associated with lower mortality and reintubation rates. Additionally, predictors of delayed extubation were identified, including preoperative mechanical ventilation, low weight, longer procedure time, and need for postoperative inotrope support. The study also noted specific outcomes for different types of surgical procedures, with Norwood operations being the only ones where no patient was extubated within the first 24 hours.

GAP: Despite the identification of predictors for delayed extubation and the overall success of early extubation, the abstract leaves unanswered the question of how specific intraoperative anesthetic management strategies (e.g., types of anesthetics used, depth of anesthesia monitoring) influence the success of early extubation in this patient population. Understanding these factors could further optimize the early extubation strategy and improve outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the use of total intravenous anesthesia with propofol and remifentanil, as compared to inhalational anesthesia, will result in a significant reduction in the time to

abstract: To explore the impact of delivery mode on women's postpartum quality of life in rural China and probe factors influencing postnatal quality of life. Childbirth significantly affects puerpera's physical, psychological and social domains of quality of life. Under the circumstance of increasing high caesarean section rate in rural China, the impact of delivery mode on postnatal quality of life remains unclear. Cross-sectional study design. Women residing in rural areas and in their 0-12 months after childbirth from 30 rural townships participated in a household survey. A structured questionnaire was used to evaluate women's socio-demographic characteristics, previous pregnant experiences, foetal characteristics and use of maternal health services. The scale for rural postnatal quality of life was adopted to assess postnatal quality of life from six dimensions: physical complaints and pain, sleep and energy, sex satisfaction, interpersonal communication, self-evaluated living stress and perceived life satisfaction. The overall caeserean section rate was 70·0% (962/1375), and most of them (59·7%) were selected by maternal request. None of six dimensions and total score of quality of life displayed significant difference between women with normal delivery and cesaerean section. It was found that postnatal home visit related to good postnatal quality of life and lower husband education level, male gender of infant were associated with poor quality of life.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that women who undergo caesarean sections by maternal request will exhibit a significant decline in physical and psychological quality of life scores 2-5 years postpartum compared to those who have normal deliveries, independent of the effects of postnatal home visits and socio-demographic factors.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the high caesarean section rate (70.0%) in rural China, with most of these procedures chosen by maternal request. Despite this, there were no significant differences found in postnatal quality of life between women who had a normal delivery and those who had a caesarean section. Additionally, postnatal home visits were associated with good postnatal quality of life, while lower husband education level and having a male infant were linked to poor quality of life.

GAP: One of the main knowledge gaps left unanswered by this study is the potential long-term effects of delivery mode on postpartum quality of life, particularly considering the high rate of caesarean sections by maternal request. The study's cross-sectional design and focus on the 0-12 months postpartum period do not provide insight into how these factors might influence quality of life beyond the first year after childbirth.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that women who undergo caesarean sections by maternal request will exhibit a significant decline in physical and psychological quality of life scores 2-5 years postpartum compared to those who have normal deliveries, independent of the effects of postnatal home visits and socio-demographic factors.

abstract: Irregular bleeding affects many users of combined menopausal hormone therapy (HT) and commonly leads to invasive and expensive investigations to exclude underlying malignancy. In most cases no abnormality is found. The main objective of this study was to explore the role of uterine natural killer (uNK) cells and their regulatory cytokine IL-15 in irregular bleeding in HT users. This was a prospective observational study conducted between 2002 and 2004. The study was conducted in a tertiary referral menopause clinic at King Edward Memorial Hospital, Western Australia. Patients included 117 postmenopausal women taking combined HT. Outpatient endometrial biopsies were taken during and outside bleeding episodes. The relationship between endometrial uNK cells (CD56+) and bleeding patterns was measured. We also addressed the impact of HT exposure on uNK cell populations, the relationship between endometrial IL-15 expression and uNK cell populations, and killer Ig like receptor genotype in subjects with irregular bleeding. Endometrial CD56+ uNK cells were significantly increased in biopsies obtained during bleeding episodes (P<0.001), compared with HT users with no bleeding. The highest level of IL-15 expression was also seen in biopsies taken during bleeding. No clear relationship between killer Ig like receptor genotype and bleeding on HT was observed.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that inhibition of IL-15 expression in the endometrium will reduce the frequency and severity of irregular bleeding episodes in postmenopausal women undergoing combined hormone therapy by decreasing the proliferation and activity of uterine natural killer cells.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that endometrial CD56+ uNK cells are significantly increased in biopsies obtained during bleeding episodes in postmenopausal women taking combined hormone therapy (HT), and that the highest level of IL-15 expression is also seen in biopsies taken during bleeding. Additionally, no clear relationship was observed between killer Ig like receptor genotype and bleeding on HT.

GAP: The study establishes a correlation between uNK cells, IL-15 expression, and irregular bleeding in HT users but does not elucidate the functional mechanisms by which increased uNK cells and IL-15 expression contribute to bleeding, nor does it explore potential therapeutic strategies to modulate uNK cell activity or IL-15 expression to prevent or reduce irregular bleeding in this population.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that inhibition of IL-15 expression in the endometrium will reduce the frequency and severity of irregular bleeding episodes in postmenopausal women undergoing combined hormone therapy by decreasing the proliferation and activity of uterine natural killer cells.

abstract: We evaluated the differences between percutaneous ethanol injection with and without aspiration of ethanol-mixed fluid for treatment of benign cystic thyroid nodules. We examined 60 patients with benign cystic thyroid nodules confirmed by fine-needle aspiration biopsy and divided them into 2 groups according to nonaspiration (group A, n = 30) or aspiration (group B, n = 30) of ethanol-mixed fluid after intracystic ethanol injection. We evaluated in both groups the complete disappearance of the cystic portion of the thyroid nodule on follow-up ultrasonography (first follow-up ultrasonography; mean, 4.6 months in group A; mean, 4.4 months in group B) (chi-square test), side effects or complications during and after the procedure (chi-square test), and the total procedure time (Student t test). Most patients showed complete disappearance of the cystic portion of the thyroid nodule (group A, n = 29; group B, n = 28), and they revealed no recurrence on follow-up ultrasonography. There was no statistical difference in the success rates between group A and group B (P>.05). Pain, the most common side effect, and other mild side effects or complications occurred in small numbers of patients in each group, but there was no significant difference in side effects or complications between the 2 groups (P>.05), except for intracystic hemorrhage (P<.05) and the complaint of all group B patients due to a double puncture (P<.001). The total procedure time was nearly double in group B than in group A because of the additional procedures, such as complete evacuation of the ethanol-mixed fluid and the 10-minute compression.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients treated with percutaneous ethanol injection for benign cystic thyroid nodules will exhibit no significant changes in thyroid function, as measured by thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels and free thyroxine (FT4) levels, at a follow-up
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that percutaneous ethanol injection with or without aspiration of ethanol-mixed fluid is effective in treating benign cystic thyroid nodules, with high success rates (29/30 in group A and 28/30 in group B) and no significant difference in success rates between the two groups. However, the aspiration group (group B) experienced more side effects, such as intracystic hemorrhage and discomfort due to double puncture, and had a longer procedure time compared to the non-aspiration group (group A).

GAP: The study does not investigate the long-term effects of percutaneous ethanol injection on the thyroid gland, particularly the potential impact on thyroid function. The follow-up period was relatively short (mean of 4.6 months in group A and 4.4 months in group B), and it is unclear whether the treatment has any delayed consequences on thyroid hormone production or the development of new thyroid nodules.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients treated with percutaneous ethanol injection for benign cystic thyroid nodules will exhibit no significant changes in thyroid function, as measured by thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels and free thyroxine (FT4) levels, at a follow-up

abstract: Cholecystectomy for GB polyps that are larger than 10 mm is generally recommended because of the high probability of neoplasm. In contrast, a follow-up strategy is preferred for GB polyps smaller than 10 mm. However, there are no treatment guidelines for polyps that grow in size during the follow-up period.STUDY: We retrospectively investigated 145 patients with GB polyps who underwent at least 1 ultrasonographic follow-up examination over an interval greater than 6 months, before cholecystectomy at Samsung medical center, South Korea, from 1994 to 2007. The growth rate was determined based on the change in size per time interval between 2 ultrasonographic examinations (mm/mo). The median age of the patients was 48 years (range: 25 to 75). One hundred twenty-five non-neoplastic polyps and 20 neoplastic polyps were found. Neoplastic polyps were more frequently found in patients older than 60 years, those with hypertension, a polyp size greater than 10 mm, and a rapid growth rate greater than 0.6 mm/mo. On multivariate analysis, however, the growth rate was not related to the neoplastic nature of a polyp, but older age (>60 y) and large size (>10 mm) were significantly associated with neoplastic polyps.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific cellular senescence markers in gallbladder polyps will be significantly higher in patients over 60 years old with polyps larger than 10 mm, compared to younger patients with smaller polyps, and will correlate with the presence of neoplastic characteristics.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that neoplastic polyps are more common in patients older than 60 years, those with hypertension, polyp size greater than 10 mm, and a rapid growth rate greater than 0.6 mm/mo. However, multivariate analysis revealed that the growth rate is not related to the neoplastic nature of a polyp, but older age and large size are significantly associated with neoplastic polyps.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific biological mechanisms underlie the association between older age, large polyp size, and increased likelihood of neoplasia, particularly since growth rate was not a significant factor in the multivariate analysis. This gap suggests a need to investigate other factors that could contribute to the development of neoplastic polyps, especially in older adults.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the expression of specific cellular senescence markers in gallbladder polyps will be significantly higher in patients over 60 years old with polyps larger than 10 mm, compared to younger patients with smaller polyps, and will correlate with the presence of neoplastic characteristics.

abstract: Prior literature identified the use of Performance Measurement Systems (PMS) as crucial in addressing improved processes of care. Moreover, a strategic use of PMS has been found to enhance quality, compared to non-strategic use, although a clear understanding of this linkage is still to be achieved. This paper deals with the test of direct and indirect models related to the link between the strategic use of PMS and the level of improved processes in health care organizations. Indirect models were mediated by the degree of perceived managerial discretion. A PLS analysis on a survey of 97 Italian managers working for health care organizations in the Lombardy region was conducted. The response rate was 77.6%. The strategic use of PMS in health care organizations directly and significantly (p < 0.001) enhances performance in terms of improved processes. Perceived managerial discretion is positively and significantly (p < 0.001) affected by the strategic use of PMS, whereas the mediation effect is non-significant.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the frequency of Performance Measurement System (PMS) use, measured as the number of times PMS data is reviewed and acted upon per quarter, will moderate the relationship between strategic PMS use and the level of improved processes in healthcare organizations, such that more frequent use will be associated with greater process improvements.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The strategic use of Performance Measurement Systems (PMS) in health care organizations directly and significantly enhances performance in terms of improved processes, and perceived managerial discretion is positively and significantly affected by the strategic use of PMS, although the mediation effect on improved processes is non-significant.

GAP: The study establishes a direct link between the strategic use of PMS and improved processes, as well as a positive impact on perceived managerial discretion, but it does not explore how different types of performance metrics or the frequency of PMS use might influence the effectiveness of PMS in enhancing healthcare processes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the frequency of Performance Measurement System (PMS) use, measured as the number of times PMS data is reviewed and acted upon per quarter, will moderate the relationship between strategic PMS use and the level of improved processes in healthcare organizations, such that more frequent use will be associated with greater process improvements.

abstract: Uterus-specific synthetic Prostaglandin analogues (gemeprost, sulproston etc.) have been widely employed for termination of pregnancy in the second trimester. Since paracervical anaesthesia may be useful during this procedure, we investigated in this prospective randomised study its impact on the clinical course of abortion and pain especially in the late first and second stage of labour. 20 women scheduled for elective abortion (fetal reasons) between the 16th and 23rd week of gestation were to be given 1 mg gemeprost vaginally every 6 hours. They were allocated at random: 10 women received only Pethidin intravenously and Butylscopolamine rectally, another 10 women were additionally treated by paracervical anaesthesia (2 x 10 ml 0.5% Bupivacain solution) at a cervical dilatation of 2-3 cm. A median of 3 gemeprost applications were administered in both groups. In the group without paracervical anaesthesia the median induction to abortion interval was 20 hours (range: 8-44 hours), 13 hours (range: 8-36 hours, NS) resulting for the paracervical anaesthesia group. The intervals from the last application of prostaglandin until abortion and from 3 cm cervical dilatation to abortion were slightly, but not significantly shorter in the paracervical anaesthesia group. The requirement of Butylscopolamine was higher in the latter group (p<0.05). The requirement of Pethidin and the intensity of pain (measured by pain scale according to Huskisson) especially in the late first stage of labour were not statistically different between both groups. Side effects of paracervical anaesthesia did not occur.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the increased requirement for Butylscopolamine in patients receiving paracervical anaesthesia during second-trimester abortion is due to a paracervical anaesthesia-induced increase in uterine contractions, which can be
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the addition of paracervical anaesthesia to the standard treatment of gemeprost, Pethidin, and Butylscopolamine for second-trimester abortion did not significantly reduce the induction to abortion interval or the intensity of pain, but it did increase the requirement for Butylscopolamine. The intervals from the last application of prostaglandin until abortion and from 3 cm cervical dilatation to abortion were slightly shorter in the paracervical anaesthesia group, although not statistically significant.

GAP: The study does not investigate the potential long-term effects of paracervical anaesthesia on uterine tone and future fertility, nor does it explore the mechanisms by which paracervical anaesthesia increases the requirement for Butylscopolamine. Additionally, the study's small sample size and lack of significant differences in primary outcomes leave questions about the efficacy and optimal application of paracervical anaesthesia in this context.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the increased requirement for Butylscopolamine in patients receiving paracervical anaesthesia during second-trimester abortion is due to a paracervical anaesthesia-induced increase in uterine contractions, which can be

abstract: Several studies have shown associations between hyperglycemia and risk of cardiovascular disease (CVD) and mortality, yet glucose-lowering treatment does little to mitigate this risk. We examined whether associations between hyperglycemia and CVD risk were explained by underlying insulin resistance. In 60 middle-aged individuals without diabetes we studied the associations of fasting plasma glucose, 2-hour post oral glucose tolerance test plasma glucose, insulin sensitivity as well as body fat percentage with CVD risk. Insulin sensitivity was measured as the glucose infusion rate during a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp, body fat percentage was measured by dual X-ray absorptiometry, and CVD risk was estimated using the Framingham risk score. Associations of fasting plasma glucose, 2-hour plasma glucose, insulin sensitivity and body fat percentage with the Framingham risk score were assessed in linear regression models. Both fasting and 2-hour plasma glucose levels were associated with higher Framingham risk score (fasting glucose: r(2) = 0.21; 2-hour glucose: r(2) = 0.24; P<0.001 for both), and insulin sensitivity with lower Framingham risk score (r(2) = 0.36; P<0.001). However, adjustment for insulin sensitivity and 2-hour glucose made the effect of fasting glucose non-significant (P = 0.060). Likewise, when adjusting for insulin sensitivity and fasting glucose, the association between 2-hour glucose and Framingham risk score disappeared (P = 0.143). In contrast, insulin sensitivity was still associated with Framingham risk score after adjusting for glucose levels (P<0.001). Body fat was not associated with Framingham risk score when taking insulin sensitivity into account (P = 0.550).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that a 6-month intervention aimed at improving insulin sensitivity through lifestyle modifications, such as increased physical activity and dietary changes, will result in a significant reduction in CVD risk, as measured by the Framingham risk score, in middle-aged individuals with hyperglycemia, independent of changes in
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that both fasting and 2-hour plasma glucose levels are associated with a higher cardiovascular disease (CVD) risk, as estimated by the Framingham risk score, and that insulin sensitivity is associated with a lower CVD risk. However, when adjusting for insulin sensitivity, the effects of fasting and 2-hour glucose on CVD risk become non-significant, suggesting that insulin sensitivity may be a mediating factor in the relationship between glucose levels and CVD risk. Additionally, body fat percentage is not associated with CVD risk when insulin sensitivity is taken into account.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether improving insulin sensitivity can reduce CVD risk in individuals with hyperglycemia, and what specific mechanisms or pathways are involved in the relationship between insulin sensitivity and CVD risk. The study also does not explore the potential effects of interventions aimed at improving insulin sensitivity on CVD outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that a 6-month intervention aimed at improving insulin sensitivity through lifestyle modifications, such as increased physical activity and dietary changes, will result in a significant reduction in CVD risk, as measured by the Framingham risk score, in middle-aged individuals with hyperglycemia, independent of changes in

abstract: Laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy (LSG) is currently being performed with increasing frequency worldwide. It offers an excellent weight loss and resolution of comorbidities in the short term with a very low incidence of complications. However, the ever present risk of a staple line leak is still a major concern. Since 2005, data from obese patients that undergo bariatric procedures in Germany are prospectively registered in an online database and analyzed at the Institute of Quality Assurance in Surgical Medicine. For the current analysis, all patients that had undergone primary sleeve gastrectomy for morbid obesity within a 7-year period were considered. Using the GBSR, data from 5.400 LSGs were considered for analysis. Staple line leak rate decreased during the study period from 6.5 to 1.4 %. Male gender, higher BMI, concomitant sleep apnea, conversion to laparotomy, longer operation time, use of both buttresses and oversewing, and the occurrence of intraoperative complications were associated with a significantly higher leakage rate. On multivariate analysis, operation time and year of procedure only had a significant impact on staple line leak rate.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that surgeons with specialized training in advanced suturing techniques will have a lower staple line leak rate compared to those without such training, independent of operation time and year of procedure, in patients undergoing laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the decrease in staple line leak rate over a 7-year period from 6.5% to 1.4% in patients undergoing laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy (LSG), and the identification of factors associated with a higher leakage rate, such as male gender, higher BMI, concomitant sleep apnea, and longer operation time. Multivariate analysis revealed that operation time and year of procedure significantly impacted staple line leak rate.

GAP: Despite identifying factors associated with staple line leaks and improvements over time, the abstract leaves unanswered the question of whether specific surgical techniques or training programs contribute to the reduction in staple line leak rates over the years, independent of operation time and year of procedure.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that surgeons with specialized training in advanced suturing techniques will have a lower staple line leak rate compared to those without such training, independent of operation time and year of procedure, in patients undergoing laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy.

abstract: Reliable longitudinal data of patients with functional somatic symptoms in general practice are lacking. To identify distinctive features in patients with chronic functional somatic symptoms, and to determine whether these symptoms support the hypothesis of the existence of specific somatic syndromes. Observational study, with a comparison control group. Four primary care practices affiliated with the University of Nijmegen in the Netherlands. One hundred and eighty-two patients diagnosed between 1998 and 2002 as having chronic functional somatic symptoms and 182 controls matched by age, sex, socioeconomic status, and practice were included. Data on comorbidity, referrals, diagnostic tests, and hospital admissions over a period of 10 years prior to the diagnosis were collected. Medication use and number of visits to the general practitioner (GP) were extracted from the moment computerised registration was started. In the 10 years before the diagnosis of chronic functional somatic symptoms, significantly more patients than controls presented functional somatic symptoms in at least two body systems, and used more somatic and psychotropic drugs. They visited the GP twice as much, statistically had significantly more psychiatric morbidity, and were referred more often to mental health workers and somatic specialists. The number of patients undergoing diagnostic tests was higher for patients with chronic functional somatic symptoms than for controls, but hospital admissions rates were equal.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that a multidisciplinary treatment approach, combining psychological therapy with somatic care, initiated immediately after diagnosis of chronic functional somatic symptoms, will reduce the frequency of general practitioner visits and the use of psychotropic medications over a two-year follow-up period compared to standard care.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study include that patients with chronic functional somatic symptoms, compared to controls, exhibit more frequent presentation of functional somatic symptoms in at least two body systems, increased use of somatic and psychotropic drugs, higher rates of visiting the general practitioner, greater psychiatric morbidity, more referrals to mental health workers and somatic specialists, and a higher number of diagnostic tests. However, hospital admission rates were found to be equal between the two groups.

GAP: This study provides valuable insights into the characteristics and healthcare utilization patterns of patients with chronic functional somatic symptoms but leaves unanswered the question of whether early intervention or a specific treatment approach can alter the long-term course of these symptoms and reduce the associated healthcare utilization. Specifically, it does not explore the potential impact of targeted interventions on the progression or management of chronic functional somatic symptoms.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that a multidisciplinary treatment approach, combining psychological therapy with somatic care, initiated immediately after diagnosis of chronic functional somatic symptoms, will reduce the frequency of general practitioner visits and the use of psychotropic medications over a two-year follow-up period compared to standard care.

abstract: Urine samples were examined by wet smear microscopy, incubated in 5% CO(2) for 1-2 days, and species-specific real-time polymerase chain reaction (PCR) for A. schaalii was performed. In 5 of the 29 screened urines, A. schaalii was found only by real-time PCR in quantities equivalent to ≥ 10(4) -10(5) CFU/mL. In addition, A. schaalii was found in quantities equivalent to ≥ 10(6) CFU/mL by both culture and PCR in two children with a urinary tract infection and large numbers of leucocytes in the urine.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that individuals with A. schaalii urinary tract infections with bacterial loads between 10(4) - 10(5) CFU/mL will exhibit mild or asymptomatic clinical presentations compared to those with higher bacterial loads (≥ 10(6) CFU/mL), and that this difference in clinical presentation will be correlated with distinct inflammatory cytokine profiles in the urine.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that A. schaalii was detected in urine samples using real-time PCR, with quantities ranging from ≥ 10(4) -10(5) CFU/mL in 5 out of 29 screened urines, and in higher quantities (≥ 10(6) CFU/mL) in two children with urinary tract infections who also had large numbers of leucocytes in their urine.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the presence of A. schaalii in urine is correlated with specific clinical symptoms or outcomes in individuals with lower bacterial loads (i.e., < 10(6) CFU/mL), as the two cases with high bacterial loads also had overt urinary tract infections, but the clinical status of the five individuals with lower bacterial loads is not specified.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that individuals with A. schaalii urinary tract infections with bacterial loads between 10(4) - 10(5) CFU/mL will exhibit mild or asymptomatic clinical presentations compared to those with higher bacterial loads (≥ 10(6) CFU/mL), and that this difference in clinical presentation will be correlated with distinct inflammatory cytokine profiles in the urine.

abstract: Medicare beneficiaries who have chronic conditions are responsible for a disproportionate share of Medicare fee-for-service expenditures. The objective of this study was to analyze the change in the health of Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in Part A (hospital insurance) between 2008 and 2010 by comparing the prevalence of 11 chronic conditions. We conducted descriptive analyses using the 2008 and 2010 Chronic Conditions Public Use Files, which are newly available from the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services and have administrative (claims) data on 100% of the Medicare fee-for-service population. We examined the data by age, sex, and dual eligibility (eligibility for both Medicare and Medicaid). Medicare Part A beneficiaries had more chronic conditions on average in 2010 than in 2008. The percentage increase in the average number of chronic conditions was larger for dual-eligible beneficiaries (2.8%) than for nondual-eligible beneficiaries (1.2%). The prevalence of some chronic conditions, such as congestive heart failure, ischemic heart disease, and stroke/transient ischemic attack, decreased. The deterioration of average health was due to other chronic conditions: chronic kidney disease, depression, diabetes, osteoporosis, rheumatoid arthritis/osteoarthritis. Trends in Alzheimer's disease, cancer, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease showed differences by sex or dual eligibility or both.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the increase in average number of chronic conditions among dual-eligible Medicare beneficiaries from 2008 to 2010 will be positively correlated with an increase in hospitalization rates for ambulatory care-sensitive conditions, such as diabetes and chronic kidney disease, compared to non-dual-eligible beneficiaries during the same period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include: (1) an increase in the average number of chronic conditions among Medicare Part A beneficiaries from 2008 to 2010, with a larger increase for dual-eligible beneficiaries, (2) a decrease in the prevalence of certain chronic conditions such as congestive heart failure and ischemic heart disease, and (3) an increase in the prevalence of other chronic conditions like chronic kidney disease, depression, and diabetes.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether the observed changes in chronic condition prevalence and the overall deterioration in average health among Medicare beneficiaries are associated with changes in healthcare utilization patterns, such as hospitalization rates, outpatient visits, or medication adherence, and whether these patterns differ between dual-eligible and non-dual-eligible beneficiaries.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the increase in average number of chronic conditions among dual-eligible Medicare beneficiaries from 2008 to 2010 will be positively correlated with an increase in hospitalization rates for ambulatory care-sensitive conditions, such as diabetes and chronic kidney disease, compared to non-dual-eligible beneficiaries during the same period.

abstract: To determine whether successful completion of the Perinatal Education Programme (PEP) improves obstetric practice. The three midwife obstetric units (MOUs) in a health district of Mpumalanga were included in the study. Two MOUs enrolled in the PEP and the third did not. A 'before-and-after' study design was used to assess any changes in practice, and to monitor whether any changes occurred in the district during the time of the study; data were also collected at the third MOU. Data were collected by scoring of the obstetric files after the patient had delivered. We ascertained whether the obstetric history, syphilis testing, blood group testing, haemoglobin measurement and uterine growth assessment were performed during antenatal care along with whether appropriate action was taken. For intrapartum care, estimation of fetal weight, the performance of pelvimetry, blood pressure monitoring, urine testing, evaluation of head above pelvis, fetal heart rate monitoring, monitoring of contractions and plotting of cervical dilatation, and whether the appropriate actions were taken, were assessed. Eight of the 13 midwives at the two MOUs completed the PEP and all demonstrated an improvement in knowledge. Case notes of 303 patients from the various clinics were studied. There was no change in the referral patterns of any of the clinics during the study period. The obstetric history was well documented, but in no group was there a satisfactory response to a detected problem; appropriate action was taken in between 0% and 12% of cases. Syphilis testing was performed in 56-82% of cases, with no difference between the groups. The haemoglobin level was measured in only 4-15% of patients, with no difference before or after completion of the PEP. Where a problem in uterine growth was detected, an appropriate response occurred in 0-8% of patients and no difference before or after completion of the PEP was ascertained. In all groups, estimation of fetal weight and pelvimetry were seldom performed, the urine and fetal heart rate documentation were moderately well done and the blood pressure monitoring, assessment of head above pelvis, monitoring of contractions and plotting of cervical dilatation were usually performed. No differences before or after the PEP were detected. Where problems were detected, appropriate actions taken during labour improved, but not significantly.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the implementation of a targeted clinical decision-support system, in conjunction with the Perinatal Education Programme, will significantly increase the proportion of midwives taking appropriate actions in response to detected problems during antenatal and intrapartum care
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that completion of the Perinatal Education Programme (PEP) by midwives improved their knowledge, but did not significantly change their obstetric practice, including areas such as syphilis testing, haemoglobin measurement, uterine growth assessment, and intrapartum care. Despite improved knowledge, appropriate actions were rarely taken in response to detected problems, with rates ranging from 0% to 12%. The study also found that certain aspects of care, such as estimation of fetal weight and pelvimetry, were seldom performed, while others, like blood pressure monitoring and fetal heart rate documentation, were more consistently done.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific factors contribute to the gap between midwives' improved knowledge after completing the PEP and the lack of significant change in their clinical practice. It also does not explore potential strategies for improving the translation of knowledge into practice, particularly in terms of taking appropriate actions in response to detected problems.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the implementation of a targeted clinical decision-support system, in conjunction with the Perinatal Education Programme, will significantly increase the proportion of midwives taking appropriate actions in response to detected problems during antenatal and intrapartum care

abstract: Examine whether patients with prostate cancer choose the more aggressive of two radiotherapeutic options, whether this choice is reasoned, and what the determinants of the choice are. One hundred fifty patients with primary prostate cancer (T(1-3)N(0)M(0)) were informed by means of a decision aid of two treatment options: radiotherapy with 70 Gy versus 74 Gy. The latter treatment is associated with more cure and more toxicity. The patients were asked whether they wanted to choose, and if so which treatment they preferred. They also assigned importance weights to the probability of various outcomes, such as survival, cure and adverse effects. Patients who wanted to choose their own treatment (n = 119) are described here. The majority of these patients (75%) chose the lower radiation dose. Their choice was highly consistent (P<or = .001), with the importance weights assigned to the probability of survival, cure (odds ratio [OR] = 6.7 and 6.9) and late GI and genitourinary adverse effects (OR = 0.1 and 0.2). The lower dose was chosen more often by the older patients, low-risk patients, patients without hormone treatment, and patients with a low anxiety or depression score.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with primary prostate cancer who are provided with personalized, detailed information on the potential long-term quality of life outcomes associated with each radiotherapeutic option will exhibit a significant increase in the consistency between their treatment choices and their stated preferences for outcomes such as survival, cure, and adverse effects, compared to patients who receive standard information.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The majority of patients (75%) with primary prostate cancer chose the lower radiation dose (70 Gy) over the higher dose (74 Gy) when given the option, and their choices were highly consistent with their assigned importance weights to outcomes such as survival, cure, and adverse effects. Patient characteristics such as age, risk level, hormone treatment status, and anxiety/depression scores also influenced treatment choice.

GAP: While the study identified factors that influence patients' choices between two radiotherapeutic options for prostate cancer, it does not explore how the provision of detailed information on the potential long-term quality of life outcomes associated with each treatment option affects patients' decision-making processes and ultimate treatment choices.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with primary prostate cancer who are provided with personalized, detailed information on the potential long-term quality of life outcomes associated with each radiotherapeutic option will exhibit a significant increase in the consistency between their treatment choices and their stated preferences for outcomes such as survival, cure, and adverse effects, compared to patients who receive standard information.

abstract: Mechanically ventilated patients experience profound stress. Interventions are needed to ameliorate stress that does not cause adverse effects. The purpose of this study was to explore the influence of music on stress in a sample of patients over the duration of ventilatory support.RESEARCH METHODOLOGY/ Randomised controlled trial; randomised patients (56.8+16.9 years, 61% male, APACHE III 57.2+18.3) receiving ventilatory support to: (1) patient-directed music (PDM) where patients self-initiated music listening whenever desired from a preferred collection, (2) headphones only to block ICU noise, or (3) usual ICU care. Twenty-four hour urinary cortisol samples were collected from a sub-set of subjects with intact renal function and not receiving medications known to influence cortisol levels (n=65). 12 ICUs in the Midwestern United States. Urinary free cortisol (UFC), an integrative biomarker of stress. Controlling for illness severity, gender, and baseline UFC (29-45 mg/day), mixed models analysis revealed no significant differences among groups in UFC over the course of ventilatory support.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that mechanically ventilated patients who listen to music for a cumulative duration of at least 4 hours per day, with sessions timed to coincide with peak stress periods (e.g., during ventilator weaning or medical procedures), will exhibit significantly lower urinary free cortisol levels compared to those who listen to music for less than 2 hours per day or at random times.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patient-directed music (PDM), headphones only to block ICU noise, and usual ICU care had no significant differences in urinary free cortisol (UFC) levels, an integrative biomarker of stress, among mechanically ventilated patients over the course of ventilatory support, after controlling for illness severity, gender, and baseline UFC.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the timing and duration of music exposure, rather than just its presence or absence, have an impact on stress reduction in mechanically ventilated patients, as the current study only examined the overall effect of patient-directed music without considering the specifics of music listening patterns.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that mechanically ventilated patients who listen to music for a cumulative duration of at least 4 hours per day, with sessions timed to coincide with peak stress periods (e.g., during ventilator weaning or medical procedures), will exhibit significantly lower urinary free cortisol levels compared to those who listen to music for less than 2 hours per day or at random times.

abstract: Spasticity and loss of function in an affected arm are common after stroke. Although botulinum toxin is used to reduce spasticity, its functional benefits are less easily demonstrated. This paper reports an exploratory meta-analysis to investigate the relationship between reduced arm spasticity and improved arm function. Individual data from stroke patients in two randomised controlled trials of intra-muscular botulinum toxin were pooled. The Modified Ashworth Scale (elbow, wrist, fingers) was used to calculate a "Composite Spasticity Index". Data from the arm section of the Barthel Activities of Daily Living Index (dressing, grooming, and feeding) and three subjective measures (putting arm through sleeve, cleaning palm, cutting fingernails) were summed to give a "Composite Functional Index". Change scores and the time of maximum change were also calculated. Maximum changes in both composite measures occurred concurrently in 47 patients. In 26 patients the improvement in spasticity preceded the improvement in function with 18 showing the reverse. There was a definite relationship between the maximum change in spasticity and the maximum change in arm function, independent of treatment (rho = -0.2822, p = 0.0008, n = 137). There was a clear relationship between the changes in spasticity and in arm function in patients treated with botulinum toxin (Dysport) at 500 or 1000 units (rho = -0.5679, p = 0.0090, n = 22; rho = -0.4430, p = 0.0018, n = 47), but not in those treated with placebo or 1500 units.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the optimal timing of botulinum toxin administration, in terms of dose and treatment interval, will significantly influence the strength and direction of the correlation between spasticity reduction and functional improvement in stroke patients, such that a dose of 500-1000 units administered at 4-6 week intervals will result in a stronger correlation between reduced spasticity and improved arm
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include the discovery of a significant relationship between reduced arm spasticity and improved arm function in stroke patients treated with botulinum toxin (Dysport) at specific doses (500 or 1000 units), as measured by the Composite Spasticity Index and the Composite Functional Index. The correlation between spasticity reduction and functional improvement was dose-dependent, with no significant relationship found in patients treated with placebo or 1500 units of botulinum toxin.

GAP: A key knowledge gap left unanswered by this work is the underlying mechanism by which botulinum toxin at specific doses facilitates the transition from reduced spasticity to improved functional outcomes in stroke patients. Specifically, the temporal relationship between spasticity reduction and functional improvement, and how different doses of botulinum toxin influence this relationship, requires further investigation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the optimal timing of botulinum toxin administration, in terms of dose and treatment interval, will significantly influence the strength and direction of the correlation between spasticity reduction and functional improvement in stroke patients, such that a dose of 500-1000 units administered at 4-6 week intervals will result in a stronger correlation between reduced spasticity and improved arm

abstract: To study the efficiency and safety of holmium:YAG laser lithotripsy for ureteral stones. A series of 188 patients with 208 ureteral stones were treated with semirigid ureteroscopy and holmium:YAG laser lithotripsy from January 2003 to December 2005. Of the stones, 116 were lower ureteral, 37 middle ureteral, and 55 upper ureteral. The success rate was 92.7% at the time of ureteroscopy and 96.7% at 3 months. The failures were secondary to retropulsion of the stones (3.3%). There were no perforations and one stricture. Stenting was done in 90% of patients.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that adjusting the holmium:YAG laser energy settings based on the stone size and composition will significantly reduce the rate of stone retropulsion during ureteroscopic lithotripsy, thereby further improving the overall success rate of the procedure.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include a high success rate (92.7% at the time of ureteroscopy and 96.7% at 3 months) of holmium:YAG laser lithotripsy for treating ureteral stones, with a low rate of complications (3.3% retropulsion, no perforations, and one stricture), and the frequent use of stenting (in 90% of patients).

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of whether the high success rate and low complication rate of holmium:YAG laser lithotripsy can be maintained or improved by optimizing the parameters of the laser treatment, such as energy settings or pulse duration, particularly in relation to the size, composition, or location of the ureteral stones.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that adjusting the holmium:YAG laser energy settings based on the stone size and composition will significantly reduce the rate of stone retropulsion during ureteroscopic lithotripsy, thereby further improving the overall success rate of the procedure.

abstract: The specific aim of this investigation was to evaluate the proficiency of health care providers and patients in the proper use of metered-dose inhalers. Health care providers, which include house staff, nurses, and respiratory care practitioners who provide care to patients with asthma in the primary general medicine clinic or the pulmonary medicine clinic of a university-county hospital in which patients were referred, were surveyed and assigned a performance score regarding the knowledge base of the appropriate use of metered-dose inhalers. Patients who attended the primary care general medicine and pulmonary subspecialty clinic were also assessed as to their proficiency in the use of metered-dose inhalers. A significant percentage of patients had a poor understanding of the technique used with the metered-dose inhaler. House staff and nursing staff were also less proficient in the proper use of the metered-dose inhaler. The respiratory care practitioners were the most knowledgeable of the health care providers.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that a standardized, multidisciplinary educational program focused on metered-dose inhaler technique will significantly improve the proficiency of both patients and healthcare providers, particularly house staff and nursing staff, leading to enhanced asthma management outcomes as measured by improved lung function and reduced hospital readmissions over a six-month follow-up period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study indicate that a significant percentage of patients have a poor understanding of the proper technique for using metered-dose inhalers. Additionally, among healthcare providers, house staff and nursing staff demonstrate limited proficiency, whereas respiratory care practitioners show the highest level of knowledge regarding the correct use of metered-dose inhalers.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of whether targeted educational interventions can improve the proficiency of both patients and healthcare providers (especially house staff and nursing staff) in the proper use of metered-dose inhalers, and if such improvements can lead to better patient outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that a standardized, multidisciplinary educational program focused on metered-dose inhaler technique will significantly improve the proficiency of both patients and healthcare providers, particularly house staff and nursing staff, leading to enhanced asthma management outcomes as measured by improved lung function and reduced hospital readmissions over a six-month follow-up period.

abstract: To investigate the effect of bracket-ligature combination on the amount of orthodontic space closure over three months. Randomized clinical trial with three parallel groups. A hospital orthodontic department (Chesterfield Royal Hospital, UK). Forty-five patients requiring upper first premolar extractions. Informed consent was obtained and participants were randomly allocated into one of three groups: (1) conventional pre-adjusted edgewise brackets and elastomeric ligatures; (2) conventional pre-adjusted edgewise brackets and Super Slick(®) low friction elastomeric ligatures; (3) Damon 3MX(®) passive self-ligating brackets. Space closure was undertaken on 0·019×0·025-inch stainless steel archwires with nickel-titanium coil springs. Participants were recalled at four weekly intervals. Upper alginate impressions were taken at each visit (maximum three). The primary outcome measure was the mean amount of space closure in a 3-month period. A one-way ANOVA was undertaken [dependent variable: mean space closure (mm); independent variable: group allocation]. The amount of space closure was very similar between the three groups (1 mm per 28 days); however, there was a wide variation in the rate of space closure between individuals. The differences in the amount of space closure over three months between the three groups was very small and non-significant (P = 0·718).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the variability in individual rates of orthodontic space closure is correlated with differences in the expression levels of genes involved in bone remodeling, such as RANKL and OPG, which play a crucial role in regulating osteoclast activity and bone resorption during orthodontic tooth
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the amount of orthodontic space closure over three months was very similar between the three groups (conventional pre-adjusted edgewise brackets with elastomeric ligatures, conventional pre-adjusted edgewise brackets with Super Slick low friction elastomeric ligatures, and Damon 3MX passive self-ligating brackets), with a mean space closure rate of 1 mm per 28 days, and the differences between the groups were non-significant. Additionally, there was a wide variation in the rate of space closure between individuals.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what factors contribute to the wide variation in the rate of space closure between individuals, as the bracket-ligature combination did not have a significant impact on the amount of space closure. This gap in knowledge is important because understanding the factors that influence individual variability in orthodontic treatment outcomes could help personalize treatment plans and improve efficiency.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the variability in individual rates of orthodontic space closure is correlated with differences in the expression levels of genes involved in bone remodeling, such as RANKL and OPG, which play a crucial role in regulating osteoclast activity and bone resorption during orthodontic tooth

abstract: There has never been a nationally representative survey of medical students' personal health-related practices, although they are inherently of interest and may affect patient-counseling practices. This study evaluated the health practices and the vaccination status of first year residents working at the academic hospital Hôtel-Dieu de France. The medical files of all medicine and surgery residents in their first year of specialization between the years 2005 and 2008 were reviewed. These residents were required to go through a preventive medical visit at the University Center of Family and Community Health. One hundred and nine residents (109) were included in the study; 68 (6239%) were male and 41 (37.61%) were female with a mean age of 26 years. Only 6 residents (5.50%) practiced physical activity according to international guidelines (more than three times a week for more than 30 minutes each time). Most residents (n = 76 ; 69.73%) used to skip one or two meals especially breakfast and as a consequence 30 male (44.11%) and 4 female (9.75%) students were overweight, with a statistical difference between the two sexes (Fisher test, p-value = 0.001). Twenty-eight residents (25.69%) were smokers with a male predominance. Fourteen residents of both genders (12.84%) drank alcohol regularly (>3 times a week) and 71 (65.14%) had a drink occasionally (once a month or less). Only 25 residents (23%) of the cohort had a complete and up-to-date immunization status. The immunization gap was basically against measles, mumps, rubella (MMR) and diphtheria, tetanus, poliomyelitis (dT Polio). Ninety-nine residents (90.83%) had full immunization against hepatitis B with an adequate response in 78 residents (71.56%).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that first-year medical residents who participate in a targeted health and wellness program, including physical activity promotion, nutrition counseling, and vaccination education, will demonstrate improved patient counseling skills on healthy behaviors and show a significant increase in their own adherence to recommended health practices compared to those who do not participate in such a
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study of first-year medical residents at Hôtel-Dieu de France found that the majority do not engage in regular physical activity, with only 5.50% meeting international guidelines. Many residents have poor dietary habits, with 69.73% skipping meals, leading to a significant number being overweight, particularly among males. Additionally, 25.69% of residents are smokers, and only 23% have a complete and up-to-date immunization status, with significant gaps in MMR and dT Polio vaccinations. However, 90.83% have full immunization against hepatitis B.

GAP: The study provides insight into the health practices and vaccination status of first-year medical residents but does not explore how these factors might impact their ability to counsel patients on healthy behaviors or their overall performance as healthcare providers. It also does not investigate potential interventions to improve the health practices and vaccination rates among medical residents.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that first-year medical residents who participate in a targeted health and wellness program, including physical activity promotion, nutrition counseling, and vaccination education, will demonstrate improved patient counseling skills on healthy behaviors and show a significant increase in their own adherence to recommended health practices compared to those who do not participate in such a

abstract: Vaginal intraepithelial neoplasia is a little known disease which could be related to risk factors different from simple HPV infections. To ascertain wheter vaginal lesions have a natural history similar to cervical lesions.MATERIALS & A retrospective study to identify patients with vaginal lesions and synchronous cervical lesions through biopsy. The rate of mild cervical lesions (koilocytosis, warts, CIN I with and without koilocytosis) was compared with the rate of severe cervical lesions (CIN II and III, cervical carcinoma) in patients with mild vaginal lesions (warts and koilocytosis, and low-grade VAIN) and in patients with severe vaginal lesions (high-grade VAIN). Using koilocytosis as a marker, the rate of "active" cervical lesions was compared with the rate of "non active" cervical lesions in patients with "active" versus "non active" vaginal lesions. Finally, the rates of mild and severe cervical lesions were compared among each group of VAIN (low-grade, high-grade, with or without koilocytosis). In patients with mild vaginal lesions, mild cervical lesions were significantly more frequent than severe cervical lesions. In patients with "active" vaginal lesions the rate of "active" cervical lesions was significantly higher than "non active" cervical lesions. The differences in rates of mild cervical lesions and severe cervical lesions among patients with high-grade VAIN and low-grade VAIN (with and without koilocytosis) were not significant.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the expression of specific microRNAs, known to be involved in the regulation of cellular proliferation and differentiation, will be differentially altered in vaginal lesions compared to cervical lesions, and that these alterations will correlate with the severity of VAIN and the presence of high-risk HPV types.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that in patients with mild vaginal lesions, mild cervical lesions were more frequent than severe cervical lesions. Additionally, in patients with "active" vaginal lesions, the rate of "active" cervical lesions was higher than "non active" cervical lesions. However, there were no significant differences in the rates of mild and severe cervical lesions among patients with high-grade VAIN and low-grade VAIN, regardless of koilocytosis.

GAP: The study does not investigate the molecular mechanisms or specific risk factors that contribute to the progression of vaginal intraepithelial neoplasia (VAIN) or the relationship between VAIN and cervical lesions, particularly in the context of HPV infection and other potential cofactors.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the expression of specific microRNAs, known to be involved in the regulation of cellular proliferation and differentiation, will be differentially altered in vaginal lesions compared to cervical lesions, and that these alterations will correlate with the severity of VAIN and the presence of high-risk HPV types.

abstract: To assess the acceptability to patients of the use of patients' first names by doctors and doctors' first names by patients in general practice. An administered questionnaire survey. 5 General practices in Lothian. 475 Patients consulting 30 general practitioners. Response by patients to questionnaire on attitude to use of first names. Most of the patients either liked (223) or did not mind (175) being called by their first names. Only 77 disliked it, most of whom were aged over 65. Most patients (324) did not, however, want to call the doctor by his or her first name.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients who are addressed by their first names by their doctors and who are also allowed to address their doctors by their first names will exhibit increased satisfaction with their care and adherence to medical recommendations compared to patients who maintain a more formal relationship with their healthcare providers.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The majority of patients (398 out of 475) are either indifferent or positive about being called by their first names by doctors, with a notable exception being patients over 65 who tend to dislike it. However, most patients (324 out of 475) prefer not to call their doctors by their first names, indicating a asymmetry in the preference for formality in doctor-patient communication.

GAP: The study does not explore the underlying reasons for the asymmetry in preferences regarding the use of first names between doctors and patients, nor does it examine how this preference affects the doctor-patient relationship or health outcomes.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients who are addressed by their first names by their doctors and who are also allowed to address their doctors by their first names will exhibit increased satisfaction with their care and adherence to medical recommendations compared to patients who maintain a more formal relationship with their healthcare providers.

abstract: The brain-dead donor supply has become one of the criteria limiting the performance of heart transplantation. Conventional screening criteria are too limiting and exclude suitable heart donors. Echocardiography is now widely available and is a reliable tool to assess left ventricular dysfunction in brain-dead donors. Yet few data are available on the degree of left ventricular dysfunction where a transplantation is possible. Fifty-five potential brain-dead heart donors (age 38 +/- 11 years) were prospectively evaluated by transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) before harvesting. Fractional area change (FAC) was used to assess left ventricular function in potential brain-dead donors. Transplanted hearts were evaluated on the fifth postoperative day. The transplantation was considered a success if the recipient was alive, not retransplanted, without an assistance device or an epinephrine infusion of more than 1 mg/h and showed an ejection fraction above 40%. Of the 55 potential heart donors, 20 exhibited an FAC of less than 50%. Forty hearts were harvested, 36 of which were successfully transplanted. Nine patients had an FAC below 50% (group H2) and 27 had an FAC over 50% (group H1). Four patients died: 2 from hemorrhage (FAC>50% in donors); 1 from right and one from left ventricular dysfunction (FAC<50% in donors). The FAC increased significantly from 51 +/- 15% to 57 +/- 11% in 18 hearts that underwent TEE in donors and afterwards in recipients. Overall actuarial survival was 86.2% versus 64.6% at 1 and 2 years in group H1 and group H2, respectively (p = NS).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the degree of improvement in left ventricular function (as measured by change in FAC) after heart transplantation from brain-dead donors will be positively correlated with the level of ischemia-induced stress protein expression in the donor heart, as measured by immunohistochemical analysis of tissue samples taken at the time of harvesting.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) can be used to assess left ventricular function in brain-dead donors, and that hearts with fractional area change (FAC) below 50% can still be successfully transplanted with good outcomes. The study also showed that the FAC increases significantly after transplantation, and that overall actuarial survival is higher in recipients who received hearts with FAC above 50% (group H1) compared to those with FAC below 50% (group H2), although the difference was not statistically significant.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific factors contribute to the improvement in left ventricular function (as measured by FAC) after transplantation, and whether there are any donor or recipient characteristics that can predict which hearts with initially low FAC will have the most significant improvement in function after transplantation.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the degree of improvement in left ventricular function (as measured by change in FAC) after heart transplantation from brain-dead donors will be positively correlated with the level of ischemia-induced stress protein expression in the donor heart, as measured by immunohistochemical analysis of tissue samples taken at the time of harvesting.

abstract: Twenty-eight female Sprague Dawley rats were allocated randomly to 4 groups. The sham group (group 1) was only subjected to catheter insertion, not to pneumoperitoneum. Group 2 received a 1 mg/kg dose of 0.9% sodium chloride by the intraperitoneal route for 10 min before pneumoperitoneum. Groups 3 and 4 received 6 and 12 mg/kg edaravone, respectively, by the intraperitoneal route for 10 min before pneumoperitoneum. After 60 min of pneumoperitoneum, the gas was deflated. Immediately after the reperfusion period, both ovaries were excised for histological scoring, caspase-3 immunohistochemistry and biochemical evaluation including glutathione (GSH) and malondialdehyde (MDA) levels. Also, total antioxidant capacity (TAC) was measured in plasma samples to evaluate the antioxidant effect of edaravone. Ovarian sections in the saline group revealed higher scores for follicular degeneration and edema (p<0.0001) when compared with the sham group. Administration of different doses of edaravone in rats significantly prevented degenerative changes in the ovary (p<0.0001). Caspase-3 expression was only detected in the ovarian surface epithelium in all groups, and there was a significant difference between the treatment groups and the saline group (p<0.0001). Treatment of rats with edaravone reduced caspase-3 expression in a dose-dependent manner. Moreover, biochemical measurements of oxidative stress markers (MDA, GSH and TAC) revealed that prophylactic edaravone treatment attenuated oxidative stress induced by I/R injury.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that edaravone-treated rats that have undergone ischemia/reperfusion injury will exhibit improved fertility outcomes, as measured by increased litter size and decreased time to pregnancy, compared to untreated rats when mated after recovery from the injury.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the protective effects of edaravone against ovarian damage induced by ischemia/reperfusion (I/R) injury in a rat model, as evidenced by reduced follicular degeneration, edema, and caspase-3 expression, as well as attenuation of oxidative stress markers such as MDA, GSH, and TAC. Edaravone's effects were dose-dependent, with higher doses resulting in greater protection.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the efficacy of edaravone in preventing ovarian damage from I/R injury, it does not investigate the long-term reproductive outcomes or the potential impact of edaravone on fertility in these rats. Specifically, it remains unknown whether edaravone treatment can improve reproductive function and fertility in rats that have undergone I/R injury.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that edaravone-treated rats that have undergone ischemia/reperfusion injury will exhibit improved fertility outcomes, as measured by increased litter size and decreased time to pregnancy, compared to untreated rats when mated after recovery from the injury.

abstract: The National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale (NIHSS) is a valid, reproducible scale that measures neurological deficit. Of 42 possible points, 7 points are directly related to measurement of language compared with only 2 points related to neglect. We examined the placebo arm of the NINDS t-PA stroke trial to test the hypothesis that the total volume of cerebral infarction in patients with right hemisphere strokes would be greater than the volume of cerebral infarction in patients with left hemisphere strokes who have similar NIHSS scores. The volume of stroke was determined by computerized image analysis of CT films and CT images stored on computer tape and optical disks. Cube-root transformation of lesion volume was performed for each CT. Transformed lesion volume was analyzed in a logistic regression model to predict volume of stroke by NIHSS score for each hemisphere. Spearman rank correlation was used to determine the relation between the NIHSS score and lesion volume. The volume for right hemisphere stroke was statistically greater than the volume for left hemisphere strokes, adjusting for the baseline NIHSS (P<0. 001). For each 5-point category of the NIHSS score<20, the median volume of right hemisphere strokes was approximately double the median volume of left hemisphere strokes. For example, for patients with a left hemisphere stroke and a 24-hour NIHSS score of 16 to 20, the median volume of cerebral infarction was 48 mL (interquartile range 14 to 111 mL) as compared with 133 mL (interquartile range 81 to 208 mL) for patients with a right hemisphere stroke (P<0.001). The median volume of a right hemisphere stroke was roughly equal to the median volume of a left hemisphere stroke in the next highest 5-point category of the NIHSS. The Spearman rank correlation between the 24-hour NIHSS score and 3-month lesion volume was 0.72 for patients with left hemisphere stroke and 0.71 for patients with right hemisphere stroke.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the differential weighting of language and neglect components in the NIHSS will predict a significant interaction effect between hemisphere (left vs. right) and NIHSS component (language vs. neglect) on the volume of cerebral infarction, such that language deficits will be more strongly associated with infarction volume in left hemisphere strokes, while
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that the volume of cerebral infarction in patients with right hemisphere strokes is significantly greater than in patients with left hemisphere strokes who have similar NIHSS scores, with the median volume of right hemisphere strokes being approximately double that of left hemisphere strokes for NIHSS scores less than 20. The study also found a strong correlation between NIHSS scores and lesion volume for both left and right hemisphere strokes.

GAP: One knowledge gap left unanswered by this study is the underlying neurological mechanisms that contribute to the observed difference in infarction volume between right and left hemisphere strokes, particularly in relation to the distinct weighting of language and neglect components in the NIHSS. The study primarily focuses on the correlation between NIHSS scores and lesion volume without exploring how specific components of the NIHSS, such as language versus neglect, influence the volume of cerebral infarction in each hemisphere.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the differential weighting of language and neglect components in the NIHSS will predict a significant interaction effect between hemisphere (left vs. right) and NIHSS component (language vs. neglect) on the volume of cerebral infarction, such that language deficits will be more strongly associated with infarction volume in left hemisphere strokes, while

abstract: This clinical study investigated whether the vascular-guided multilayer preauricular approach (VMPA) to the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) could improve access and decrease complications. This retrospective evaluation consisted of a consecutive series of patients who underwent TMJ surgeries through the VMPA from January through December 2013. Patients with a history of TMJ surgery were excluded. Clinical data, including operating times, subjective complaints of incision scars, functional conditions of the auriculotemporal nerve and facial nerve, and other complications, were recorded and analyzed. All patients in this study were followed for at least 6 months. All patients (606 joints) had successful TMJ surgeries through the VMPA. All incisions healed favorably with an uneventful recovery.
