The participants were asked to assess their own sense of security in seven different communication settings on a visual analog scale, and to specify perceived changes in their communication behavior 3 months after the course. The first three courses were attended by 31 participants. Course evaluation revealed high satisfaction scores with methods as well as with clarity and relevance of the contents. Self-assessment of participants showed a growing sense of security in different communication settings. Important increases could be demonstrated for communicating a diagnosis of cancer with good or less good prognosis, recurrence of cancer or a far progressive cancer disease without curative approach. 3 months after the course, participants described multiple changes indicating increased sensibility and professionalism in communication behavior.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that physicians who participate in the described 2-day course model will exhibit sustained improvement in patient-reported satisfaction with communication and emotional support over a 6-month period, compared to a control group of physicians who do not receive the training.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study include high satisfaction scores among participants for a 2-day course model aimed at improving communicative skills for physicians dealing with terminally ill patients, increased self-assessed sense of security in various communication settings, and reported changes towards more sensitive and professional communication behavior 3 months after the course. Specifically, significant improvements were noted in communicating diagnoses of cancer with varying prognoses and in discussing recurrence or progression of cancer without a curative approach.

GAP: While the study demonstrates the effectiveness of the course in enhancing communicative skills and participant confidence, it leaves unanswered the question of whether these improvements translate into better patient outcomes, such as increased patient satisfaction, improved patient-physician relationships, or enhanced quality of life for terminally ill patients. Additionally, the long-term sustainability of these communication skills and behavioral changes beyond the 3-month follow-up period is not addressed.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that physicians who participate in the described 2-day course model will exhibit sustained improvement in patient-reported satisfaction with communication and emotional support over a 6-month period, compared to a control group of physicians who do not receive the training.

abstract: Treatment delays in breast cancer are generally thought to affect prognosis but the impact on survival remains unclear. Indicators for breast cancer care include time to primary treatment. The purpose of this study was to evaluate whether time to primary treatment (TPT) in breast cancer impacts survival. A total of 648 breast cancer patients treated in the University Malaya Medical Center (UMMC), Malaysia between 2004 and 2005 were included in the study. TPT was calculated from the date of pathological diagnosis to the date of primary treatment. Mortality data was obtained from the National Registry of Births and Deaths. Last date of follow-up was November 2010. Median TPT was 18 days. Majority 508 (69.1%) of the patients received treatment within 30 days after diagnosis. The majority was surgically treated. Ethnicity (p=0.002) and stage at presentation (p=0.007) were significantly associated with delayed TPT. Malay ethnicity had delayed TPT compared to the Chinese; Hazard Ratio (HR) 1.9 (Confidence Interval (CI) 1.237, 2.987). Delayed TPT did not affect overall survival on univariate and multivariate analyses.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that delayed time to primary treatment will be associated with reduced overall survival in triple-negative breast cancer patients, but not in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer patients, due to differences in tumor aggressiveness and treatment response.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the median time to primary treatment (TPT) for breast cancer patients was 18 days, with the majority of patients receiving treatment within 30 days. Ethnicity and stage at presentation were significantly associated with delayed TPT, with Malay ethnicity having a higher risk of delayed TPT. However, delayed TPT did not affect overall survival in univariate and multivariate analyses.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether the lack of association between delayed TPT and overall survival is consistent across different breast cancer subtypes, such as hormone receptor-positive or triple-negative breast cancer, and whether specific tumor biology characteristics might influence the impact of TPT on survival.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that delayed time to primary treatment will be associated with reduced overall survival in triple-negative breast cancer patients, but not in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer patients, due to differences in tumor aggressiveness and treatment response.

abstract: To provide insight into the factors by which obesity in itself may directly lead to early arterial damage, we aimed to determine early sonographic markers of obesity-related vascular dysfunction in young obese males. Thirty-five young obese males and 23 age-matched healthy male volunteers were recruited into the study. Common carotid artery pulsatility index and resistance index were calculated from blood flow velocities curves obtained by pulsed Doppler ultrasonography. The mean pulsatility index, resistance index, body mass index, waist circumference, systolic and diastolic blood pressure, homeostasis model assessment for insulin resistance, plasma fasting glucose, insulin, C-peptide, triglycerides, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and high-sensitivity C-reactive protein were statistically higher in obese subjects than in healthy controls.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that a targeted exercise intervention aimed at reducing visceral fat in young obese males will decrease the pulsatility index and resistance index in the common carotid artery to levels comparable with those of age-matched healthy controls within a 6-month period.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that young obese males have higher mean pulsatility index, resistance index, body mass index, waist circumference, systolic and diastolic blood pressure, homeostasis model assessment for insulin resistance, plasma fasting glucose, insulin, C-peptide, triglycerides, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and high-sensitivity C-reactive protein compared to age-matched healthy male volunteers, indicating early signs of vascular dysfunction in obese individuals.

GAP: The study establishes a correlation between obesity and early markers of vascular dysfunction but does not investigate the specific mechanisms by which obesity leads to increased pulsatility and resistance indices in the common carotid artery, nor does it explore potential interventions that could mitigate these effects.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that a targeted exercise intervention aimed at reducing visceral fat in young obese males will decrease the pulsatility index and resistance index in the common carotid artery to levels comparable with those of age-matched healthy controls within a 6-month period.

abstract: Preventive home visits are offered to community dwelling older people in Denmark aimed at maintaining their functional ability for as long as possible, but only two thirds of older people accept the offer from the municipalities. The purpose of this study is to investigate 1) whether socioeconomic status was associated with acceptance of preventive home visits among older people and 2) whether municipality invitational procedures for the preventive home visits modified the association. The study population included 1,023 community dwelling 80-year-old individuals from the Danish intervention study on preventive home visits. Information on preventive home visit acceptance rates was obtained from questionnaires. Socioeconomic status was measured by financial assets obtained from national registry data, and invitational procedures were identified through the municipalities. Logistic regression analyses were used, adjusted by gender. Older persons with high financial assets accepted preventive home visits more frequently than persons with low assets (adjusted OR = 1.5 (CI95%: 1.1-2.0)). However, the association was attenuated when adjusted by the invitational procedures. The odds ratio for accepting preventive home visits was larger among persons with low financial assets invited by a letter with a proposed date than among persons with high financial assets invited by other procedures, though these estimates had wide confidence intervals.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the inclusion of a personalized message emphasizing the potential health benefits of preventive home visits in the invitation letter will increase the acceptance rate of these visits among older individuals with low financial assets, compared to a standard invitation letter with a proposed date.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that older individuals with high financial assets are more likely to accept preventive home visits than those with low financial assets, with an adjusted odds ratio of 1.5. However, the association between socioeconomic status and acceptance of preventive home visits is modified by the municipality's invitational procedures, with a proposed date in the invitation letter potentially increasing acceptance rates among individuals with low financial assets.

GAP: The study does not investigate the specific mechanisms by which invitational procedures influence the acceptance of preventive home visits, particularly among individuals with low financial assets. It also does not examine whether the content or tone of the invitation letter, beyond the inclusion of a proposed date, affects acceptance rates.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the inclusion of a personalized message emphasizing the potential health benefits of preventive home visits in the invitation letter will increase the acceptance rate of these visits among older individuals with low financial assets, compared to a standard invitation letter with a proposed date.

abstract: Some studies suggest that open access articles are more often cited than non-open access articles. However, the relationship between open access and citations count in a discipline such as intensive care medicine has not been studied to date. The present article analyzes the effect of open access publishing of scientific articles in intensive care medicine journals in terms of citations count. We evaluated a total of 161 articles (76% being non-open access articles) published in Intensive Care Medicine in the year 2008. Citation data were compared between the two groups up until April 30, 2011. Potentially confounding variables for citation counts were adjusted for in a linear multiple regression model. The median number (interquartile range) of citations of non-open access articles was 8 (4-12) versus 9 (6-18) in the case of open access articles (p=0.084). In the highest citation range (>8), the citation count was 13 (10-16) and 18 (13-21) (p=0.008), respectively. The mean follow-up was 37.5 ± 3 months in both groups. In the 30-35 months after publication, the average number (mean ± standard deviation) of citations per article per month of non-open access articles was 0.28 ± 0.6 versus 0.38 ± 0.7 in the case of open access articles (p=0.043). Independent factors for citation advantage were the Hirsch index of the first signing author (β=0.207; p=0.015) and open access status (β=3.618; p=0.006).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that articles published with immediate open access in intensive care medicine journals will receive a significantly higher number of citations within the first two years of publication compared to articles published with delayed open access or traditional non-open access models, due to increased visibility and accessibility of the research.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study indicate that open access articles in intensive care medicine journals tend to have a higher citation count compared to non-open access articles, with a significant difference in the highest citation range and in the average number of citations per article per month 30-35 months after publication. The study also identifies the Hirsch index of the first signing author and open access status as independent factors for citation advantage.

GAP: While the study establishes a relationship between open access status and citation counts in intensive care medicine, it does not investigate how the timing of open access publication (immediate vs. delayed) affects citation counts, nor does it explore the potential impact of open access on the quality or relevance of the cited research.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that articles published with immediate open access in intensive care medicine journals will receive a significantly higher number of citations within the first two years of publication compared to articles published with delayed open access or traditional non-open access models, due to increased visibility and accessibility of the research.

abstract: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) is a common condition which presents in infants at 2-12 weeks of postnatal life, and whose cause remains obscure. Multiple associated abnormalities have been recognized within the external hypertrophied pyloric muscle layer, but the internal component of the pyloric mucosa has received scant attention in the literature to date. Our purpose in this study was to show that pyloric mucosal redundancy is a constant finding in infants with IHPS, to discuss its possible cause, and to explore the hypothesis of a relationship between pyloric mucosal redundancy and the development of IHPS. We identified 102 consecutive infants with surgically confirmed IHPS and determined the thickness of the pyloric mucosa compared with the thickness of the surrounding hypertrophied muscle. Fifty-one infants who did not have pyloric stenosis served as controls. Mean mucosal thickness in patients with IHPS approximated mean muscle thickness, with a ratio of 0.89. In infants with IHPS, the pyloric mucosa constitutes approximately one third of the cross-sectional diameter of the pyloric mass and fills and obstructs the pyloric canal.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that inhibition of the signaling pathways responsible for mucosal growth and proliferation in the pylorus during the early postnatal period will prevent the development of infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis in susceptible infants.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that infants with infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) have pyloric mucosal redundancy, where the mean mucosal thickness approximates the mean muscle thickness, and the pyloric mucosa constitutes about one third of the cross-sectional diameter of the pyloric mass, obstructing the pyloric canal. This condition is consistently found in infants with IHPS, suggesting a potential relationship between pyloric mucosal redundancy and the development of IHPS.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of how pyloric mucosal redundancy leads to the development of IHPS, specifically the molecular or cellular mechanisms underlying this relationship. It also does not explore whether reducing or preventing pyloric mucosal redundancy could prevent or alleviate IHPS.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that inhibition of the signaling pathways responsible for mucosal growth and proliferation in the pylorus during the early postnatal period will prevent the development of infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis in susceptible infants.

abstract: To analyse associations between indicators for adoption of new drugs and to test the hypothesis that physicians' early adoption of new drugs is a personal trait independent of drug groups. In a population-based cohort study using register data, we analysed the prescribing of new drugs by Danish general practitioners. Angiotensin-II antagonists, triptans, selective cyclo-oxygenase-2 antagonists and esomeprazol were used in the assessment. As indicators of new drug uptake, we used adoption time, cumulative incidence, preference proportion, incidence rate and prescription cost and volume. For each measure, we ranked the general practices. Ranks were pair-wise plotted, and Pearson's correlation coefficient ( r) was calculated. Next, we analysed the correlation between ranks across different drug classes. For all indicators, the general practitioners' adoption of one group of drugs was poorly associated with adoption of others ( r</=0.49), indicating that early adoption of one type of drugs is not associated with early adoption of another. For all drug groups, adoption time adjusted for practice size was only weakly associated with other indicators ( r: -0.56 to -0.27). Indicators, based on cost and volume of drugs, were highly correlated ( r: 0.96-0.99), and the others correlated reasonably well ( r: 0.51-0.91).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that general practitioners with a higher degree of participation in continuing medical education programs related to pharmacology and therapeutics will exhibit a significantly shorter adoption time for new drugs across multiple drug classes, compared to those with lower participation rates.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that the adoption of new drugs by general practitioners is poorly associated across different drug classes, with correlation coefficients ranging from 0.49 or less. Additionally, the study found that indicators based on cost and volume of drugs are highly correlated, while adoption time adjusted for practice size is only weakly associated with other indicators.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what factors influence the variation in adoption rates of new drugs among general practitioners, and whether there are specific physician or practice characteristics that can predict early adoption of new drugs.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that general practitioners with a higher degree of participation in continuing medical education programs related to pharmacology and therapeutics will exhibit a significantly shorter adoption time for new drugs across multiple drug classes, compared to those with lower participation rates.

abstract: Epidemiological studies have suggested inverse relationships between blood pressure and prevalence of conditions such as migraine and headache. It is not yet clear whether similar relationships can be established for back pain in particular in prospective studies. Associations between blood pressure and chronic low back pain were explored in the cross-sectional HUNT 2 survey of a Norwegian county in 1995-1997, including 39,872 individuals who never used antihypertensive medication. A prospective study, comprising 17,209 initially back pain-free individuals and 5740 individuals reporting low back pain, was established by re-examinations in the HUNT 3 survey in 2006-2008. Associations were assessed by logistic regression with respect to systolic, diastolic and pulse pressure, with adjustment for education, work status, physical activity, smoking, body mass and lipid levels. In the cross-sectional study, all three blood pressure measures showed inverse relationships with prevalence of low back pain in both sexes. In the prospective study of disease-free women, baseline pulse pressure and systolic pressure were inversely associated with risk of low back pain [odds ratio (OR) 0.93 per 10 mm Hg increase in pulse pressure, 95% confidence interval (CI) 0.89-0.98, p = 0.007; OR 0.95 per 10 mm Hg increase in systolic pressure, 95% CI 0.92-0.99, p = 0.005]. Results among men were equivocal. No associations were indicated with the occurrence of pain in individuals with low back pain at baseline.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the observed inverse relationship between blood pressure and low back pain risk in women is mediated by the effects of blood pressure on the microcirculation and inflammatory processes within the intervertebral discs, which can be tested through a longitudinal study examining the relationship between blood pressure, disc health (assessed via MRI), and low back pain incidence in a cohort of initially asymptomatic women.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study include the observation of inverse relationships between blood pressure measures (systolic, diastolic, and pulse pressure) and the prevalence of low back pain in a cross-sectional study of 39,872 individuals. Additionally, in a prospective study of 17,209 initially back pain-free individuals and 5,740 individuals with low back pain, baseline pulse pressure and systolic pressure were found to be inversely associated with the risk of developing low back pain in women, but results were equivocal in men.

GAP: A significant knowledge gap left unanswered by this work is the underlying physiological mechanism by which higher blood pressure appears to be protective against the development of low back pain, particularly in women. Additionally, the study's findings do not explain why the associations observed were not consistent across genders.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the observed inverse relationship between blood pressure and low back pain risk in women is mediated by the effects of blood pressure on the microcirculation and inflammatory processes within the intervertebral discs, which can be tested through a longitudinal study examining the relationship between blood pressure, disc health (assessed via MRI), and low back pain incidence in a cohort of initially asymptomatic women.

abstract: Health services often spend more on safety interventions than seems cost-effective. This study investigates whether the public value safety-related health care improvements more highly than the same improvements in contexts where the health care system is not responsible. An online survey was conducted to elicit the relative importance placed on preventing harms caused by 1) health care (hospital-acquired infections, drug administration errors, injuries to health care staff), 2) individuals (personal lifestyle choices, sports-related injuries), and 3) nature (genetic disorders). Direct valuations were obtained from members of the public by using a person trade-off or "matching" method. Participants were asked to choose between two preventative interventions of equal cost and equal health benefit per person for the same number of people, but differing in causation. If participants indicated a preference, their strength of preference was measured by using person trade-off. Responses were obtained from 1030 people, reflecting the sociodemographic mix of the UK population. Participants valued interventions preventing hospital-acquired infections (1.31) more highly than genetic disorders (1.0), although drug errors were valued similarly to genetic disorders (1.07), and interventions to prevent injury to health care staff were given less weight than genetic disorders (0.71). Less weight was also given to interventions related to lifestyle (0.65) and sports injuries (0.41).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that providing participants with detailed information about the cost-effectiveness and preventability of each type of intervention will reduce the disparity in valuations between hospital-acquired infections and other types of interventions, resulting in a significant decrease in the valuation ratio of hospital-acquired infections to genetic disorders.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the public values safety-related health care improvements differently depending on the context, with interventions preventing hospital-acquired infections being valued more highly than those preventing genetic disorders, drug errors, and injuries to healthcare staff, as well as lifestyle and sports injuries. Specifically, the valuations were: hospital-acquired infections (1.31), genetic disorders (1.0), drug errors (1.07), injuries to healthcare staff (0.71), lifestyle-related injuries (0.65), and sports injuries (0.41).

GAP: The study does not investigate how these valuations might change when the public is provided with additional information about the preventability, severity, or cost-effectiveness of the interventions, leaving open the question of whether the valuations are based on an intrinsic preference for certain types of interventions or a lack of understanding of the underlying factors.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that providing participants with detailed information about the cost-effectiveness and preventability of each type of intervention will reduce the disparity in valuations between hospital-acquired infections and other types of interventions, resulting in a significant decrease in the valuation ratio of hospital-acquired infections to genetic disorders.

abstract: This study aims to evaluate the efficacy of closed reduction and the effects of timing and fracture types on patient satisfaction. Only patients with isolated nasal fractures were included in the study. Patients with additional maxillofacial fractures and patients whose application time to our clinic was more than 10 days after the trauma were excluded. Patients were classified into 5 types according to their fracture. All patients underwent closed reduction and external fixation under local anesthesia. Patients were asked about their satisfaction in a survey at 28th day and sixth month after the surgery. Patients were divided into groups according to fracture type and intervention time, and the results of the survey were evaluated. Of the 43 patients included in the study, 38 were male, 5 were female, and the average age was 24.9. The average intervention time of the patients was 5.44 days. Twenty-eight (65%) of 43 patients were satisfied with the result, whereas 15 (35%) patients were not happy with their operation. In a comparison of patient satisfaction rates according to fracture type, the mild fracture group had a higher satisfaction rate compared to the severe fracture group.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients who undergo closed reduction and external fixation for nasal fractures within 3 days of trauma will have significantly higher satisfaction rates at 6 months post-surgery compared to those who undergo the procedure between 4-10 days after trauma.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients with mild nasal fractures had a higher satisfaction rate after closed reduction and external fixation under local anesthesia compared to those with severe fractures. The overall patient satisfaction rate was 65%, with 35% of patients being dissatisfied with their operation. The average intervention time was 5.44 days, and the majority of patients (38 out of 43) were male with an average age of 24.9.

GAP: The study does not investigate the relationship between the timing of intervention and patient satisfaction in a more nuanced manner, specifically whether there is an optimal time window for intervention that maximizes patient satisfaction. The current study only excludes patients whose application time to the clinic was more than 10 days after the trauma, but it does not explore the effects of earlier versus delayed intervention within the 10-day window.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients who undergo closed reduction and external fixation for nasal fractures within 3 days of trauma will have significantly higher satisfaction rates at 6 months post-surgery compared to those who undergo the procedure between 4-10 days after trauma.

abstract: There are three main service delivery channels: clinical services, outreach, and family and community. To determine which delivery channels are associated with the greatest reductions in under-5 mortality rates (U5MR), we used data from sequential population-based surveys to examine the correlation between changes in coverage of clinical, outreach, and family and community services and in U5MR for 27 high-burden countries. Household survey data were abstracted from serial surveys in 27 countries. Average annual changes (AAC) between the most recent and penultimate survey were calculated for under-five mortality rates and for 22 variables in the domains of clinical, outreach, and family- and community-based services. For all 27 countries and a subset of 19 African countries, we conducted principal component analysis to reduce the variables into a few components in each domain and applied linear regression to assess the correlation between changes in the principal components and changes in under-five mortality rates after controlling for multiple potential confounding factors. AAC in under 5-mortality varied from 6.6% in Nepal to -0.9% in Kenya, with six of the 19 African countries all experiencing less than a 1% decline in mortality. The strongest correlation with reductions in U5MR was observed for access to clinical services (all countries: p = 0.02, r² = 0.58; 19 African countries p<0.001, r² = 0.67). For outreach activities, AAC U5MR was significantly correlated with antenatal care and family planning services, while AAC in immunization services showed no association. In the family- and community services domain, improvements in breastfeeding were associated with significant changes in mortality in the 30 countries but not in the African subset; while in the African countries, nutritional status improvements were associated with a significant decline in mortality.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the quality of clinical services, as measured by the presence of skilled birth attendants and adherence to standardized treatment protocols, will moderate the relationship between access to clinical services and reductions in under-5 mortality rates, such that countries with higher quality clinical services will experience greater declines in U5MR compared to those with lower quality services.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The research found that access to clinical services has the strongest correlation with reductions in under-5 mortality rates (U5MR) across 27 high-burden countries, with a significant correlation also observed for antenatal care, family planning services, and improvements in breastfeeding and nutritional status in specific contexts. The study used principal component analysis and linear regression to assess the correlation between changes in service delivery channels and U5MR, controlling for potential confounding factors.

GAP: While the study identified significant correlations between certain service delivery channels and reductions in U5MR, it does not examine the specific mechanisms by which these channels influence mortality rates, particularly in terms of how the quality of clinical services affects health outcomes. The study also does not investigate how different combinations of service delivery channels might interact to impact U5MR.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the quality of clinical services, as measured by the presence of skilled birth attendants and adherence to standardized treatment protocols, will moderate the relationship between access to clinical services and reductions in under-5 mortality rates, such that countries with higher quality clinical services will experience greater declines in U5MR compared to those with lower quality services.

abstract: Polyarteritis nodosa (PAN) is a systemic vasculitis involving mainly medium-sized arteries and, rarely, small-sized arteries. The diagnosis is principally based on clinical exams, biopsy of an affected organ, and/or arteriography of renal or mesenteric arteries. Once diagnosed, immunosuppressive agents, such as glucocorticoids and cyclophosphamide, are generally introduced as soon as possible. Whether spontaneous remission of PAN occurs is therefore largely unknown. We describe the case of a 51-year-old woman who presented with a 4-day-history of intense pain in her left flank, hypertension, fever, microscopic hematuria, and acute renal failure. Contrast-enhanced renal ultrasound strongly suggested bilateral renal infarction. Medical history and an extensive workup allowed to exclude systemic embolism, recreational drug abuse, cardiac arrhythmias, and thrombophilia. A possible diagnosis of PAN was considered; however, within 2 weeks of admission, spontaneous remission of her clinical and biological symptoms occurred without the use of any immunosuppressive treatment. Finally, 3 months later, renal arteriography confirmed the diagnosis of PAN. The patient remains free of symptoms 1 year after initial presentation.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with Polyarteritis nodosa who undergo spontaneous remission will exhibit a unique immunological profile characterized by elevated levels of anti-inflammatory cytokines and reduced levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines compared to those who do not experience spontaneous remission, and that this profile can be used as a predictive biomarker for spontaneous remission in PAN.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract include the case of a 51-year-old woman with a possible diagnosis of Polyarteritis nodosa (PAN) who experienced spontaneous remission of her clinical and biological symptoms within 2 weeks without immunosuppressive treatment. The diagnosis of PAN was later confirmed by renal arteriography 3 months after initial presentation. The patient remained symptom-free 1 year after the initial presentation.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of what triggers spontaneous remission in PAN and whether there are specific factors or mechanisms that contribute to this phenomenon. The abstract highlights the rarity of observing spontaneous remission in PAN due to the common practice of immediately introducing immunosuppressive agents upon diagnosis, suggesting a need to understand the natural history of the disease and factors influencing its course.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with Polyarteritis nodosa who undergo spontaneous remission will exhibit a unique immunological profile characterized by elevated levels of anti-inflammatory cytokines and reduced levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines compared to those who do not experience spontaneous remission, and that this profile can be used as a predictive biomarker for spontaneous remission in PAN.

abstract: Celiac disease (CD) is believed to be a permanent intolerance to gluten. A number of patients, however, discontinue the gluten-free diet (GFD) without developing symptoms or signs. The aim of our study was to investigate whether CD patients are capable of developing tolerance to gluten. All 77 adult patients from our hospital known to have biopsy-proven CD for more than 10 years were invited to participate. We investigated symptoms, gluten consumption, antibodies for CD and other autoimmunity, human leukocyte antigen (HLA)-typing, bone mineral density, and performed small bowel biopsies. Tolerance was defined as no immunological or histological signs of CD while consuming gluten. Sixty-six patients accepted participation, but after review of the diagnostic biopsies 53 were found to have true CD. Twenty-three percent of patients had a gluten-containing diet, 15% admitted gluten transgression and 62% followed the GFD. Patients on a GFD had significantly more osteoporosis. Normal small bowel mucosa was found in four of eight on gluten-containing diet and in four of four with gluten transgression. Two patients were considered to have developed tolerance to gluten. One of them was HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative CD patients who develop tolerance to gluten will exhibit a unique immunological profile characterized by elevated levels of regulatory T cells and decreased levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines in response to gluten exposure, compared to HLA-DQ2/DQ8 positive CD patients and HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative CD patients who do not develop tolerance.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that a subset of patients with celiac disease (CD) may develop tolerance to gluten, as evidenced by the absence of immunological or histological signs of CD while consuming gluten. Specifically, 2 out of 53 patients with true CD were considered to have developed tolerance, and one of them was HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative. Additionally, patients on a gluten-free diet (GFD) had a higher incidence of osteoporosis, and some patients with gluten exposure showed normal small bowel mucosa.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific factors contribute to the development of tolerance to gluten in some CD patients, particularly in those who are HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative. The mechanisms underlying this tolerance and the potential for predicting which patients may develop tolerance are not explored.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative CD patients who develop tolerance to gluten will exhibit a unique immunological profile characterized by elevated levels of regulatory T cells and decreased levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines in response to gluten exposure, compared to HLA-DQ2/DQ8 positive CD patients and HLA-DQ2/DQ8 negative CD patients who do not develop tolerance.

abstract: The US Preventive Services Task Force recommends against spirometry in the absence of symptoms. However, as much as 50% of COPD cases in the United States remain undiagnosed. Report of symptoms, smoking history, and spirometric data were collected from subjects screened for a work-related medical evaluation (N = 3,955). Prevalence of airflow obstruction and respiratory symptoms were assessed. Sensitivity, specificity, positive and negative predictive values, and relative risks of predicting symptoms and smoking history for COPD were calculated. Forty-four percent of smokers in our sample had airways obstruction (AO). Of these, 36% reported a diagnosis of or treatment for COPD. Odds ratio (95% CI) for AO with smoking (>or = 20 pack-years) was 3.73 (3.12- 4.45), 1.98 (1.73-2.27) for cough, 1.79 (1.55-2.08) for dyspnea, 1.95 (1.70-2.34) for sputum, and 2.59 (2.26-2.97) for wheeze. Respiratory symptoms were reported by 92% of smokers with AO, 86% smokers with restriction, 76% smokers with normal spirometry, and 73% of nonsmokers. Sensitivity (92% vs 90%), specificity (19% vs 22%), positive (47% vs 40%) and negative (75% vs 80%) predictive values for the presence of one or more symptoms were similar between smokers and all subjects.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that among asymptomatic smokers with normal spirometry, a cumulative smoking exposure of 10-19 pack-years will significantly increase the risk of developing airflow obstruction within a 5-year follow-up period compared to those with less than 10 pack-years of smoking exposure.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that 44% of smokers in the sample had airways obstruction (AO), with a significant association between smoking history and AO, as well as between AO and respiratory symptoms such as cough, dyspnea, sputum, and wheeze. The presence of one or more symptoms showed high sensitivity but low specificity for predicting AO in both smokers and the overall population. Additionally, a substantial proportion of smokers with AO did not report a diagnosis or treatment for COPD, indicating potential underdiagnosis.

GAP: The study establishes associations between smoking, respiratory symptoms, and airways obstruction but does not explore the longitudinal progression of airflow obstruction in asymptomatic smokers or the impact of early intervention on the progression of COPD in this subgroup. It also does not investigate the specific thresholds of smoking exposure that correlate with the onset of symptoms or the development of AO.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that among asymptomatic smokers with normal spirometry, a cumulative smoking exposure of 10-19 pack-years will significantly increase the risk of developing airflow obstruction within a 5-year follow-up period compared to those with less than 10 pack-years of smoking exposure.

abstract: Web search engines are an important tool in communication and diffusion of knowledge. Among these, Google appears to be the most popular one: in August 2008, it accounted for 87% of all web searches in the UK, compared with Yahoo's 3.3%. Google's value as a diagnostic guide in general medicine was recently reported. The aim of this comparative cross-sectional study was to evaluate whether searching Google with disease-related terms was effective in the identification and diagnosis of complex immunological and allergic cases. Forty-five case reports were randomly selected by an independent observer from peer-reviewed medical journals. Clinical data were presented separately to three investigators, blinded to the final diagnoses. Investigator A was a Consultant with an expert knowledge in Internal Medicine and Allergy (IM&A) and basic computing skills. Investigator B was a Registrar in IM&A. Investigator C was a Research Nurse. Both Investigators B and C were familiar with computers and search engines. For every clinical case presented, each investigator independently carried out an Internet search using Google to provide a final diagnosis. Their results were then compared with the published diagnoses. Correct diagnoses were provided in 30/45 (66%) cases, 39/45 (86%) cases, and in 29/45 (64%) cases by investigator A, B, and C, respectively. All of the three investigators achieved the correct diagnosis in 19 cases (42%), and all of them failed in two cases.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the use of Google's advanced search features, such as exact phrase matching and site-specific searching, will significantly increase the accuracy of diagnoses made by non-expert healthcare professionals, such as Research Nurses, to a level comparable to that of medical experts, when searching for complex immunological and allergic cases.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that using Google to search for disease-related terms can be effective in identifying and diagnosing complex immunological and allergic cases, with correct diagnoses provided in 66%, 86%, and 64% of cases by a Consultant, a Registrar, and a Research Nurse, respectively. The study also showed that the level of medical expertise and computing skills can influence the accuracy of diagnoses made using Google searches.

GAP: The study does not investigate how the effectiveness of Google as a diagnostic tool changes when the search terms are modified or when additional features such as Google's advanced search options are used. It also does not explore whether the accuracy of diagnoses can be improved by using a combination of search engines or by incorporating artificial intelligence-based search algorithms.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the use of Google's advanced search features, such as exact phrase matching and site-specific searching, will significantly increase the accuracy of diagnoses made by non-expert healthcare professionals, such as Research Nurses, to a level comparable to that of medical experts, when searching for complex immunological and allergic cases.

abstract: Various factors contribute to the effective implementation of evidence-based treatments (EBTs). In this study, cognitive processing therapy (CPT) was administered in a Veterans Affairs (VA) posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) specialty clinic in which training and supervision were provided following VA implementation guidelines. The aim was to (a) estimate the proportion of variability in outcome attributable to therapists and (b) identify characteristics of those therapists who produced better outcomes. We used an archival database of veterans (n = 192) who completed 12 sessions of CPT by therapists (n = 25) who were trained by 2 nationally recognized trainers, 1 of whom also provided weekly group supervision. Multilevel modeling was used to estimate therapist effects, with therapists treated as a random factor. The supervisor was asked to retrospectively rate each therapist in terms of perceived effectiveness based on supervision interactions. Using single case study design, the supervisor was interviewed to determine what criteria she used to rate the therapists and emerging themes were coded. When initial level of severity on the PTSD Checklist (PCL; McDonald&Calhoun, 2010; Weathers, Litz, Herman, Huska,&Keane, 1993) was taken into account, approximately 12% of the variability in the PCL at the end of treatment was due to therapists. The trainer, blind to the results, identified the following characteristics and actions of effective therapists: effectively addressing patient avoidance, language used in supervision, flexible interpersonal style, and ability to develop a strong therapeutic alliance.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that therapists who receive targeted training to enhance their ability to address patient avoidance, use effective language in supervision, develop a flexible interpersonal style, and foster a strong therapeutic alliance will demonstrate significantly improved treatment outcomes, as measured by reduction in PTSD symptoms, compared to therapists who receive standard training.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study are that approximately 12% of the variability in PTSD treatment outcomes using cognitive processing therapy (CPT) can be attributed to the therapists, and that effective therapists are characterized by their ability to address patient avoidance, use of language in supervision, flexible interpersonal style, and ability to develop a strong therapeutic alliance.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of whether these characteristics of effective therapists can be taught or improved through additional training or supervision, and whether such improvements would lead to better treatment outcomes for patients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that therapists who receive targeted training to enhance their ability to address patient avoidance, use effective language in supervision, develop a flexible interpersonal style, and foster a strong therapeutic alliance will demonstrate significantly improved treatment outcomes, as measured by reduction in PTSD symptoms, compared to therapists who receive standard training.

abstract: The seafaring industry remains a hazardous occupation that requires sophisticated systems of risk and fitness assessment. This study aims to investigate the extent of agreement between Approved Doctors (ADs) and Medical Referees (MRs) when they assess a seafarer's fitness. Between 2003 and 2009 a total of 232,878 seafarer medical examinations were carried out by ADs, of which 465 were considered by the MRs because the seafarer appealed against the AD's decision. The extent of agreement between ADs and MRs was studied. Two hundred and sixty-eight (58%) cases seen by the ADs were classed as category 4 "permanently unfit"; the referees only placed 85 (18%) of them in this category. On the other hand, 252 (54%) cases seen by the MRs were classed as category 2 "fit with restrictions", while the ADs had only placed 111 (24%) in this category. The overall agreement between the assessors (AD vs. MR) was poor (Kappa K = 0.18). For cardiovascular diseases and for mental ill-health, access to additional information by the MR was the commonest reason for changing the fitness category, but for all other conditions factors such as the experience and knowledge of the MRs or their different interpretation of the standards were the most frequent reasons for a change to fitness category or to restrictions.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that providing Approved Doctors with standardized guidelines and additional training on the interpretation of medical standards for seafarer fitness evaluations will significantly increase the agreement between Approved Doctors and Medical Referees, as measured by an increase in Kappa coefficient to above 0.6, within a 12-month period following the intervention.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found a poor agreement (Kappa K = 0.18) between Approved Doctors (ADs) and Medical Referees (MRs) in assessing seafarers' fitness, with significant discrepancies in the classification of seafarers as "permanently unfit" or "fit with restrictions". The main reasons for these discrepancies included access to additional information, differences in experience and knowledge, and varying interpretations of standards, particularly for cardiovascular diseases, mental ill-health, and other conditions.

GAP: The study identifies the reasons for disagreement between ADs and MRs but does not explore how standardizing the assessment process or providing additional training to ADs on the interpretation of medical standards could improve the consistency of fitness evaluations. It also leaves unanswered whether such interventions could reduce the discrepancy in classification rates between ADs and MRs.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that providing Approved Doctors with standardized guidelines and additional training on the interpretation of medical standards for seafarer fitness evaluations will significantly increase the agreement between Approved Doctors and Medical Referees, as measured by an increase in Kappa coefficient to above 0.6, within a 12-month period following the intervention.

abstract: Hepatorenal syndrome (HRS) is the functional renal failure associated with advanced cirrhosis and has also been described in fulminant hepatic failure. Without liver transplantation its prognosis is dismal. Our study included patients with type 1 HRS associated with cirrhosis, who were not liver transplant candidates.AIM: To identify variables associated with improved survival. Sixty-eight patients fulfilled the revised Ascites Club Criteria for type 1 HRS. None of them was suitable for liver transplantation. All the patients were treated with combinations of: albumin, midodrine and octreotide, pressors, and hemodialysis. Median survival was 13 days for the whole group. Survival varied with the end-stage liver disease (ESLD) etiology: autoimmune, 49 days, cardiac cirrhosis, 22 days, idiopathic, 15.5 days, viral, 15 days, hepatitis C and alcohol, 14.5 days, alcohol 8 days, and neoplasia 4 days (p = 0.048). Survival of HRS associated with alcoholic liver disease versus other etiologies was not statistically significant (p = 0.1). Increased serum creatinine (p = 0.02) and urinary sodium 6-10 mEq/l (p = 0.027) at the initiation of therapy were prognostic factors for mortality. HRS treatment modalities (p = 0.73), use of dialysis (p = 0.56), dialysis modality (p = 0.35), use of vasopressors (p = 0.26), pre-existing renal disease (p = 0.49), gender (p = 0.90), and age (p = 0.57) were not associated with survival.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the differential expression of inflammatory cytokines and fibrogenic factors in liver tissue, correlated with the specific etiology of end-stage liver disease, will predict survival outcomes in patients with type 1 hepatorenal syndrome, independent of traditional prognostic factors such as serum creatinine and urinary sodium levels.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that in patients with type 1 hepatorenal syndrome (HRS) associated with cirrhosis, median survival was 13 days, and survival varied significantly with the etiology of end-stage liver disease (ESLD). Increased serum creatinine and urinary sodium levels at the initiation of therapy were identified as prognostic factors for mortality. Additionally, the study found that certain factors such as HRS treatment modalities, use of dialysis, dialysis modality, use of vasopressors, pre-existing renal disease, gender, and age were not associated with survival.

GAP: The study provides insight into prognostic factors and survival rates based on ESLD etiology but does not explore the underlying mechanisms by which different etiologies of liver disease influence survival outcomes in HRS patients. It also does not investigate potential therapeutic strategies that could specifically target these mechanisms to improve survival.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the differential expression of inflammatory cytokines and fibrogenic factors in liver tissue, correlated with the specific etiology of end-stage liver disease, will predict survival outcomes in patients with type 1 hepatorenal syndrome, independent of traditional prognostic factors such as serum creatinine and urinary sodium levels.

abstract: Although dose-volume parameters in image-guided brachytherapy have become a standard, the use of posterior-inferior border of the pubic symphysis (PIBS) points has been recently proposed in the reporting of vaginal doses. The aim was to evaluate their pertinence. Nineteen patients who received image-guided brachytherapy after concurrent radiochemotherapy were included. Per treatment, CT scans were performed at Days 2 and 3, with reporting of the initial dwell positions and times. Doses delivered to the PIBS points were evaluated on each plan, considering that they were representative of one-third of the treatment. The movements of the applicator according to the PIBS point were analysed. Mean prescribed doses at PIBS -2, PIBS, PIBS +2 were, respectively, 2.23 ± 1.4, 6.39 ± 6.6, and 31.85 ± 36.06 Gy. Significant differences were observed between the 5 patients with vaginal involvement and the remaining 14 at the level of PIBS +2 and PIBS: +47.60 Gy and +7.46 Gy, respectively (p = 0.023 and 0.03). The variations between delivered and prescribed doses at PIBS points were not significant. However, at International commission on radiation units and measurements rectovaginal point, the delivered dose was decreased by 1.43 ± 2.49 Gy from the planned dose (p = 0.019). The delivered doses at the four points were strongly correlated with the prescribed doses with R(2) ranging from 0.93 to 0.95. The movements of the applicator in regard of the PIBS point assessed with the Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine coordinates were insignificant.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the dose delivered to the PIBS +2 point will be a significant predictor of local recurrence in patients undergoing image-guided brachytherapy for vaginal cancer, with higher doses corresponding to improved local control rates.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that the use of posterior-inferior border of the pubic symphysis (PIBS) points in image-guided brachytherapy allows for accurate dosimetry, with significant differences in doses delivered to PIBS points between patients with and without vaginal involvement. The delivered doses at PIBS points were strongly correlated with prescribed doses, and the movements of the applicator in relation to the PIBS point were insignificant. Additionally, the study observed a decrease in delivered dose at the International Commission on Radiation Units and Measurements rectovaginal point compared to the planned dose.

GAP: The study does not investigate the long-term clinical outcomes of using PIBS points in image-guided brachytherapy, such as local control rates, toxicity, and survival rates, and how these outcomes correlate with the doses delivered to PIBS points.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the dose delivered to the PIBS +2 point will be a significant predictor of local recurrence in patients undergoing image-guided brachytherapy for vaginal cancer, with higher doses corresponding to improved local control rates.

abstract: Stock et al. (Eur Respir J 25:47-53, 2005) recently estimated asthma prevalence in Germany using claims data on prescriptions and hospital diagnoses and found high prevalence peaks in infants. Our objective was to critically assess and discuss various aspects of identifying children with asthma using prescription data. We replicated the selection procedure of Stock et al. using data on 290,919 children aged 0-17 years insured in the Gmünder ErsatzKasse (GEK) in 2005. Asthma prevalence was also estimated in a sample of 17,641 children aged 0-17 years participating in the German Health Interview and Examination Survey for Children and Adolescents (KiGGS) from 2003 to 2006. In children aged 0-4 years insured in the GEK, prevalences were found to range from 11.7 to 17.7% for boys and from 7.2 to 11.1% for girls when the criteria of Stock et al. were applied. A steady decline in prevalences was observed in older age groups. Asthma prevalence estimated in the KiGGS data showed a quite different distribution. In the age group 0-4 years, prevalences were found to range from 0 to 2.6% in boys and from 0 to 1.0% in girls; in children>4 years, prevalences were found to increase with increasing age.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the discrepancies in asthma prevalence estimates between claims data and health survey data in young children (0-4 years) can be attributed to a higher rate of transient wheezing episodes being misclassified as asthma in
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include the estimation of asthma prevalence in children aged 0-17 years using claims data and a health survey, with significant discrepancies observed between the two methods, particularly in young children (0-4 years) where claims data showed high prevalence rates (11.7-17.7% for boys, 7.2-11.1% for girls) whereas the health survey (KiGGS) showed much lower rates (0-2.6% for boys, 0-1.0% for girls), and an increase in prevalence with age in the KiGGS data for children over 4 years.

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what factors contribute to the significant disparity in asthma prevalence estimates between claims data and health survey data, especially in young children, and whether these differences are due to over-diagnosis or misclassification in claims data, under-reporting in health surveys, or actual differences in asthma manifestation and diagnosis across different age groups.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the discrepancies in asthma prevalence estimates between claims data and health survey data in young children (0-4 years) can be attributed to a higher rate of transient wheezing episodes being misclassified as asthma in

abstract: Parental drinking has been shown to be associated with offspring drinking. However, the relationship appears to be more complex than often assumed and few studies have tracked it over longer time periods. To explore the long-term (10-year) transmission of familial drinking during adolescence to offspring drinking patterns in young adulthood. Swedish longitudinal study, assessing the relationship between familial drinking in 2000 and offspring drinking in 2010 using simultaneous quantile regression analysis (n=744).DATA: Data on familial drinking was gathered from the Swedish level-of-living surveys (LNU) and from partner LNU in 2000 while data on offspring drinking in young adulthood was gathered from LNU 2010. Drinking among offspring, parents and potential stepparents was measured through identical quantity-frequency indices referring to the past 12 months in 2010 and 2000 respectively. Young adults whose families were abstainers in 2000 drank substantially less across quintiles in 2010 than offspring of non-abstaining families. The difference, however, was not statistically significant between quintiles of the conditional distribution. Actual drinking levels in drinking families were not at all or weakly associated with drinking in offspring. Supplementary analyses confirmed these patterns.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that offspring of parents who engage in moderate drinking will exhibit increased sensitivity to social norms around alcohol consumption in young adulthood, compared to offspring of abstaining or heavy-drinking parents, as measured by self-reported adherence to social drinking norms in a survey of young adults.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract are that offspring from families who were abstainers in 2000 drank substantially less in 2010 compared to offspring of non-abstaining families, and that actual drinking levels in drinking families were not strongly associated with drinking in offspring.

GAP: This work leaves unanswered the question of what specific factors or mechanisms contribute to the transmission of drinking behaviors from parents to offspring, particularly in cases where parental drinking is present. The study found an association between familial abstention and reduced offspring drinking, but did not explore the underlying reasons for this relationship or how parental drinking behaviors are communicated to offspring.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that offspring of parents who engage in moderate drinking will exhibit increased sensitivity to social norms around alcohol consumption in young adulthood, compared to offspring of abstaining or heavy-drinking parents, as measured by self-reported adherence to social drinking norms in a survey of young adults.

abstract: To investigate the effect of fenofibrate on sleep apnoea indices. Proof-of-concept study comprising a placebo run-in period (1 week, 5 weeks if fibrate washout was required) and a 4-week randomized, double-blind treatment period. Thirty-four subjects (mean age 55 years, body mass index 34 kg/m 2 , fasting triglycerides 3.5 mmol/L) with diagnosed sleep apnoea syndrome not treated with continuous positive airways pressure were enrolled and randomized to once daily treatment with fenofibrate (145 mg NanoCrystal(R) tablet) or placebo. Overnight polysomnography, computerized attention/vigilance tests and blood sampling for measurement of lipids, insulin, fasting plasma glucose and fibrinogen were performed at the end of each study period. NCT00816829. As this was an exploratory study, a range of sleep variables were evaluated. The apnoea/hypopnoea index (AHI) and percentage of time spent with arterial oxygen saturation (SpO(2))<90% were relevant as they have been evaluated in other clinical trials. Other variables included total apnoeas, hypopnoeas and oxygen desaturations, and non-cortical micro-awakenings related to respiratory events per hour. Fenofibrate treatment significantly reduced the percentage of time with SpO(2)<90% (from 9.0% to 3.5% vs. 10.0% to 11.5% with placebo, p = 0.007), although there was no significant change in the AHI (reduction vs. control 14% (95%CI -47 to 40%, p = 0.533). Treatment reduced obstructive apnoeas (by 44%, from 18.5 at baseline to 15.0 at end of treatment vs. 29.0 to 30.5 on placebo, p = 0.048), and non-cortical micro-awakenings per hour (from 23.5 to 18.0 vs. 24.0 to 25.0 with placebo, p = 0.004). Other sleep variables were not significantly influenced by fenofibrate. Exploratory study in patients with mild to moderate sleep apnoea, limited treatment duration; concomitant hypnotic treatment (35%); lack of correction for multiplicity of testing.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that prolonged fenofibrate treatment (beyond 4 weeks) will lead to a significant improvement in cognitive function, as measured by standardized attention and vigilance tests, in patients with sleep apnoea syndrome, mediated by its effects
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings from the abstract are that fenofibrate treatment significantly reduces the percentage of time with arterial oxygen saturation (SpO2) < 90%, decreases obstructive apnoeas by 44%, and lowers non-cortical micro-awakenings per hour in patients with sleep apnoea syndrome. However, it does not significantly change the apnoea/hypopnoea index (AHI). These effects are observed over a 4-week treatment period in a group of 34 subjects with mild to moderate sleep apnoea.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of whether the beneficial effects of fenofibrate on sleep apnoea indices are sustained over longer treatment periods and whether these effects translate into improved cognitive function and quality of life for patients. Additionally, the mechanisms by which fenofibrate exerts its effects on sleep apnoea, particularly its impact on obstructive apnoeas and oxygen desaturations, are not fully understood.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that prolonged fenofibrate treatment (beyond 4 weeks) will lead to a significant improvement in cognitive function, as measured by standardized attention and vigilance tests, in patients with sleep apnoea syndrome, mediated by its effects

abstract: To determine the potential prognostic value of using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) to identify patients with disorders of consciousness, who show potential for recovery. Observational study. Unit for acute rehabilitation care. Patients (N=22) in a vegetative state (VS; n=10) and minimally conscious state (MCS; n=12) during the first 200 days after the initial incident. Not applicable. Further course on the Coma Recovery Scale-Revised. Participants performed a mental imagery fMRI paradigm. They were asked to alternately imagine playing tennis and navigating through their home. In 14 of the 22 examined patients (VS, n=5; MCS, n=9), a significant activation of the regions of interest (ROIs) of the mental imagery paradigm could be found. All 5 patients with activation of a significant blood oxygen level dependent signal, who were in a VS at the time of the fMRI examination, reached at least an MCS at the end of the observation period. In contrast, 5 participants in a VS who failed to show activation in ROIs, did not (sensitivity 100%, specificity 100%). Six of 9 patients in an MCS with activation in ROIs emerged from an MCS. Of 3 patients in an MCS who did not show activation, 2 patients stayed in an MCS and 1 patient emerged from the MCS (sensitivity 85%, specificity 40%).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that the degree of functional connectivity between the default mode network and the frontoparietal network during the mental imagery fMRI paradigm will predict the likelihood and speed of recovery from a vegetative state to a minimally conscious state, with higher connectivity being associated
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study found that patients in a vegetative state (VS) and minimally conscious state (MCS) who showed significant activation of regions of interest (ROIs) during a mental imagery fMRI paradigm had a higher likelihood of recovering to at least a minimally conscious state (all 5 VS patients with activation recovered) or emerging from an MCS (6 out of 9 MCS patients with activation). The study reported high sensitivity and specificity for predicting recovery in VS patients (100% sensitivity and specificity) but lower specificity in MCS patients (40%).

GAP: The study leaves unanswered the question of what specific neural mechanisms or patterns of brain activity are associated with the transition from a vegetative state to a minimally conscious state, and how these might be modulated or enhanced to improve recovery outcomes. Additionally, the relatively small sample size and the variability in outcomes among MCS patients with activation suggest a need for further investigation into the predictive value of fMRI in this population.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that the degree of functional connectivity between the default mode network and the frontoparietal network during the mental imagery fMRI paradigm will predict the likelihood and speed of recovery from a vegetative state to a minimally conscious state, with higher connectivity being associated

abstract: To compare the accuracy achieved by a trained urology nurse practitioner (UNP) and consultant urologist in detecting bladder tumours during flexible cystoscopy. Eighty-three patients underwent flexible cystoscopy by both the UNP and consultant urologist, each unaware of the other's findings. Before comparing the findings, each declared whether there was tumour or any suspicious lesion requiring biopsy. Of 83 patients examined by flexible cystoscopy, 26 were found to have a tumour or a suspicious lesion. One tumour was missed by the UNP and one by the urologist; each tumour was minute. Analysis using the chance-corrected proportional agreement (Kappa) was 0.94, indicating very close agreement.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that in a large, multicenter study of patients undergoing flexible cystoscopy, the diagnostic accuracy of trained urology nurse practitioners will be non-inferior to that of consultant urologists for detecting bladder tumors larger than 5mm in diameter, but may be inferior for detecting smaller tumors.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The key findings of the abstract are that a trained urology nurse practitioner (UNP) and a consultant urologist have very close agreement (Kappa = 0.94) in detecting bladder tumors during flexible cystoscopy, with each missing only one minute tumor out of 26 patients found to have a tumor or suspicious lesion.

GAP: This study leaves unanswered the question of whether the high level of agreement between UNPs and consultant urologists in detecting bladder tumors during flexible cystoscopy can be replicated in a larger, more diverse patient population, and whether the accuracy of UNPs and urologists varies depending on specific tumor characteristics, such as size, location, or histological type.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that in a large, multicenter study of patients undergoing flexible cystoscopy, the diagnostic accuracy of trained urology nurse practitioners will be non-inferior to that of consultant urologists for detecting bladder tumors larger than 5mm in diameter, but may be inferior for detecting smaller tumors.

abstract: Although consensus guidelines for pretreatment evaluation and monitoring of propranolol therapy in patients with infantile hemangiomas (IH) have been formulated, little is known about the cardiovascular side effects. We sought to analyze cardiovascular evaluations in patients with IH at baseline and during treatment with an oral beta-blocker. Data from 109 patients with IH were retrospectively analyzed. Patient and family history, pretreatment electrocardiogram (ECG), heart rate, and blood pressure were evaluated before initiation of beta-blocker therapy. Blood pressure and standardized questionnaires addressing side effects were evaluated during treatment. Questionnaire analyses (n = 83) identified 3 cases with a family history of cardiovascular disease in first-degree relatives. ECG findings were normal in each case and no serious complication of therapy occurred. ECG abnormalities were found in 6.5% of patients but there were no contraindications to beta-blocker therapy and no major complications. Hypotension in 9 patients did not require therapy adjustment. In all, 88 parents (81%) reported side effects during beta-blocker treatment. The relatively small patient cohort is a limitation.
hypothesis: We hypothesize that prolonged use of propranolol in patients with infantile hemangiomas will not significantly increase the risk of developing cardiovascular disease in these patients over a 5-year follow-up period, compared to a matched cohort not treated with propranolol.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings from the abstract include that in a retrospective analysis of 109 patients with infantile hemangiomas (IH) treated with oral beta-blocker therapy, specifically propranolol, there were minimal cardiovascular side effects. Key observations include: 6.5% of patients had ECG abnormalities but no contraindications to beta-blocker therapy, 9 patients experienced hypotension that did not require therapy adjustment, and 81% of parents reported side effects during treatment, with 3 cases having a family history of cardiovascular disease in first-degree relatives.

GAP: Despite the analysis of cardiovascular evaluations and side effects in patients with IH during beta-blocker therapy, the study leaves unanswered the long-term effects of propranolol treatment on the cardiovascular health of these patients, particularly in relation to the development of future cardiovascular diseases or the potential impact on cardiac function over an extended period.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that prolonged use of propranolol in patients with infantile hemangiomas will not significantly increase the risk of developing cardiovascular disease in these patients over a 5-year follow-up period, compared to a matched cohort not treated with propranolol.

abstract: Implementation of the complex treatment strategies that have been shown to improve survival of patients with congestive heart failure (CHF) may require certain expertise. We wanted to examine the association between pattern of outpatient care and survival of patients with CHF. In a retrospective cohort study conducted with national Veterans Health Administration (VHA) databases, we examined the association between the pattern of outpatient care and survival in 11,661 patients discharged from VA hospitals between October 1, 1991, and September 30, 1992, with the primary diagnosis of CHF (cohort 1). Patients were divided into 4 groups, on the basis of their pattern of outpatient care over a 12-month period after discharge: 1) general medicine clinic visits only (GM-only); 2) cardiology clinic visits only (CARD-only); 3) general medicine and cardiology (MIXED) clinic visits; and 4) neither general medicine nor cardiology clinic visits (no-GM/CARD). We used the Cox proportional hazards model to evaluate 1-year survival, controlling for clinical and demographic factors. Consistency of our results was examined by performing identical analysis on a cohort of patients discharged from VHA hospitals between October 1, 1994, and September 30, 1995 (cohort 2, n = 10,141). The overall 1-year mortality rate was 23% in the primary cohort. The unadjusted mortality rate was highest for patients in the no-GM/CARD follow up (29%) and lowest for patients in the MIXED group (19%). By use of the MIXED group as reference and adjusting for important clinical and demographic factors, the risk of death (risk ratio [95% CI]) was 1.12 (0.94-1.34) in the CARD-only group, 1.26 (1.15-1.38) in the GM-only group, and 1.48 (1.28-1.72) in the no-GM/CARD group. Cohort-2 results were consistent with cohort 1 for most covariates, and significant survival differences were again found between GM-only and the MIXED group (1.25 [1.14-1.37]).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that patients with congestive heart failure who receive a mixed model of care with at least bimonthly visits to both general medicine and cardiology clinics over a 6-month period will have a significantly lower risk of 1-year mortality compared to those receiving less frequent or specialized
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The main findings of the study include the association between patterns of outpatient care and survival in patients with congestive heart failure (CHF), with the mixed model of care (general medicine and cardiology clinic visits) showing the lowest 1-year mortality rate (19%) compared to other models, including general medicine only, cardiology only, and no visits. The study used a retrospective cohort analysis of over 11,000 patients and found significant survival differences between these groups, even after adjusting for clinical and demographic factors. The results were consistent across two cohorts.

GAP: Despite demonstrating the benefits of a mixed model of care for patients with CHF, the study leaves unanswered the question of what specific aspects of the mixed care model contribute to improved survival rates. It does not investigate how the frequency, timing, or content of visits in the mixed model influence patient outcomes, nor does it explore potential mechanisms by which integrated care might lead to better survival.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that patients with congestive heart failure who receive a mixed model of care with at least bimonthly visits to both general medicine and cardiology clinics over a 6-month period will have a significantly lower risk of 1-year mortality compared to those receiving less frequent or specialized

abstract: To identify the features of PMR that may predict the duration of steroid therapy, the occurrence of relapses and the late development of GCA. Prospective cohort study of 176 patients with PMR, followed up for 5 years. Baseline factors associated with the duration of steroids therapy were identified using Cox regression. Predictors of relapse and the late development of GCA were identified using binary logistic regression. A total of 176 patients with PMR were included, of whom 124 stopped steroids within 5 years. The probability of stopping steroids within 5 years was independently reduced by an elevated plasma viscosity (PV) [hazard ratio (HR) = 0.49; 95% CI 0.29, 0.82 for a PV>or = 2.00 mPa s compared with a PV<or = 1.80 mPa s; overall P = 0.024] and by starting treatment at>15 mg prednisolone (HR = 0.63; 95% CI 0.41, 0.97; P = 0.036). Either of these independently reduced the chances of stopping steroids within a given time interval between 27 and 51%. No significant predictors of relapse were identified. Predictors of late GCA on univariable analysis were female sex [odds ratio (OR) = 8.16; 95% CI 1.06, 63.13; P = 0.044], HLA-DRB1*0101 or -*0401 alleles (OR = 4.95; 95% CI 1.05, 23.34; P = 0.043), PV>or = 2.00 mPa s compared with PV<or = 1.80 mPa s (OR = 10.64; 95% CI 1.28, 88.38; P = 0.029) and initial prednisolone dose>15 mg (OR = 4.53; 95% CI 1.61, 12.79; P = 0.004).
hypothesis: We hypothesize that elevated plasma viscosity in PMR patients will be associated with increased levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as interleukin-6 (IL-6) and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α), which in turn will contribute to a longer duration of steroid therapy and a higher risk of developing GCA.
raw_output:
FINDINGS: The study identified several factors associated with the duration of steroid therapy and the late development of Giant Cell Arteritis (GCA) in patients with Polymyalgia Rheumatica (PMR). Specifically, elevated plasma viscosity (PV) and higher initial prednisolone doses were associated with reduced probability of stopping steroids within 5 years. Additionally, female sex, certain HLA-DRB1 alleles, elevated PV, and higher initial prednisolone doses were identified as predictors of late GCA.

GAP: The study did not investigate the potential mechanisms by which elevated plasma viscosity and higher initial prednisolone doses influence the duration of steroid therapy and the development of GCA. Furthermore, the study did not examine the impact of these factors on the underlying inflammatory processes in PMR patients.

HYPOTHESIS: We hypothesize that elevated plasma viscosity in PMR patients will be associated with increased levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as interleukin-6 (IL-6) and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α), which in turn will contribute to a longer duration of steroid therapy and a higher risk of developing GCA.

abstract: The evolution of Earth-Moon system is described by the dark matter field fluid model proposed in the Meeting of Division of Particle and Field 2004, American Physical Society. The current behavior of the Earth-Moon system agrees with this model very well and the general pattern of the evolution of the Moon-Earth system described by this model agrees with geological and fossil evidence. The closest distance of the Moon to Earth was about 259000 km at 4.5 billion years ago, which is far beyond the Roche's limit. The result suggests that the tidal friction may not be the primary cause for the evolution of the Earth-Moon system. The average dark matter field fluid constant derived from Earth-Moon system data is 4.39 x 10^(-22) s^(-1)m^(-1). This model predicts that the Mars's rotation is also slowing with the angular acceleration rate about -4.38 x 10^(-22) rad s^(-2).
